[80202-5] Clotting time of depleted plasma with 1:1 phospholipid by Coagulation assay --after addition of factor X activated -- **Component:** Clotting time **Property:** Time **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** PPP **Scale:** Qn **Method:** Coag.depleted plasma with 1:1 phospholipid.after addition of factor X activated  **Short name:** Clot tm PPP Cg dpl pls 1:1php pst fct X  **Definition:** The test measures clotting time of a patient's plasma mixed with phospholipid (PL)-depleted plasma after the addition of activated Factor X. [80203-3] HIV 1 and 2 Ab [Identifier] in Serum, Plasma or Blood by Rapid immunoassay -- **Component:** HIV 1 & 2 Ab **Property:** Prid **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Ser/Plas/Bld **Scale:** Nom **Method:** IA.rapid  **Short name:** HIV 1 & 2 Ab SerPlBld IA.rapid  **Definition:** This assay detects and differentiates between six distinct HIV antibodies: HIV-1 gp160, gp41, p31, and p24 and HIV-2 gp140 and gp36. It is intended for use as an additional, more specific test to confirm the presence of HIV-1 and HIV-2 antibodies found by screening procedures.  It may also be used to confirm the presence of antibodies to HIV-1 and HIV-2 in pediatric subjects (children as young as 2 years of age). Because this assay examines antibodies to 6 distinct proteins, it yields a higher degree of specificity for HIV-1 and -2 compared to other methods (such as Multispot). This term was created for, but not limited in use to, the submitter's Bio-Rad Geenius HIV 1/2 Supplemental test kit.

[80207-4] Leishmania infantum+donovani IgM Ab [Titer] in Serum or Plasma by Immunofluorescence -- **Component:** Leishmania infantum+donovani Ab.IgM **Property:** Titr **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Ser/Plas **Scale:** SemiQn **Method:** IF  **Short name:** L infantum+donovani IgM Titr SerPl IF  **Definition:** This assay detects antibodies against Leishmania donovani IgM for L. infantum screening based on the known cross reactivity between L. donovani and L. infantum. If the Leishmania IIFT is positive, a monospecific test (example Westernblot) can be performed to differentiate the pathogenic agents and confirm the diagnosis. Immunoglobulin specific slides distinguish between IgG, IgM and IgA. No reaction at a 1:100 titer indicates no IgM class antibodies against Leishmania donovani are detected, but infection cannot be excluded. A reaction of a titer at 1:100 is positive indicating a former or acute infection. This term was created for, but not limited in use to, the submitter's Euroimmun Anti-Leishmania donovani indirect immunofluorescence test kit.

[80356-9] Cause of death entity axis code [Automated] -- **Component:** Cause of death entity axis code **Property:** Find **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** Nom **Method:** Automated  **Definition:** A cause of death ICD code determined by computer and called an "entity axis code". The result value (an ICD code) is derived by natural language processing from the cause of death textual information entered by the provider on the death certificate. A separate cause of death code is assigned to each individual text entry entered on the death certificate. The reporting structure also includes the part/line number and sequence within the line for each distinct text block processed and successfully encoded. This term was created for the CDC HL7 Version 2.5 Implementation Guide: Reporting Death Information from the EHR to Vital Records, R1.2.
[80357-7] Cause of death record axis code [Automated] -- **Component:** Cause of death record axis code **Property:** Find **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** Nom **Method:** Automated  **Definition:** A computer generated set of ICD codes that best describe the person's cause of death (called "record axis codes") that are generated from the system-assigned entity axis codes by: a) eliminating duplicate codes, and b) combining codes into single more comprehensive codes where warranted.  This term was created for the CDC HL7 Version 2.5 Implementation Guide: Reporting Death Information from the EHR to Vital Records, R1.2.

[80384-1] Choriogonadotropin [Presence] in Urine by Rapid immunoassay -- **Component:** Choriogonadotropin **Property:** PrThr **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Urine **Scale:** Ord **Method:** IA.rapid  **Short name:** HCG Ur Ql IA.rapid  **Definition:** This term was created for, but is not limited in use to, the bioMerieux bioNexia hCG Urine kit, which is intended for the qualitative detection of human chorionic gonadotropin (hCG) in urine to aid in the early detection of pregnancy. The bioNexia hCG assay has a sensitivity of 25 mIU/mL, at which level there is no cross-reactivity interference from the structurally related glycoprotein hormones FSH, LH and TSH, and which allows detection of pregnancy as early as one day after the first missed menses. The bioNexia hCG Urine assay results should be read 3 minutes after the sample is applied to the sample well.  This lab test is used as pregnancy test (for early pregnancy detection)
[80385-8] Choriogonadotropin [Presence] in Serum by Rapid immunoassay -- **Component:** Choriogonadotropin **Property:** PrThr **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Ser **Scale:** Ord **Method:** IA.rapid  **Short name:** HCG Ser Ql IA.rapid  **Definition:** This term was created for, but is not limited in use to, the bioMerieux bioNexia hCG Combo kit, which is intended for the qualitative detection of human chorionic gonadotropin (hCG) in urine [LOINC: 80384-1] or serum to aid in the early detection of pregnancy. The bioNexia hCG Combo assay has a sensitivity of 25 mIU/mL, at which level there is no cross-reactivity interference from the structurally related glycoprotein hormones FSH, LH and TSH, and which allows detection of pregnancy as early as one day after the first missed menses. The assay result should be read 5 minutes after the serum sample is applied to the sample well.    This lab test is used as pregnancy test (for early pregnancy detection)

[80408-8] Meets CDC field triage criteria -- **Component:** Meets CDC field triage criteria **Property:** Find **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** Ord  **Short name:** Meets CDC field triage criteria  **Definition:** Indicates whether the patient meets the CDC field triage criteria pertaining to the decision by pre-hospital personnel to transport trauma victims to a specialized acute care facility (trauma center) versus an undesignated, non-specialized acute care facility. The goal of the decision scheme is to match the clinical needs of an injured patient to the resources and expertise of a given facility to care for them. This term was created for, but not limited in use to, the acsCDCFieldTraumaCriteria variable included in the OASIS Emergency Date Exchange Language - Tracking of Emergency Patients (EDXL-TEP) and the HL7 v2.7.1 standard ADT and RAS messages.
[80409-6] Bed type -- **Component:** Bed type **Property:** Type **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** Nom  **Short name:** Bed type  **Definition:** The type of bed needed to support that patient. This term was created for, but not limited in use to, the contingencyMedicalSpecialtyCode variable used in the OASIS Emergency Date Exchange Language - Tracking of Emergency Patients (EDXL-TEP) specification and the HL7 v2.7.1 standard ADT and RAS messages. It is used to inform the facility where a patient is being transported about the bed type needed and to support mass casualty patient movement scenarios.
[80410-4] Date and time of transfer departure -- **Component:** Date and time of transfer departure **Property:** TmStp **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** Qn  **Short name:** Date+time trans departure  **Definition:** The date and time the patient departed via emergency transportation services from the encounter location. This concept does not accommodate the use case of self-transportation or patient discharge.

[80515-0] Kappa light chains.free [Mass/volume] in Serum by Nephelometry -- **Component:** Immunoglobulin light chains.kappa.free **Property:** MCnc **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Ser **Scale:** Qn **Method:** Nephelometry  **Short name:** Kappa LC Free Ser Neph-mCnc  **Definition:** This nephelometric assay provides a sensitive test for monitoring patients with monoclonal light chain diseases but no M-spike on protein electrophoresis, and may be more sensitive than immunofixation for detection of monoclonal free light chains. In some patients with non-secretory myeloma, systemic amyloidosis, or light chain deposition disease, the nephelometric free light chain assay provides a positive identification of a monoclonal serum light chain when the serum immunofixation is negative.

[80517-6] Kappa light chains.free/Lambda light chains.free [Mass Ratio] in Serum by Nephelometry -- **Component:** Immunoglobulin light chains.kappa.free/Immunoglobulin light chains.lambda.free **Property:** MRto **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Ser **Scale:** Qn **Method:** Nephelometry  **Short name:** Kappa LC Free/Lambda Free Ser Neph  **Definition:** An elevated kappa/lambda ratio supports the diagnosis of a monoclonal gammopathy and requires further investigation. Borderline elevated kappa/lambda ratios occur with renal impairment, and may require appropriate renal function tests. Elevated levels of both kappa and lambda with normal kappa/lambda ratio - may be due to (1) renal impairment (common); (2) Over-production of polyclonal FLC from inflammatory conditions (common); (3) Bi-clonal gammopathies of different free light chain types (rare).

[80541-6] Nicotine [Mass/mass] in Hair -- **Component:** Nicotine **Property:** MCnt **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Hair **Scale:** Qn  **Short name:** Nicotine Hair-mCnt  **Definition:** Hair analysis for nicotine is a bio-marker for smoker status and maybe a valuable bio-marker of long term exposure that can be readily applied in epidemiological studies of exposure to tobacco smoke, and more specifically environmental tobacco smoke (ETS). The long term (up to several months) exposure assessment is the major advantage. Uncertainties over the mechanism by which drugs are incorporated into hair lead to caution in the use of hair in forensic toxicology and medicine, but this may not be a serious issue for ETS studies. Issues do remain with this testing however, such as how hair color, chemical treatments of hair, or type of hair texture can affect results. [PMID: 12198265]

DBS specimens are also useful for outpatient TDM in adults who have been trained to collect their own DBS because most analytes are relatively stable in DBS and the DBS card does not require specialized transportation. [PMID: 25204403]

Collecting hair specimens also is less invasive than urine or blood sampling. Drawbacks for hair analysis include expense, and environmental contamination of the specimen.

[80617-4] Cefuroxime free [Mass/volume] in Serum or Plasma -- **Component:** Cefuroxime.free **Property:** MCnc **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Ser/Plas **Scale:** Qn  **Short name:** Cefuroxime.free SerPl-mCnc
[80618-2] Zika virus IgM Ab [Units/volume] in Cerebral spinal fluid by Immunoassay -- **Component:** Zika virus Ab.IgM **Property:** ACnc **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** CSF **Scale:** Qn **Method:** IA  **Short name:** ZIKV IgM CSF IA-aCnc  **Definition:** Quantitative analysis of Zika virus IgM antibodies in cerebral spinal fluid (CSF) specimens by immunoassay-based methods. Similar to most EIA tests, results are reported as a ratio of optical densities.   Zika virus IgM antibodies become detectable 4-8 days after the onset of symptoms and persist for months. Since IgM does not normally cross the blood-brain barrier, detection of Zika virus IgM in CSF implies CNS infection. Serological cross-reactivity is strong between Zika and other flaviviruses, especially Dengue virus. Therefore, positive results for this test are presumptive and should be confirmed by neutralizing antibody testing [LOINC: 80621-6]. Negative results for this test on a sample taken during the first week of illness does not rule out infection and the test should be repeated on a convalescent sample.
[80619-0] Zika virus IgM Ab [Units/volume] in Serum or Plasma by Immunoassay -- **Component:** Zika virus Ab.IgM **Property:** ACnc **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Ser/Plas **Scale:** Qn **Method:** IA  **Short name:** ZIKV IgM SerPl IA-aCnc  **Definition:** Quantitative analysis of Zika virus IgM antibodies in serum or plasma specimens by immunoassay-based methods. Similar to most EIA tests, results are reported as a ratio of optical densities.   Zika virus IgM antibodies become detectable 4-8 days after the onset of symptoms and persist for months. Serological cross-reactivity is strong between Zika and other flaviviruses, especially Dengue virus. Therefore, positive results for this test are presumptive and should be confirmed by neutralizing antibody testing [LOINC: 80620-8]. Negative results for this test on a sample taken during the first week of illness does not rule out infection and the test should be repeated on a convalescent sample.
[80620-8] Zika virus neutralizing antibody [Titer] in Serum by Neutralization test -- **Component:** Zika virus Ab.Neut **Property:** Titr **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Ser **Scale:** SemiQn **Method:** Neut  **Short name:** ZIKV NAb Titr Ser Nt  **Definition:** Quantitative result for Zika virus neutralizing antibodies in serum. Serological cross-reactivity is strong between Zika and other flaviviruses, especially Dengue virus. Therefore, positive EIA or IF assays for Zika virus IgM antibodies are presumptive only and should be confirmed by a neutralization assay, such as the plaque-reduction neutralization test (PRNT), which can discriminate between Zika virus and cross-reacting antibodies in primary flavivirus infections. Recent Zika virus infection is confirmed by stable high neutralizing antibody titers, seroconversion, or a 4x or greater increases in neutralizing antibody titer in a patient's appropriately timed acute and convalescent phase sera.

[80642-2] Floxacillin Free [Mass/volume] in Serum or Plasma -- **Component:** Floxacillin.free **Property:** MCnc **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Ser/Plas **Scale:** Qn  **Short name:** Floxacillin free SerPl-mCnc
[80643-0] Gadolinium [Mass/mass] in Tissue -- **Component:** Gadolinium **Property:** MCnt **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Tiss **Scale:** Qn  **Short name:** Gadolinium Tiss-mCnt  **Definition:** Due to its paramagnetic properties, chelated gadolinium is commonly employed as contrast media for magnetic resonance imaging (MRI) and computer tomography (CT) scanning. Gadolinium is eliminated primarily by the renal filtration. In healthy subjects with normal renal function, the plasma half-life of gadolinium is approximately 90 minutes. Patients with reduced renal function exhibit increased gadolinium excretion half-life and may be affected by nephrogenic systemic fibrosis (NSF). In this syndrome, prolonged retention of gadolinium is thought to allow the gadolinium cation to dissociate from its synthetic organic chelator and deposit predominantly in the skin, although other organs may be affected as well. These patients are often severely debilitated by progressive skin thickening and tightening. Fibrosis of skeletal muscle, lungs, liver, testes, and myocardium have also been observed, often with fatal results. Three hemodialysis treatments are required to substantially remove gadolinium from patients with impaired renal function; peritoneal dialysis is not effective.

[80658-8] Activated clotting time (ACT) of Blood induced by Diatomaceous earth -- **Component:** Activated clotting time **Property:** Time **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Bld **Scale:** Qn **Method:** Coag.diatomaceous earth induced  **Short name:** ACT Bld Diatomaceous earth ind  **Definition:** This is a point-of-care electrochemical assay that measures venous or arterial whole blood activated clotting time (ACT) by using a Celite clot activator, which stimulates the intrinsic coagulation pathway. Celite is the commonly-used brand name of the diatomaceous earth (a naturally occurring, soft, siliceous sedimentary rock) used in ACT assays. Different activators result in different clotting times. For example, the ACT reference ranges for glass, kaolin and Celite are 110-190 sec, 90-150 sec and 100-170 sec, respectively. [http://www.practical-haemostasis.com/Miscellaneous/Miscellaneous%20Tests/act.html]  In addition, aprotinin may affect the ACT result when Celite is used, therefore, the test is not recommended for use with patients receiving aprotinin.  The ACT is primarily used to monitor a patient's state of anticoagulation due to heparin that is administered during a medical or surgical procedure. It is commonly employed in cardiac catheterization, percutaneous transluminal coronary angioplasty (PTCA), renal dialysis, hemodialysis, and extra-corporeal circulation during bypass. This term was created for, but not limited in use to, the submitter's Abbott Celite activated clotting time test kit.
[80659-6] Activated clotting time (ACT) of Blood induced by Kaolin -- **Component:** Activated clotting time **Property:** Time **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Bld **Scale:** Qn **Method:** Coag.kaolin induced  **Short name:** ACT Bld KCT  **Definition:** The activated clotting time (ACT) of blood is a measure of the amount of time for a fibrin clot to form in the presence of a known amount of kaolin activator.

[80737-0] Calculated panel reactive antibody - Serum -- **Component:** Calculated panel reactive antibody **Property:** RelRto **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Ser **Scale:** Qn **Method:** Calculated  **Short name:** cPRA Ser Calc  **Definition:** A patient's "calculated panel reactive antibody" (cPRA) determines the likelihood that a transplant recipient and donor would be incompatible. The cPRA is calculated based on how many and which HLA antibodies the patient has compared to known information about the HLA antigen frequencies from ~1200 donors from 2003-2005.[PMID: 19958328] The more antibodies the patient has and the more common the HLA antigens are in the donor pool, the higher the cPRA. Calculated cPRA is not the same as a patient's percent PRA, which is simply the percentage of a pool of donors (~100) to which a patient had reactive antibodies (see the HLA Ab panel [LOINC: 46993-2]). Note that "panel" is part of the antibody name and a single result is expected. This is not panel term.

[80821-2] Zika virus neutralizing antibody [Presence] in Cerebral spinal fluid by Neutralization test -- **Component:** Zika virus Ab.Neut **Property:** PrThr **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** CSF **Scale:** Ord **Method:** Neut  **Short name:** ZIKV NAb CSF Ql Nt  **Definition:** Qualitative result for Zika virus neutralizing antibodies in CSF. Serological cross-reactivity is strong between Zika and other flaviviruses, especially Dengue virus. Therefore, positive EIA or IF assays for Zika virus IgM antibodies are presumptive only and should be confirmed by a neutralization assay, such as the plaque-reduction neutralization test (PRNT), which can discriminate between Zika virus and cross-reacting antibodies in primary flavivirus infections. Recent Zika virus infection is confirmed by stable high neutralizing antibody titers, seroconversion, or a 4x or greater increases in neutralizing antibody titer in a patient's appropriately timed acute and convalescent phase specimens.
[80822-0] Zika virus neutralizing antibody [Presence] in Serum by Neutralization test -- **Component:** Zika virus Ab.Neut **Property:** PrThr **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Ser **Scale:** Ord **Method:** Neut  **Short name:** ZIKV NAb Ser Ql Nt  **Definition:** Qualitative result for Zika virus neutralizing antibodies in serum. Serological cross-reactivity is strong between Zika and other flaviviruses, especially Dengue virus. Therefore, positive EIA or IF assays for Zika virus IgM antibodies are presumptive only and should be confirmed by a neutralization assay, such as the plaque-reduction neutralization test (PRNT), which can discriminate between Zika virus and cross-reacting antibodies in primary flavivirus infections. Recent Zika virus infection is confirmed by stable high neutralizing antibody titers, seroconversion, or a 4x or greater increases in neutralizing antibody titer in a patient's appropriately timed acute and convalescent phase sera.

[80828-7] Pepsin A+Pepsinogen A [Mass/volume] in Lower respiratory specimen by Immunoassay -- **Component:** Pepsin A+Pepsinogen A **Property:** MCnc **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Respiratory system specimen.lower **Scale:** Qn **Method:** IA  **Short name:** PepsinA+PepsinogenA Lower resp IA-mCnc  **Definition:** The presence of gastric pepsin A in a patient's airway is a marker for aspiration and assists in the diagnosis of gastroesophageal reflux. Chronic aspiration of gastric contents may worsen lung disease in premature infants, or other mechanically ventilated patients. [PMID: 16954956] This assay detects both pepsin A and pepsinogen A and does not differentiate between the two. A level of less than 3 is considered in range. (One unit of pepsin A=0.1ng/ml pepsin A is a measure of concentration (ng/ml) expressed in an arbitrarily defined unit.)

[80891-5] Symptom score [AUASI] -- **Component:** Symptom score **Property:** Score **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** Qn **Method:** AUASI  **Definition:** The American Urology Association Symptom Index (AUA-SI) score is the cumulative score of answers to 7 questions. The answer to each question gets a score of 0 to 5, so the possible range of total symptom scores is 0-35. The International Prostate Symptoms Score (IPSS) uses the same symptom scoring as the AUASI; the score for the additional quality of life question in the IPSS survey is not part of the total symptom score calculation but the two can be used in conjunction to monitor treatment efficacy.
[80892-3] Heparin unfractionated [Units/volume] in Blood by Heparin protamine titration -- **Component:** Heparin.unfractionated **Property:** ACnc **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Bld **Scale:** Qn **Method:** Heparin protamine titration  **Short name:** UFH Bld HPT-aCnc  **Definition:** The heparin assay measures the patient's actual circulating concentration of unfractionated heparin in a sample of fresh whole blood by using titration with known quantities of protamine in individual assay channels. The channel whose quantity of protamine neutralizes the heparin in the test sample will have the shortest clotting time, and the concentration of heparin is calculated based on the known amount of protamine in that channel. The heparin concentration is used along with the activated clotting time in the cardiac OR to give a more complete picture of the patient's heparin anti-coagulation status. Using this test, the clinician/perfusionist knows how much heparin is present, and how much additional heparin is needed during the procedure, and the amount of protamine sulfate that is required to reverse the remaining heparin in the patient at the end of the procedure. This term was created for, but not limited in use to, the submitter's Medtronic HMS Heparin Assay test kit.
[80893-1] Projected heparin concentration to reach target ACT [Units/volume] in Blood by calculation -- **Component:** Projected heparin concentration to reach target ACT **Property:** ACnc **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Bld **Scale:** Qn **Method:** Calculated  **Short name:** Proj heparin conc Bld Calc-aCnc  **Definition:** The projected heparin concentration is the amount of heparin required to increase the patient's ACT to the clinician specified target value and is based the target ACT and the slope of the patient's response to known amounts of heparin. This term was created for, but is not limited in use to, the submitter's Medtronic Heparin Dose Response test kit.

[80906-1] Bridged race [CDC] -- **Component:** Bridged race **Property:** Type **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** Nom **Method:** CDC  **Definition:** Used by the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC), National Center for Health Statistics (NCHS) to report race data that has been adjusted in such a way that an individual is assigned a single race code. The adjustment process is used for persons for whom more than one race is reported. In such cases, a single race is assigned by using an algorithm defined by NCHS. The goal is to provide race statistics that are comparable with those used historically in order to facilitate time series analysis.

[80912-9] Gadolinium [Mass/volume] in Serum or Plasma -- **Component:** Gadolinium **Property:** MCnc **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Ser/Plas **Scale:** Qn  **Short name:** Gadolinium SerPl-mCnc  **Definition:** Due to its paramagnetic properties, chelated gadolinium is commonly employed as contrast media for magnetic resonance imaging (MRI) and computer tomography (CT) scanning. Gadolinium is eliminated primarily by the renal filtration. In healthy subjects with normal renal function, the plasma half-life of gadolinium is approximately 90 minutes. Patients with reduced renal function exhibit increased gadolinium excretion half-life and may be affected by nephrogenic systemic fibrosis (NSF). In this syndrome, prolonged retention of gadolinium is thought to allow the gadolinium cation to dissociate from its synthetic organic chelator and deposit predominantly in the skin, although other organs may be affected as well. These patients are often severely debilitated by progressive skin thickening and tightening. Fibrosis of skeletal muscle, lungs, liver, testes, and myocardium have also been observed, often with fatal results. Three hemodialysis treatments are required to substantially remove gadolinium from patients with impaired renal function; peritoneal dialysis is not effective.

This term was created for, but not limited in use to, the HL7 Version 2.6 Implementation Guide: Vital Records Death Reporting, Release 1

[81092-9] Tricuspid valve Orifice area by US.doppler -- **Component:** Orifice area **Property:** Area **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Tricuspid valve **Scale:** Qn **Method:** US.doppler  **Short name:** TV Orifice area DOP
[81093-7] Tricuspid valve Effective regurgitant orifice area by US.doppler+Calculated by PISA method -- **Component:** Effective regurgitant orifice area **Property:** Area **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Tricuspid valve **Scale:** Qn **Method:** US.doppler+Calculated by PISA method  **Short name:** TV EROA PISA  **Definition:** The effective regurgitant orifice area (EROA) is the size of the defect where the valve leaflets are not aligning normally (coaptation defect). The severity of regurgitation is related to the EROA. EROA can be calculated by the Volumetric method as regurgitant volume divided by the velocity time integral of the regurgitant jet velocity (VTIRegJet), which is recorded by continuous wave Doppler. [PMID: 18276823]
[81094-5] Tricuspid valve Effective regurgitant orifice area by US.doppler+Calculated by volumetric method -- **Component:** Effective regurgitant orifice area **Property:** Area **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Tricuspid valve **Scale:** Qn **Method:** US.doppler+Calculated by volumetric method  **Short name:** TV EROA DOP+Calc by vol  **Definition:** The effective regurgitant orifice area (EROA) is the size of the defect where the valve leaflets are not aligning normally (coaptation defect). The severity of regurgitation is related to the EROA. EROA can be calculated by the Volumetric method as regurgitant volume divided by the velocity time integral of the regurgitant jet velocity (VTIRegJet), which is recorded by continuous wave Doppler. [PMID: 18276823]

[81154-7] Dengue and Chikungunya and Zika virus panel by NAA with probe detection -- **Component:** Dengue & Chikungunya & Zika virus panel **Property:** - **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** - **Method:** Probe.amp.tar  **Short name:** DENV + CHIKV + ZIKV Pnl NAA+pr  **Definition:** This LOINC panel is used to report the qualitative detection and differentiation of Dengue, Chikungunya, and Zika viruses in human serum or CSF specimens, as well as for detecting Zika virus in human urine or amniotic fluid specimens. The panel is based on, but not limited in use to, the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention's Trioplex real time reverse transcriptase PCR assay, which is intended for use during the acute phase of infection when viral RNA is detectable for a limited period of time following onset of symptoms (approximately five days for Dengue, eight days for Chikungunya and seven days for Zika). Negative results do not rule out infection with Dengue, Chikungunya and/or Zika virus.

[81174-5] Tumor type in Stomach cancer by Macroscopy -- **Component:** Tumor type **Property:** Type **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Stomach cancer **Scale:** Ord **Method:** Macroscopy  **Short name:** Tumor Type Stomach cancer Ql Macro  **Definition:** The macroscopic growth pattern is used to classify the tumor type. Early gastric cancer growth patterns are classified using criteria similar to those used in endoscopy, including protruded (Type 0-I), elevated (Type 0-IIa), flat (Type 0-IIb), depressed (Type 0-IIc) or excavated (Type 0-III). Depressed type has a higher risk for increased stage and lymphatic invasion.
[81175-2] Distance of tumor from closest margin [Length] in Specimen by Macroscopy -- **Component:** Distance of tumor from closest margin **Property:** Len **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Specimen **Scale:** Qn **Method:** Macroscopy  **Short name:** Tumor from Closest Margin Spec Macro  **Definition:** In a specimen, the distance from the tumor to the nearest proximal or distal margin (cut end) is recorded. The tumor distance to margins is based on macroscopic assessment and confirmed or amended on microscopy ([LOINC: 81182-8] or [LOINC: 81183-6]), especially when the macroscopic size is difficult to assess (i.e. in early gastric carcinoma and diffuse carcinoma).
[81176-0] Distance of tumor from circumferential resection margin [Length] in Specimen by Macroscopy -- **Component:** Distance of tumor from circumferential resection margin **Property:** Len **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Specimen **Scale:** Qn **Method:** Macroscopy  **Short name:** Distance Tumor from CRM Spec Macro  **Definition:** The distance between the tumor and circumferential resection margin (CRM) is recorded for tumors of the gastric cardia. This measurement is facilitated by horizontal sectioning through the tumor. To increase accuracy, the distance between the tumor and the CRM is subsequently measured microscopically [LOINC: 81184-4]. Involvement of the circumferential resection margin is an indicator of poor prognosis.
[81177-8] Serosal classification [Appearance] in Stomach cancer -- **Component:** Serosal classification **Property:** Aper **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Stomach cancer **Scale:** Nom  **Short name:** Serosal classification Stomach cancer  **Definition:** The appearance of the serosa is described, including any abnormalities, nodules or plaques that may represent serosal involvement by carcinoma. Serosal involvement upstages a gastric carcinoma from T3 to T4.

[81252-9] Discrete genetic variant -- **Component:** Simple variant **Property:** ID **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** Nom  **Short name:** Simple var ID  **Definition:** This term is used to report the unique identifier of the simple variant found in this study. The identifier may come from various sources, including NCBI's ClinVar and Ensembl. For example, the variant NM_014049.4(ACAD9):c.1249C>T (p.Arg417Cys) has the ClinVar ID 30880 and would be reported in OBX-5 as 30880^NM_014049.4(ACAD9):c.1249C>T (p.Arg417Cys)^ClinVar. [http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/clinvar/variation/30880/]
[81253-7] Simple variant name Nominal -- **Component:** Simple variant name **Property:** Find **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** Nom  **Short name:** Simple var name  **Definition:** This term is used to report the formal name of a simple variant, including the reference sequence and nucleotide change. For example, NM_014049.4(ACAD9):c.1249C>T (p.Arg417Cys) represents an ACAD9 gene variant with nucleotide change c.1249C>T and protein change p.Arg417Cys that has the NCBI reference sequence NM_014049.4. [http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/clinvar/variation/30880/]
[81254-5] Genomic allele start-end -- **Component:** Variant exact start-end **Property:** ID **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** Nom  **Short name:** Gen allele loc ID  **Definition:** The variant start-end location is the first genomic position in the reference allele that contains a change from the reference allele. For example, for the simple variant NM_014049.4(ACAD9):c.1249C>T (p.Arg417Cys), the variant exact start-end location is Chr3: 128906220 on Assembly GRCh38. [http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/clinvar/variation/30880/]
[81255-2] dbSNP [ID] -- **Component:** dbSNP **Property:** ID **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** Nom  **Short name:** dbSNP ID  **Definition:** The unique identifier for the variant represented as a small nucleotide polymorphism (SNP). The dbSNP ID is used routinely as the base identifier in pharmacogenomics as well as arrCGH studies. For example, for the simple variant NM_014049.4(ACAD9):c.1249C>T (p.Arg417Cys), the dbSNP ID is 368949613. [http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/clinvar/variation/30880/]
[81256-0] COSMIC [ID] -- **Component:** COSMIC simple variant **Property:** ID **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** Nom  **Short name:** COSMIC simple var ID  **Definition:** The COSMIC ID is used to report the variant ID for the COSMIC database, which collects information about somatic cancer-related, non-structural mutations.  Information in the COSMIC database includes an ID for  the tissue source of the submission,  a gene name, a reference sequence from Ensembl, pathogenicity, and HGVS names. For example, the COSMIC Mutation ID COSM34090 is for a missense mutation in the IDH2 gene with HGVS nomenclature c.516G>T and p.R172S. [http://cancer.sanger.ac.uk/cosmic/mutation/overview?id=34090]
[8125-7] CD34 cells/cells in Blood -- **Component:** Cells.CD34/Cells **Property:** NFr **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Bld **Scale:** Qn  **Short name:** CD34 Cells NFr Bld
[81257-8] CIGAR [ID] -- **Component:** CIGAR **Property:** ID **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** Nom  **Short name:** CIGAR var ID  **Definition:** This term is used to report the unique ID from CIGAR, a syntax for describing variation that is use most frequently during alignment in sequencing studies.
[81258-6] Sample variant allelic frequency [NFr] -- **Component:** Sample variant allelic frequency **Property:** NFr **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** Qn  **Short name:** Sample VAF  **Definition:** The fraction of all reads in a study sample at a given genomic locus that identify the allele (variant) in question.  For homozygotes it will be close to 1.0; for heterozygotes it will be close to 0.5. It can be less than 0.5 in the case of mosaics or multiple chromosome, or mixtures of tumor cells and normal cells. This measure is an attribute of the variant and applies when the method is a Next Generation Sequencing (NGS) or similar. Such methods provide many reads from the sample for each locus. To report population allelic frequency, see [LOINC: 92821-8].  Allelic frequency is usually reported as a decimal fraction for both Sample Variant Allelic Frequency and Population Allelic Frequency, although it is occasionally reported as a percent. Special care/caution should be taken when reporting and converting to a decimal fraction.
[81259-4] Associated phenotype -- **Component:** Associated phenotype **Property:** Find **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** Nom  **Short name:** Prob assoc phenotype  **Definition:** The possible phenotype associated with the genetic variant found in this study.
[81260-2] Complex genetic variant [ID] -- **Component:** Complex variant **Property:** ID **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** Nom  **Short name:** Comp var ID  **Definition:** This term is used to report the unique identifier of the complex variant found in this study. The identifier may come from various sources, including NCBI's ClinVar and Ensembl. For example, the variant NM_000106.5(CYP2D6):c.[886C>T;457G>C] - Haplotype has the ClinVar ID 16895. [http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/clinvar/variation/16895/]
[81261-0] Complex variant name Nominal -- **Component:** Complex variant name **Property:** Find **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** Nom  **Short name:** Comp var name  **Definition:** This term is used to report the formal name of a complex variant, including the reference sequence and details about the variation. For example, NM_000106.5(CYP2D6):c.[886C>T;457G>C] - Haplotype represents a CYP2D6 gene variant with haplotype nucleotide changes c.886C>T and c.457G>C and the NCBI reference sequence NM_000106.5. [http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/clinvar/variation/16895/]
[81262-8] Complex variant HGVS name -- **Component:** Complex variant HGVS name **Property:** Find **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** Nom  **Short name:** Comp var HGVS name  **Definition:** This term is used to report the name of the complex variant found in this study in HGVS format. For example, c.[886C>T;457G>C], which represents two separate base substitutions in one gene on one chromosome, or c.[886C>T];[457G>C], which represents two separate base substitutions in one gene on two different chromosomes.

[81273-5] fentaNYL and Norfentanyl panel - Specimen -- **Component:** Fentanyl & Norfentanyl panel **Property:** - **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** XXX **Scale:** -  **Short name:** fentaNYL+Norfentanyl pnl Spec  **Definition:** This panel contains tests used to evaluate toxicity and overdose of fentanyl. Fentanyl (also known as fentanil, brand names Sublimaze, Actiq, Durogesic, Duragesic, and others) is a potent, synthetic opioid analgesic with a rapid onset and short duration of action. It has been used to treat breakthrough pain and is commonly used in pre-procedures as a pain reliever as well as an anesthetic in combination with a benzodiazepine.  Norfentanyl is an active metabolite of fentanyl.
[81274-3] Doxepin and Nordoxepin panel - Gastric fluid -- **Component:** Doxepin & Nordoxepin panel **Property:** - **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Gast fld **Scale:** -  **Short name:** Doxepin+Nor Pnl Gast  **Definition:** This panel contains terms to evaluate toxicity and overdose of doxepin, that is recommended for the treatment of psychoneurotic patients with depression or anxiety. Nordoxepin (N-desmethyldoxepin) is the major active metabolite and is usually present at concentrations equal to doxepin.

[81288-3] Precision of boundaries Nominal -- **Component:** Precision of boundaries **Property:** Find **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** Nom  **Short name:** Boundary precision  **Definition:** The precision of the nucleotide boundaries that can be specified for a structural variant can vary widely, and in some cases the boundary can only be pinpointed down to a region that is on the order of megabases. This term should be used to report the narrative description of the boundary precision. The nucleotide ranges for the narrowest and widest possible boundaries of a structural variant can be reported using [LOINC: 81302-2] and [LOINC: 81301-4], respectively.

[81293-3] Description of ranges of DNA sequences examined -- **Component:** Range(s) of DNA sequences examined **Property:** Txt **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** Nar  **Short name:** DNA range(s) examined Nar  **Definition:** This term is used to report a narrative description of the range(s) of DNA sequences examined in this sequencing study. Genetic test reports only rarely include explicit numeric ranges (which would be reported using [LOINC: 51959-5]) beause they are often proprietary, and more often describe the regions examined in narrative. For example, "all coding regions and appropriate flanking regions." To report the region of interest (e.g., in terms of introns and exons) rather than the specific DNA sequences examined, [LOINC: 47999-8] may be used.

[81320-4] Influenza virus A H5 Asian RNA [Cycle Threshold #] in Respiratory system specimen by Nucleic acid amplification using primer-probe set H5a -- **Component:** Influenza virus A H5 Asian RNA **Property:** ThreshNum **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Respiratory System Specimen **Scale:** Qn **Method:** Probe.amp.tar.primer-probe set H5a  **Short name:** FLUAV H5 Asian RNA Ct Resp Qn NAA H5a  **Definition:** This term represents the identification of the Influenza A H5 subtype based on the H5a probe and primer set provided by the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention. "H5a" does not refer to a more specific type of the H5 virus, rather it is the name of one of the two primer and probe sets (the other is H5b) that are used to detect the Influenza A H5 subtype. For presumptive positive identification of the H5 subtype, results for both the H5a and H5b assays should be positive. If one is positive and one is negative, the result is inconclusive for the H5 subtype. Possible specimens include: upper respiratory tract clinical specimens (including nasopharyngeal swabs, nasal swabs, throat swabs, nasal aspirates, nasal washes, and dual nasopharyngeal/throat swabs), and lower respiratory tract specimens (including bronchoalveolar lavage, bronchial wash, tracheal aspirate, sputum, and lung tissue.)

"H5b" does not refer to a more specific type of the H5 virus, rather it is the name of one of the two primer and probe sets (the other is H5a) that are used to detect the Influenza A H5 subtype. For presumptive positive identification of the H5 subtype, results for both the H5a and H5b assays should be positive. If one is positive and one is negative, the result is inconclusive for the H5 subtype. [81323-8] Urine collection associated observations panel - 24 hour Urine -- **Component:** Urine collection associated observations panel **Property:** - **Time aspect:** 24H **System:** Urine **Scale:** -  **Short name:** Urine collection assoc obs Pnl 24h Ur  **Definition:** This panel contains terms to record the details of the patient's 24 hour urine sample collection.
[81324-6] Glucose tolerance 2 hours gestational panel - Serum or Plasma -- **Component:** Glucose tolerance 2H gestational panel **Property:** - **Time aspect:** - **System:** Ser/Plas **Scale:** -  **Short name:** GTT gest 2h Pnl SerPl  **Definition:** Glucose blood tests are used to screen pregnant women for gestational diabetes between their 24th and 28th week of pregnancy. The Canadian Diabetes Association recommends that all pregnant women not previously known to have diabetes be screened for gestational diabetes using one of two approaches: a one-step 2-hour oral glucose tolerance test (OGTT) with a 75-gram glucose challenge, or a two-step approach with a 50-gram challenge followed by a 75-gram challenge when appropriate. This panel contains terms for the one-step approach. After a fasting glucose level is measured, a woman is given a 75-gram dose of glucose to drink and her glucose levels are measured at 1 hour and 2 hours after the dose. Only one of the values needs to be above a cutoff value for diagnosis of gestational diabetes.

[81421-0] Baker's yeast IgG Ab [Presence] in Serum by Immunoassay -- **Component:** Saccharomyces cerevisiae Ab.IgG **Property:** PrThr **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Ser **Scale:** Ord **Method:** IA  **Short name:** Baker's yeast IgG Ql IA  **Definition:** Saccharomyces cerevisiae antibodies (ASCA) testing is used to distinguish between Crohn's disease (CD) and ulcerative colitis (UC), the two most common types of inflammatory bowel disease (IBD). Testing usually includes detecting two different classes of ASCA in the blood, IgG and IgA, as well as other antibodies, including anti-neutrophil cytoplasmic antibodies (ANCA). ASCA are more common in patients with CD, while ANCA are more common in those with UC.

[81430-1] Lactate dehydrogenase [Enzymatic activity/volume] in Peritoneal dialysis fluid by Lactate to pyruvate reaction -- **Component:** Lactate dehydrogenase **Property:** CCnc **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Dial fld prt **Scale:** Qn **Method:** Reaction: lactate to pyruvate  **Short name:** LDH DiafP L to P-cCnc  **Definition:** Elevated lactate dehydrogenase is a marker for various types of tissue injury. In patients undergoing peritoneal dialysis, elevated LDH in the dialysate may be a sign of peritonitis, and failure of dialysate LDH levels to normalize during treatment of peritonitis may be associated with incomplete recovery and a higher rate of complications compared to patients whose LDH levels decrease back to normal levels. [PMID: 7948233]
[81431-9] Regurgitation degree Aortic valve Angiography -- **Component:** Regurgitation degree **Property:** Find **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Aortic valve **Scale:** Ord **Method:** Angiography  **Short name:** Reg deg AV Angio  **Definition:** Angiography is one method for determining the degree of aortic valve regurgitation. The Sellers grading system, originally published in 1964, is based on the opacification of the left ventricle and is still used today. The Sellers grading system is as follows: 1) + = evidence of a jet of regurgitant contrast material without opacification of the left ventricle; 2) ++ = evidence of a regurgitant jet with faint opacification of the left ventricle; 3) +++ = dense opacification of the left ventricle without a distinct visible jet; and 4) ++++ = left ventricle is opacified more densely than the aorta. [PMID: 14215054]
[81432-7] Regurgitation degree Mitral valve Angiography -- **Component:** Regurgitation degree **Property:** Find **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Mitral valve **Scale:** Ord **Method:** Angiography  **Short name:** Reg deg MV Angio  **Definition:** Angiography is one method for determining the degree of mitral valve regurgitation. The Sellers grading system, originally published in 1964, is based on the opacification of the left atrium and is still used today. The Sellers grading system is as follows: 1) + = a regurgitant jet is visible with minimal, rapidly clearing staining of the left atrium; 2) ++ = evidence of a regurgitant jet with moderate but still rapidly clearing opacification of the left atrium; 3) +++ = the left atrium is as opaque as the left ventricle and aorta on the late films and clears slowly. A jet usually is not seen and the left atrium is usually significantly enlarged; and 4) ++++ = the left atrium is more densely opacified than the left ventricle or the aorta. The left atrium is usually markedly enlarged and the left ventricle is dilated. The left atrium remains intensely opacified on the whole series of films. [PMID: 14215054]

[81572-0] NM Salivary gland Views for function -- **Component:** Views for function^W radionuclide IV **Property:** Find **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Head>Salivary gland **Scale:** Doc **Method:** NM  **Short name:** NM Salivary gland Vs function W RNC IV  **Definition:** Nuclear salivary gland imaging can be done to evaluate salivary gland function as well as patency of the salivary duct and salivary tumors. In the first phase, dynamic blood flow is oberved immediately after pertechnetate injection, and then dynamic imaging is done for about 20 minutes to evaluate gland uptake. Appearance of pertechnetate should begin a minute after injection and reach its peak between 10 and 20 minutes. In the second phase of the study, the patient is given lemon juice to drink, which stimulates the excretory function of the glands. In the second phase, either static images or longer dynamic acquisition that covers both the pre- and post- stimulus phases may be used may be acquired. With normal salivary gland functioning, the amount of pertechnetate visualized in the glands should begin to decrease almost immediately after lemon stimulation. [PMID: 19223429]

[81621-5] Herpes simplex virus 1 and 2 IgG panel - Serum or Plasma -- **Component:** Herpes simplex virus 1 & 2 Ab.IgG panel **Property:** - **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Ser/Plas **Scale:** -  **Short name:** HSV 1 + 2 IgG Pnl SerPl  **Definition:** This panel contains terms that measure HSV Type 1 (HSV-1) and HSV Type 2 (HSV-2) IgG antibodies. HSV Type 1 generally infects the mucous membrane of the eye, mouth and mucocutaneous junctions of the face, and is also one of the most common causes of severe sporadic encephalitis in adults. [81622-3] Hydroxyapatite [Energy Difference] in 24 hour Urine -- **Component:** Hydroxyapatite **Property:** EngDiff **Time aspect:** 24H **System:** Urine **Scale:** Qn  **Short name:** Hydroxyapatite 24h EngDiff Ur  **Definition:** Supersaturation of urine correlates with stone type. Measurement of supersaturated urine can be used for the diagnosis and management of patients with renal lithiasis, in patients who have a radiopaque stone, or when stone analysis is not possible. The supersaturation can be used to predict the likely composition. Urine supersaturation changes can also be used to monitor the effectiveness of therapy by confirming that the crystallization potential is been decreased. The differential Gibbs free energy is a measure of the thermodynamic pressure for crystals to form.  For this term, a software program, EQUIL [PMID: 3840540] was used to calculate the urinary supersaturation for the common kidney stone components expressed as the Gibbs free energy of transfer from a supersaturated to a saturated solution. The delta G (DG) will equal zero for solutions at saturation, will be greater than zero for super-saturated solutions, and less than zero for under-saturated solutions. Positive values above the reference mean are associated with increased risk for that type of crystal formation.

[81637-1] Glucose meter to reference method correlation [Ratio] in Serum, Plasma or Blood by calculation -- **Component:** Glucose meter to reference method correlation **Property:** Ratio **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Ser/Plas/Bld **Scale:** Qn **Method:** Calculated  **Short name:** Glucose correlation SerPlBld Calc-Rto  **Definition:** The glucose correlation is a charted value used to monitor the accuracy of glucose meter results as compared to reference method results for quality assurance purposes. According to draft guidance from the Food and Drug Administration in 2014, for point-of-care blood glucose meter devices used by healthcare providers, 99% of all values should be within 10% of the reference method for glucose concentrations > 70 mg/dL and within 7 mg/dL for glucose concentrations < 70 mg/dL.[http://www.fda.gov/downloads/medicaldevices/deviceregulationandguidance/guidancedocuments/ucm380325.pdf] For self-monitoring blood glucose meters used by patients, 95% of all results should be within 15% of the reference method result and 99% of all results should be within 20% of the reference method result across the entire claimed measuring range of the instrument. [http://www.fda.gov/downloads/medicaldevices/deviceregulationandguidance/guidancedocuments/ucm380327.pdf]

[81638-9] Clotting time of Capillary blood by Sukharev method -- **Component:** Clotting time **Property:** Time **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** BldC **Scale:** Qn **Method:** Sukharev  **Short name:** Clot tm BldC Sukharev  **Definition:** The Sukharev clotting time uses capillary blood and a test tube called the Panchenkov's device that is manually tilted every 30 seconds until the blood clot has formed. The tube has a diameter of 1mm and the reference range is 2-5 minutes. In contrast, the Lee White method, which is also manual, uses a tube with a diameter of 8-10mm, which results in a larger volume of blood and longer testing time with a reference range of 5-10 minutes.

[81642-1] CYP3A4 and CYP3A5 gene targeted mutation analysis panel - Blood or Tissue by Molecular genetics method -- **Component:** CYP3A4 & CYP3A5 gene targeted mutation analysis panel **Property:** - **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Bld/Tiss **Scale:** - **Method:** Molgen  **Short name:** CYP3A4 + CYP3A5 Pnl Bld/T  **Definition:** This panel contains terms that genotype CYP3A4 and CYP3A5, determine estimated metabolic activity, and provide interpretations.
[81643-9] von Willebrand factor (vWf) ristocetin cofactor/von Willebrand factor (vWf) Ag [Ratio] in Platelet poor plasma -- **Component:** von Willebrand factor.ristocetin cofactor activity/von Willebrand factor Ag **Property:** Ratio **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** PPP **Scale:** Qn  **Short name:** vWf:RCo/vWF Ag PPP-Rto  **Definition:** This term represents a ratio of two results that are expressed as percentages of normal: the von Willebrand factor.ristocetin cofactor activity and the von Willebrand factor antigen concentration, both of which are reported as actual/Normal. The von Willebrand factor (vWF) ristocetin cofactor activity to antigen ratio can help distinguish different types of von Willebrand disease (VWD). VWD types 1 and 3 are usually associated with a quantitative decrease in vWF, which would affect both the vWF ristocetin cofactor activity as well as vWF antigen concentration, while type 2 is associated with a functional defect in VWF, which would affect the activity but not the amount of antigen present. Therefore, patients with type 2 VWD are likely to have a lower vWF ristocetin cofactor activity to antigen ratio (less than 0.6-0.7) compared to patients with types 1 or 3 VWD.

[81679-3] Preoperative measures for patient safety panel -- **Component:** Preoperative measures for patient safety panel **Property:** - **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** -  **Short name:** Preop measures for pt safety pnl  **Definition:** Preoperative measures for patient safety include procedures that are intended to ensure patient safety in that the correct procedure is performed on the correct patient and patient body site and facilitate communication between surgical team members. Such measures include: 1) preoperative verification of patient identity, procedure and site; 2) marking the operative site; and 3) taking a time-out just prior to starting the procedure during which surgical team members communicate about and confirm patient identity, site and procedure, and resolve any other relevant questions or concerns. Multiple organizations have published protocols and/or checklists for prevention of surgical errors, including The Joint Commission (http://www.jointcommission.org/standards_information/up.aspx), the World Health Organization (http://www.who.int/patientsafety/safesurgery/checklist/en/) and the Association of periOperative Registered Nurses (https://www.aorn.org/guidelines/clinical-resources/tool-kits/correct-site-surgery-tool-kit/aorn-comprehensive-surgical-checklist).

[81712-2] Actin.filamentous Ab [Presence] in Serum by Line blot -- **Component:** Actin.filamentous Ab **Property:** PrThr **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Ser **Scale:** Ord **Method:** Line blot  **Short name:** F-actin Ab Ser Ql Line blot  **Definition:** This term was created for, but is not limited in use to, the submitter's test strip, a membrane strip pre-coated with a specific set of antigens in parallel lines. If antibodies to any of those antigens are present in the patient's serum, they will bind to the specific antigen. The bound patient antibodies are then detected using labelled anti-human antibodies. Various manufacturers classify this method as an immunoblot technique. LOINC uses the immunoblot (IB) method to mean that some electrophoretic/chromatographic separation occurs such that position (size) of the band is part of the assessment (as in Western blot). Because this assay does not involve a separation step but rather uses strips with pre-fixed antigens, the LOINC Method is Line blot.

Because this assay does not involve a separation step but rather uses strips with pre-fixed antigens, the LOINC Method is Line blot.
[81742-9] Th-To Ab [Units/volume] in Serum by Line blot -- **Component:** Th-To Ab **Property:** ACnc **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Ser **Scale:** Qn **Method:** Line blot  **Short name:** Th-To Ab Ser Line blot-aCnc  **Definition:** This term was created for, but is not limited in use to, the submitter's test strip, a membrane strip pre-coated with a specific set of antigens in parallel lines. If antibodies to any of those antigens are present in the patient's serum, they will bind to the specific antigen. The bound patient antibodies are then detected using labelled anti-human antibodies. Various manufacturers classify this method as an immunoblot technique. LOINC uses the immunoblot (IB) method to mean that some electrophoretic/chromatographic separation occurs such that position (size) of the band is part of the assessment (as in Western blot). Because this assay does not involve a separation step but rather uses strips with pre-fixed antigens, the LOINC Method is Line blot.

[81746-0] Chromosome region 17p13.1 deletion in Blood or Tissue by FISH -- **Component:** Chromosome region 17p13.1 deletion **Property:** Find **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Bld/Tiss **Scale:** Doc **Method:** FISH  **Short name:** Chr 17p13.1 Del Bld/T FISH  **Definition:** Deletions at 17p13.1 involving the TP53 gene can be detected by FISH and used for the diagnosis of chronic lymphocytic leukemia (CLL), multiple myeloma as well as other forms of cancer. Labs will report the number of cells that have the probe deletion out of total number of cells examined (e.g. 80 out of 100 cells, or 80%). [81879-9] t(11;22)(q24;q12.2)(FLI1,EWSR1) and t(21;22)(q22.3;q12.2)(ERG,EWSR1) fusion transcript in Blood or Tissue by Molecular genetics method -- **Component:** t(11;22)(q24;q12.2)(FLI1,EWSR1) & t(21;22)(q22.3;q12.2)(ERG,EWSR1) fusion transcript **Property:** Find **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Bld/Tiss **Scale:** Doc **Method:** Molgen  **Short name:** EWSR1-FLI1 + EWSR1-ERG Bld/T  **Definition:** Rearrangements of the EWSR1 gene at 22q12 with FLI1 at 11q24 or ERG at 21q22 collectively occur in about 95% of Ewing sarcoma (EWS) and primitive neuroectodermal tumors (PNET), both types of small round-cell tumors. The fusion transcripts can be detected by various methods, including reverse transcriptase PCR and FISH. A positive result is consistent with a diagnosis of EWS/PNET. A negative result does not rule out the presence of fusion transcripts that may be present, but below the detection limit of an assay.

[81891-4] Episode of care secondary diagnosis -- **Component:** Episode of care diagnosis.secondary **Property:** Imp **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** Nom  **Short name:** EOC dx.secondary  **Definition:** The episode of care secondary diagnosis code includes all of the billing codes except for the first one listed on the claim form for the most recent visit in a given episode of care. This term was created for, but is not limited in use to, the the American Physical Therapy Association's Physical Therapy Outcomes Registry, for which the diagnosis code will almost always be reported using ICD-10-CM.
[81892-2] Episode of care primary diagnosis -- **Component:** Episode of care diagnosis.primary **Property:** Imp **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** Nom  **Short name:** EOC dx.primary  **Definition:** The episode of care primary diagnosis code is the billing code that is listed first on the claim form for the most recent visit in a given episode of care. This term was created for, but is not limited in use to, the the American Physical Therapy Association's Physical Therapy Outcomes Registry, for which the diagnosis code will almost always be reported using ICD-10-CM.

[81893-0] Patient satisfaction with healthcare delivery [Score] -- **Component:** Patient satisfaction with healthcare delivery **Property:** Score **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** Qn  **Short name:** Pt satisfaction w healthcare Score  **Definition:** The patient's satisfaction with the care they received on a scale of 0-100, with 0 being the lowest possible satisfaction and 100 being the highest.
[81894-8] Liver ultrasound attenuation by transient elastography -- **Component:** Ultrasound attenuation **Property:** Pres **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Liver **Scale:** Qn **Method:** US.transient elastography  **Short name:** US.TE Liver attenuation  **Definition:** A measure of the attenuation of the ultrasound signal (loss of energy) as it travels through and returns from the liver at 3.5 MHz, often reported simultaneously with liver stiffness [LOINC:77615-3]. Based on the principle that ultrasound propagation is affected by tissue fat content, the ultrasound attenuation measurement, referred to as Controlled Attenuation Parameter (CAP), is a measure of liver fat content (steatosis) and expressed in dB/m. Increasing steatosis is associated with higher levels of signal attenuation. [PMID: 22404722]

[81907-8] Send document that conforms to C-CDA R2.0 template -- **Component:** Send document that conforms to C-CDA R2.0 template **Property:** Scope.modifier.doc template **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** X12^277 **Scale:** -  **Definition:** An attachment modifier code that indicates a request to send a document that conforms to the HL7 Consolidated Clinical Document Architecture (C-CDA) R2.0 template. The attachment modifier codes are intended for use in the X12 messaging standard. [81912-8] Amino acid intake panel -- **Component:** Amino acid intake panel **Property:** - **Time aspect:** - **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** -  **Short name:** Amino acid intake Pnl  **Definition:** This panel contains terms to capture the type and amount of amino acids consumed by a patient. Amino acids are organic compounds that combine to form proteins. Amino acids and proteins are the building blocks of life, and can be classified into essential amino acids (cannot be made by the body), and nonessential amino acids (can be synthesized within the body or derived in the body from essential amino acids). This term was created for, but not limited in use to, the Academy of Nutrition and Dietetics' Nutrition Assessment Language.
[81913-6] Essential amino acid intake panel -- **Component:** Amino acid.essential intake panel **Property:** - **Time aspect:** - **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** -  **Short name:** Amino acid essential intake Pnl  **Definition:** This panel contains terms that capture the type and amount of essential amino acid intake from all sources including food, beverages, and supplements, and via enteral and parenteral routes.  Essential amino acids cannot be made by the body and they must come from food. The 9 essential amino acids are: histidine, isoleucine, leucine, lysine, methionine, phenylalanine, threonine, tryptophan, and valine. This term was created for, but not limited in use to, the Academy of Nutrition and Dietetics' Nutrition Assessment Language.

[81951-6] Fluid intake 24 hour Measured -- **Component:** Fluid intake **Property:** VRat **Time aspect:** 24H **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** Qn **Method:** Measured  **Short name:** Fluid intake 24h Measured  **Definition:** The patient's measured fluid/beverage intake in a 24 hour period. This term was created for, but is not limited in use to, the Academy of Nutrition and Dietetics' Nutrition Assessment Language.
[81952-4] Fat intake 24 hour Measured -- **Component:** Fat intake **Property:** MRat **Time aspect:** 24H **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** Qn **Method:** Measured  **Short name:** Fat intake 24h Measured  **Definition:** A patient or client's total estimated fat and cholesterol consumption from all sources, (e.g., food, beverages, and supplements) via enteral and parenteral routes, for a 24 hour period, as measured in a study.  This term was created for, but is not limited in use to, the Academy of Nutrition and Dietetics' Nutrition Assessment Language.
[81953-2] Carbohydrate intake 24 hour Measured -- **Component:** Carbohydrate intake **Property:** MRat **Time aspect:** 24H **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** Qn **Method:** Measured  **Short name:** Carbohydrate intake 24h Measured  **Definition:** The patient's measured carbohydrate consumption from all sources, for example, food, beverages, and supplements, and via enteral and parenteral routes, as measured in a study. This term was created for, but is not limited in use to, the Academy of Nutrition and Dietetics' Nutrition Assessment Language.

[82123-1] MR Guidance for radiation treatment of Unspecified body region -- **Component:** Guidance for radiation treatment **Property:** Find **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** XXX **Scale:** Doc **Method:** MR  **Short name:** MR Guided RT  **Definition:** One of the first steps in radiation oncology treatment planning is radiation simulation, during which detailed imaging is done to show the precise location of a patient's tumor and the surrounding healthy tissue. This information guides treatment planning in that it is used to determine the optimal patient positioning, to mark the patient's skin with small dots of permanent ink (tattoos) to ensure that the same precise area is treated each time, and to create custom immobilization devices such as a face mask or a leg mold. Various modalities may be used for radiation simulation to guide radiation treatment planning, including computed tomography (CT) [LOINC: 79072-5], magnetic resonance imaging (MR) [LOINC: 82123-1], positron emission tomography (PT) [LOINC: 82126-4] and ultrasound (US) [LOINC: 82124-9].

[82152-0] Behavioral Risk Factor Surveillance System Adverse Childhood Experience Module 2009-2020 [ACE.BRFSS] -- **Component:** Behavioral Risk Factor Surveillance System Adverse Childhood Experience Module 2009-2020 **Property:** - **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** - **Method:** ACE.BRFSS  **Definition:** The Adverse Childhood Experiences (ACE) questionnaire surveys a person's exposure to child abuse and neglect and household challenges prior to age 18. The Behavior Risk Factor Surveillance System (BRFSS) is Center for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC) initiative that collects data from U.S. adults using random telephone surveys. The BRFSS began using an adapted version of the original CDC-Kaiser ACE module in 2009, which adaptation is not copyrighted and is available free of charge from the CDC [http://www.cdc.gov/violenceprevention/acestudy/ace_brfss.html]. The CDC definitions of the ACEs derived from the Kaiser-CDC study are also represented in LOINC [LOINC:82813-7].

[82158-7] Graduation state Provider -- **Component:** Graduation state **Property:** Loc **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Provider **Scale:** Nom  **Short name:** Grad state Provider  **Definition:** The state in the U.S. in which the training program the provider graduated from is located. [82159-5] Respiratory pathogens DNA and RNA panel - Nasopharynx by NAA with non-probe detection -- **Component:** Respiratory pathogens DNA & RNA panel **Property:** - **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Nph **Scale:** - **Method:** Non-probe.amp.tar  **Short name:** Resp path DNA+RNA Pnl Nph NAA+n-prb  **Definition:** This respiratory pathogens panel detects and identifies respiratory viral and bacterial nucleic acids in nasopharyngeal swabs from patients suspected of respiratory tract infections by target amplification and non-probe detection methods, such as melt curve analysis. This term was created for, but not limited in use to, the submitter's test kits, FilmArray Respiratory Panel (RP) and Respiratory Panel 2 Plus (RP2plus), which detect and identify various respiratory viruses and bacteria including Adenovirus, Bordetella species, Coronavirus 229E, Coronavirus HKU1, Coronavirus NL63, Coronavirus OC43, Human Metapneumovirus, Influenza A, Influenza A subtype H1, Influenza A subtype H3, Influenza A subtype H1-2009, Influenza B, MERS Coronavirus, Parainfluenza Virus 1, Parainfluenza Virus 2, Parainfluenza Virus 3, Parainfluenza Virus 4, Human Rhinovirus/Enterovirus, Respiratory Syncytial Virus, Bordetella pertussis, Chlamydophila pneumoniae, and Mycoplasma pneumoniae.

This panel was originally created for, but is not limited in use to, the FilmArray Gastrointestinal Panel (GI) (v2.1, 2015), which detects and identifies the following bacteria, parasites, and viruses: Campylobacter (C. jejuni/C. coli/C. upsaliensis), Clostridium difficile toxin A/B, Plesiomonas shigelloides, Salmonella, Vibrio (V. parahaemolyticus/V. vulnificus/V. cholerae), including specific identification of Vibrio cholerae, Yersinia enterocolitica, Enteroaggregative Escherichia coli (EAEC), Enteropathogenic Escherichia coli (EPEC), Enterotoxigenic Escherichia coli (ETEC) lt/st, Shiga-like toxin-producing Escherichia coli (STEC) stx1/stx2, Escherichia coli O157, Shigella/Enteroinvasive Escherichia coli (EIEC), Cryptosporidium, Cyclospora cayetanensis, Entamoeba histolytica, Giardia lamblia, Adenovirus F 40/41, Astrovirus, Norovirus GI/GII, Rotavirus A, and Sapovirus (Genogroups I, II, IV, and V).

[82237-9] Total score [NDI] -- **Component:** Total score **Property:** Score **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** Qn **Method:** NDI  **Definition:** Each of the ten items on the Neck Disability Index is scored one to five for a possible total of 50. A higher score indicates a greater level of disability (0-4=none, 5-14=mild, 15-24=moderate, 25-34=severe, >35=complete). If three or more items are missing, the total score could be invalid. The total score can be multiplied by two to calculate the percentage out of 100.

[82259-3] Metabolic equivalent of task and Resting metabolic rate panel -- **Component:** Metabolic equivalent of task & Resting metabolic rate panel **Property:** - **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** -  **Short name:** MET + RMR Pnl  **Definition:** This panel includes terms and nested panels to report the activity metabolic equivalent of task (MET), which is calculated as the exercise metabolic rate/resting metabolic rate, based on different methods for determining both the exercise and resting rates.
[82260-1] MET by standard RMR panel -- **Component:** Metabolic equivalent of task by standard resting metabolic rate panel **Property:** - **Time aspect:** 1M^mean **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** -  **Short name:** MET by stand RMR Pnl  **Definition:** This panel includes terms for the metabolic equivalent of task (MET) calculated based on either measured, estimated or reported exercise metabolic rate and the standard resting metabolic rate (RMR). The standard RMR is a reference baseline oxygen consumption constant of 3.5 mL/kg/min and is approximately equivalent to 1 kcal/kg/h or 4.184 kJ/kg/h.
[82261-9] Measured activity metabolic rate/Standard RMR [Relative energy/Time] -- **Component:** Activity metabolic rate/Standard resting metabolic rate **Property:** RelEngRat **Time aspect:** 1M^mean **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** Qn **Method:** Measured  **Short name:** Meas Act metab rate/Standard RMR  **Definition:** The measured metabolic equivalent of task (MET) calculated based on the measured exercise metabolic rate and the standard resting metabolic rate (RMR). The standard RMR is defined as 3.5 mL O2/kg/min. The exercise metabolic rate can be measured (this term), estimated [LOINC: 82262-7], or reported [LOINC: 82263-5]. Additionally, METs can be further adjusted based on patient demographics, such as age, sex and BMI [LOINC: 82271-8].

[82278-3] Measured RMR -- **Component:** Metabolic rate^resting **Property:** EngRat **Time aspect:** 1M^mean **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** Qn **Method:** Measured  **Short name:** Measured RMR  **Definition:** Resting metabolic rate (RMR), sometimes called resting energy expenditure (EE), is commonly measured by indirect calorimetry, which requires quantification of oxygen consumption and sometimes carbon dioxide production while a patient is resting. Indirect calorimetry techniques for measuring RMR are commonly used in research and clinical applications.[PMCID: PMC3928639] Results are used to determine minimal energy requirements for patients receiving nutritional therapy. RMR estimates are also used to counsel patients regarding caloric intake in weight loss programs.

[82287-4] Physical activity panel -- **Component:** Physical activity panel **Property:** - **Time aspect:** RptPeriod **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** -  **Short name:** Physical activity Pnl  **Definition:** This panel contains observations used to evaluate a patient's frequency and/or level of physical activity. The panel also contains observations for reporting the patient's absolute and relative (rating of perceived exertion) intensity levels of aerobic physical activity. The results may be used to determine if a patient is not meeting, meeting or exceeding the recommended amount of aerobic, bone-strengthening, and/or muscle-strengthening physical activities based on a specific guideline, such as the 2008 Physical Activity Guidelines for Americans (2008 PAG). The 2008 PAG was issued by the U.S. Department of Health and Human Services (HHS) to provide information and guidance on the types and amounts of physical activity recommended for children, adolescents, and adults in order to achieve substantial health benefits.[http://health.gov/paguidelines/pdf/paguide.pdf]
[82288-2] Absolute aerobic exercise intensity -- **Component:** Absolute aerobic exercise intensity **Property:** Type **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** Ord  **Short name:** Absolute aerobic exer intensity  **Definition:** The patient's level of aerobic absolute exercise intensity based on the rate of energy expenditure during aerobic (endurance) physical activity. This term was created for, but not limited in use to, the absolute intensity level as classified by the 2008 Physical Activity Guidelines (PAG) for Americans. Based on the 2008 PAG, light-intensity activity expends 1.1 to 2.9 times the amount of energy expended when a person is at rest, moderate-intensity activities expend 3.0 to 5.9 times, and vigorous-intensity activities expend 6.0 or more times the energy expended at rest.
[8228-9] Phencyclidine [Presence] in Gastric fluid -- **Component:** Phencyclidine **Property:** PrThr **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Gast fld **Scale:** Ord  **Short name:** PCP Gast Ql
[82289-0] Rating of perceived exertion [Score] -- **Component:** Rating of perceived exertion **Property:** Score **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** Ord  **Short name:** RPE Score  **Definition:** The patient's perceived relative intensity level based on their level of effort relative to his or her fitness. Patients who are less fit generally require a higher level of effort than fitter people who do the same activity. This term was created for, but not limited in use to, the relative intensity level as classified by the 2008 Physical Activity Guidelines (PAG) for Americans. According to the 2008 PAG, on a scale of 0 to 10, where sitting is 0 and the highest level of effort possible is 10, moderate-intensity activity is at a level of 5 or 6, and vigorous-intensity activity is a 7 or 8.
[82290-8] Frequency of moderate to vigorous aerobic physical activity -- **Component:** Frequency of moderate to vigorous aerobic physical activity **Property:** NRat **Time aspect:** RptPeriod **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** Qn  **Short name:** Freq aerobic physical activity  **Definition:** The frequency (days/week, min/week, hours/day, etc.) that the patient spends doing moderate to vigorous aerobic physical activity per week. According to the 2008 Physical activity guidelines (PAG) for Americans, adult patients should spend at least 150 minutes (2 hours and 30 minutes) a week doing moderate-intensity aerobic physical activity or 75 minutes (1 hour and 15 minutes) a week of vigorous-intensity aerobic physical activity. Adults who do 300 minutes (5 hours) of moderate intensity aerobic physical activity a week, or 150 minutes of vigorous intensity aerobic physical activity, have greater health benefits. For children and adolescents, the 2008 PAG recommends about 60 minutes or more per day and at least 3 days per week of either moderate- or vigorous-intensity aerobic physical activity.
[82291-6] Frequency of muscle-strengthening physical activity -- **Component:** Frequency of muscle-strengthening physical activity **Property:** NRat **Time aspect:** RptPeriod **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** Qn  **Short name:** Freq muscle-strengthening  **Definition:** The frequency (days/week, hours/day, etc.) a patient spends doing muscle-strengthening activities. According to the 2008 Physical Activity Guidelines for Americans, children and adolescents should do muscle-strengthening physical activities at least 3 days per week and adults should do muscle-strengthening activities that are moderate or high intensity and involve all major muscle group at least 2 days a week.
[82292-4] Meeting recommended guidelines for physical activity -- **Component:** Meeting recommended guidelines for physical activity **Property:** Find **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** Ord  **Short name:** Meeting guidelines phys activity  **Definition:** Indicates whether a patient is meeting the recommended guidelines for various types of physical activities, including aerobic, bone-strengthening and muscle-strengthening activities. For example, according to the 2008 Physical Activity Guidelines for Americans (2008 PAG), children and adolescents should do muscle-strengthening physical activities at least 3 days per week as part of their 60 minutes or more of daily physical activity. If they do muscle-strengthen activities less than 3 day per week, they are not meeting the recommended guidelines according to the 2008 PAG. If they do more than 3 days per week, they are exceeding the recommended amount.
[82293-2] Frequency of bone-strengthening physical activity -- **Component:** Frequency of bone-strengthening physical activity **Property:** NRat **Time aspect:** RptPeriod **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** Qn  **Short name:** Freq bone-strengthening  **Definition:** The frequency (days/week, hours/day, etc.) a patient spends doing bone-strengthening activities (sometimes called weight-bearing or weight-loading activity). This kind of activity is commonly produced by impact with the ground and promotes bone growth and strength. According to the 2008 Physical Activity Guidelines (PAG) for Americans, children and adolescents should include bone-strengthening physical activity on at least 3 days of the week as part of their 60 or more minutes of daily physical activity. Bone-strengthening activities can also be aerobic and muscle-strengthening. Examples include running, jumping rope, basketball, tennis, and hopscotch.

[82296-5] Method used to measure RMR -- **Component:** Method used to measure RMR **Property:** Type **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** Nom  **Short name:** Method used for RMR  **Definition:** Resting metabolic rate (RMR), sometimes called resting energy expenditure (EE), can be measured by direct calorimetry using a metabolic chamber. In the chamber, the difference in the temperature of water entering and leaving the chamber reflects the patient's heat production. Direct calorimetry is conducted in controlled environmental chambers, but the clinical and practical application using this technique is quite limited.[PMCID: PMC3928639]
