[82316-1] Hip dysfunction and osteoarthritis outcome score for joint replacement [HOOSJR] -- **Component:** Hip dysfunction and osteoarthritis outcome score for joint replacement **Property:** - **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** - **Method:** HOOSJR  **Definition:** The Hip Dysfunction and Osteoarthritis Outcome Score for Joint Replacement (HOOS, JR) survey was developed from the original long version of the Hip Dysfunction and Osteoarthritis Outcome Score (HOOS) survey using Rasch analysis. It contains 6 questions from the original HOOS survey. Each question is coded from 0 to 4 where 0 equals no pain and 4 equals extreme pain. The survey is scored by summing the raw scores and then converting the total raw score to an interval score, which ranges from 0 to 100 where 0 represents total hip disability and 100 represents perfect hip health.[PMID: 26926772]

It contains 7 questions from the original KOOS survey. Each question is coded from 0 to 4 where 0 equals no pain and 4 equals extreme pain. The survey is scored by summing the raw scores and then converting the total raw score to an interval score, which ranges from 0 to 100 where 0 represents total knee disability and 100 represents perfect knee health.[PMID: 26926773]

[82337-7] Right atrial Intrachamber pressure estimated by US -- **Component:** Intrachamber **Property:** Pres **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Heart.atrium.right **Scale:** Qn **Method:** US+Estimated  **Short name:** RA Intrachamber pres US+est  **Definition:** Right atrial pressure (RAP) can be estimated based on the diameter and collapsibility of the inferior vena cava (IVC) during inspiration as measured by ultrasound. Smaller diameter with greater than 50% collapsibility generally indicates normal RAP, while larger diameter and less than 50% collapsibility may indicate elevated RAP. The estimated RAP can be combined with tricuspid regurgitant jet velocity measurement to estimate the systolic right ventricular pressure [LOINC: 82341-9]. [PMID: 20620859]

[82354-2] Human papilloma virus 16 and 18+45 E6+E7 mRNA [Identifier] in Cervix by NAA with probe detection -- **Component:** Human papilloma virus 16 & 18+45 E6+E7 mRNA **Property:** Prid **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Cvx **Scale:** Nom **Method:** Probe.amp.tar  **Short name:** HPV16+18+45 E6+E7mRNA Cvx NAA+probe  **Definition:** Detects E6/E7 viral messenger RNA (mRNA) of human papillomavirus (HPV) types 16, 18, and 45 in cervical specimens from women with positive HPV assay results. For women 21-years and older with atypical squamous cells of undetermined significance (ASC-US) cervical cytology results, and HPV positive assay results, this assay can be used to assess the presence or absence of high-risk HPV genotypes 16, 18, and/or 45. For women 30-years and older, the HPV 16, 18, and/or 45 genotype assay can be used to test samples from women with HPV assay positive results, along with cervical cytology results. This assay has not been evaluated for HPV vaccinated patients or in cases of suspected abuse. It differentiates HPV 16 from HPV 18 and/or HPV 45, but does not differentiate HPV 18 and HPV 45. This term was created for, but not limited in use to, the Aptima HPV 16 18/45 genotype assay which uses target capture, target amplification by transcription-mediated amplification and detection of the amplification products by hybridization protection assay (HPA).

[82367-4] Peripheral deiodinase activity [Moles/time] in Serum or Plasma by Calculated.SPINA -- **Component:** Peripheral deiodinase activity **Property:** SRat **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Ser/Plas **Scale:** Qn **Method:** Calculated.SPINA  **Short name:** Per deiod act SerPl Calc SPINA-sRate  **Definition:** The sum activity of peripheral deiodinases (GD, also referred to as deiodination capacity or total deiodinase activity) is the maximum amount of triiodothyronine (T3) produced per time-unit by deiodinases outside the central nervous system. The structure parameter inference approach (SPINA) uses Michaelis-Menten functions and pharmakokinetic data to estimate GD under conditions of substrate saturation. One calculation is based on peripheral free T3 and free T4 values, and an alternate calculation uses peripheral total T3 and free T4. GD calculated using the SPINA approach is also known as SPINA-GD. SPINA-GD may be useful in diagnosing thyroid conditions that are difficult to diagnose based on traditional thyroid function studies, such as subclinical forms of thyroid failure, hypothalamic or pituitary dysfunction, and non-thyroidal illness syndrome. [PMID: 27375554] Thyroid secretory function by SPINA, called SPINA-GT, is a related result [LOINC: 82368-2].
[82368-2] Thyroid secretory capacity [Moles/time] in Serum or Plasma by Calculated.SPINA -- **Component:** Thyroid secretory capacity **Property:** SRat **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Ser/Plas **Scale:** Qn **Method:** Calculated.SPINA  **Short name:** Thyr secr cap SerPl Calc SPINA-sRate  **Definition:** Thyroid secretory capacity (also called thyroid output or GT) is the maximum secretion rate of the thyroid gland under stimulated conditions. The structure parameter inference approach (SPINA) uses Michaelis-Menten functions and pharmakokinetic data to estimate GT under conditions of substrate saturation. One calculation is based on peripheral TSH, free T4, thyroid binding globulin and thyroid binding prealbumin values, and an alternate calculation uses peripheral TSH and total T4. GT calculated using the SPINA approach is also known as SPINA-GT. SPINA-GT may be useful in diagnosing secondary or tertiary thyroid conditions and has also been shown to be able to clearly differentiate between euthyroidism and functional thyroid disorders of primary origin. [PMID: 27375554] Peripheral deiodinase activity by SPINA, called SPINA-GD, is a related result [LOINC: 82367-4].

[82388-0] 3-Hydroxy-3-Methylglutaryl-coenzyme A reductase Ab [Units/volume] in Serum by Line blot -- **Component:** 3-Hydroxy-3-Methylglutaryl-coenzyme A reductase Ab **Property:** ACnc **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Ser **Scale:** Qn **Method:** Line blot  **Short name:** HMGCR Ab Ser Line blot-aCnc  **Definition:** This term was created for, but is not limited in use to, the submitter's test strip, a membrane strip pre-coated with a specific set of antigens in parallel lines. If antibodies to any of those antigens are present in the patient's serum, they will bind to the specific antigen. The bound patient antibodies are then detected using labelled anti-human antibodies. Various manufacturers classify this method as an immunoblot technique. LOINC uses the immunoblot (IB) method to mean that some electrophoretic/chromatographic separation occurs such that position (size) of the band is part of the assessment (as in Western blot). Because this assay does not involve a separation step but rather uses strips with pre-fixed antigens, the LOINC Method is Line blot.

Various manufacturers classify this method as an immunoblot technique. LOINC uses the immunoblot (IB) method to mean that some electrophoretic/chromatographic separation occurs such that position (size) of the band is part of the assessment (as in Western blot). Because this assay does not involve a separation step but rather uses strips with pre-fixed antigens, the LOINC Method is Line blot.

If antibodies to any of those antigens are present in the patient's serum, they will bind to the specific antigen. The bound patient antibodies are then detected using labelled anti-human antibodies. Various manufacturers classify this method as an immunoblot technique. LOINC uses the immunoblot (IB) method to mean that some electrophoretic/chromatographic separation occurs such that position (size) of the band is part of the assessment (as in Western blot). Because this assay does not involve a separation step but rather uses strips with pre-fixed antigens, the LOINC Method is Line blot.

[82479-7] TRBV30 gene segment/TRBV gene segments.total in Blood -- **Component:** TRBV30 gene segment/TRBV gene segments.total **Property:** NFr **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Bld **Scale:** Qn  **Short name:** TRBV30 seg/TRBV seg total NFr Bld  **Definition:** This assay represents the fraction of T-cell receptor beta chain variable gene segments that are in the TRBV30 gene family out of the total number of TRBV gene segments identified ("diversity ratio"). Because some portion of TRBV gene segments that are counted in the total will not correspond to any of the probes and therefore will not be identified as belonging to a specific TRBV gene family, the total of the diversity ratios reported will not equal 100%.

[82506-7] Oligosaccharides pattern [Interpretation] in Fibroblast by Screen method Narrative -- **Component:** Oligosaccharides pattern **Property:** Imp **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Fibroblasts **Scale:** Nar **Method:** Screen  **Short name:** Oligosaccharide Pattern Fib Scn-Imp  **Definition:** Oligosaccharidoses are a group of autosomal recessively inherited lysosomal disorders of glycoprotein catabolism. There is no treatment available at this time for these disorders. This assay tests for the activities of alpha-neuraminidase, alpha-mannosidase, beta-mannosidase, alpha-n-acetyl-galactosaminidase, beta-galactosidase, and alpha-fucosidase, and results are reported as an overall interpretation based on the pattern of activity that is found.

[82528-1] VHL gene full mutation analysis in Blood or Tissue by Sequencing -- **Component:** VHL gene full mutation analysis **Property:** Find **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Bld/Tiss **Scale:** Doc **Method:** Sequencing  **Short name:** VHL gene Full Mut Anl Bld/T Seq  **Definition:** Full sequencing analysis of the three coding exons and intron/exon boundaries of the VHL gene.[OMIM: 608537][NCBI Gene ID: 7428] This test is performed for patients with suspected hereditary erythrocytosis, or familial erythrocytosis, type 2 (ECYT2)[OMIM: 263400], associated with lifelong increased RBC mass, elevated RBC count, hemoglobin, and hematocrit. Mutations in the VHL gene that cause ECYT2 are inherited in an autosomal recessive fashion and associated with normal to increased serum erythropoietin (EPO) levels. For genetic testing for von Hippel-Lindau (VHL) disease, see VHL gene deletion+duplication & full mutation analysis [LOINC: 82533-1].

[82538-0] CALR gene exon 9 full mutation analysis in Blood or Tissue by Molecular genetics method -- **Component:** CALR gene exon 9 full mutation analysis **Property:** Find **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Bld/Tiss **Scale:** Doc **Method:** Molgen  **Short name:** CALR Exon 9 Full Mut Anl Bld/T  **Definition:** Full sequencing analysis of exon 9 in the CALR gene is performed to establish a diagnosis of essential thrombocythemia (ET) or primary myelofibrosis (PMF).[OMIM: 109091] All the pathologic CALR mutations reported to date are frame-shift mutations due to somatic insertions or deletions.[PMID: 24325359] Two variants, a 52-base pair deletion (c.1092_1143del, L367fs*46) and a 5-bp insertion (c.1154_1155insTTGCC, K385fs*47), are the more common and account for more than 80% of the CALR mutations seen.[PMID: 24325356] Variants outside of exon 9 are not detected by this test.

[82586-9] Nucleated cells [#] in Bone marrow by Automated count -- **Component:** Nucleated cells **Property:** Num **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Bone mar **Scale:** Qn **Method:** Automated count  **Short name:** Nuc cell Mar Auto
[82587-7] Leukocytes [#/volume] in Bone marrow by Automated count -- **Component:** Leukocytes **Property:** NCnc **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Bone mar **Scale:** Qn **Method:** Automated count  **Short name:** WBC # Mar Auto  **Definition:** A diagnostic bone marrow aspiration may be used to determine the cause of unexplained low or high blood cells counts, including too few or too many white blood cells (WBCs) (leukopenia or leukocytosis). A variety of disorders and conditions may be identified, monitored, and/or staged with this process. For example, leukemia is a cancer of the blood cells which results in the excessive production of one type of white blood cell, resulting in the presence of large quantities of cells that do not function as normal ones do and the absence of the typical distribution of white blood cells. (Lab Tests Online)
[82588-5] Erythrocytes [#/volume] in Bone marrow by Automated count -- **Component:** Erythrocytes **Property:** NCnc **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Bone mar **Scale:** Qn **Method:** Automated count  **Short name:** RBC # Mar Auto  **Definition:** A diagnostic bone marrow aspiration may be used to determine the cause of unexplained low or high peripheral blood cells counts, including too few or too many red blood cells (RBCs) (anemia or polycythemia). When the RBC concentration in blood decreases, the marrow typically increases the rate of RBC production until there is a sufficient number of RBCs in the blood or until marrow production capacity is reached. If the bone marrow cannot produce sufficient quantities of RBCs, the individual will become increasingly anemic, with symptoms such as pallor, fatigue, and shortness of breath due to decreased oxygen-carrying capacity by the red blood cells. Anemia can also occur if the production of RBC in the marrow is suppressed. Polycythemia is defined as an increase in the number of erythrocytes in the blood and marrow. (Lab Tests Online) https://labtestsonline.org/understanding/analytes/bone-marrow/tab/test/

[82606-5] Allergy or intolerance criticality -- **Component:** Allergy or intolerance criticality **Property:** Imp **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** Ord  **Short name:** Allergy or intolerance criticality  **Definition:** A clinical judgment as to the worst case for the severity of a future reaction. 'High criticality' is flagged if the clinician has identified a propensity for a more serious or potentially life-threatening reaction, such as anaphylaxis, and implies an absolute contraindication to deliberate or voluntary exposure to the substance. This term was created for, but not limited in use to, the Allergy Intolerance Criticality observation used in the HL7 CDA® R2 IG: C-CDA Templates for Clinical Notes DSTU Release 2.1 - US Realm.

[82608-1] Advancing care information for population -- **Component:** Advancing care information **Property:** Find **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** {Setting}^Population **Scale:** Doc  **Definition:** Advancing Care Information (ACI) is a performance category under the Merit-based Incentive Payment System (MIPS). The ACI section is used to capture ACI quality reporting measures from providers over a specific reporting period. Providers report on various objectives, including protection of patient health information, electronic prescribing, patient electronic access, coordination of patient care, health information exchange, and public health reporting. This term was created for, but not limited in use to, ACI section in the HL7 CDA Quality Reporting Document Architecture (QRDA) III document.

The CPIA section is used to capture clinical practice improvement activities, such as care coordination, shared decision-making, safety checklists, and expanded practice access. This term was created for, but not limited in use to, the HL7 CDA Quality Reporting Document Architecture (QRDA) III document.

[82665-1] Total score [DAST] -- **Component:** Total Score **Property:** Score **Time aspect:** 12Mo **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** Qn **Method:** DAST  **Definition:** Each of the 28 DAST items receive a score of 0 or 1. The total score is the sum of the item scores. A total score of 6 or above may indicate a substance abuse or dependence problem; however, some researchers have suggested different cutoff scores for different populations. [PMID:17306727]

[82674-3] Asthma Control Test [ACT] -- **Component:** Asthma Control Test **Property:** - **Time aspect:** 4W **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** - **Method:** ACT  **Definition:** The Asthma Control Test (ACT) is a patient-reported measure that assesses the degree of asthma control based on asthma symptoms, use of rescue medications, and effect of asthma on daily function. The 5 items ask the patient to rate asthma symptoms and related behaviors over the previous 4 weeks. Each item is scored on a scale of 1-5, with higher scores indicating greater asthma control. A total score of 20 or higher indicates well-controlled asthma [PMID: 19767070], while a total score of 19 or less indicates poorly controlled asthma [PMID: 16522452].

[82720-4] Natural killer cell cytotoxicity panel - Blood mononuclear cells -- **Component:** Natural killer cell cytotoxicity panel **Property:** - **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Bld MC **Scale:** -  **Short name:** NK cytotox Pnl Bld MC  **Definition:** The natural killer (NK) cytotoxicity profile assesses the functionality of natural killer cells and is used to evaluate patients with various conditions, including recurrent herpes viral infections, hemophagocytic lymphohistiocytosis, and suspected or known monogenic defects. The NK cytotoxicity profile is also used for the evaluation of immune reconstitution post hematopoietic cell transplantation and post immunomodulatory therapy. Peripheral blood mononuclear cells (PBMCs) are used as the specimen for NK cytotoxicity assays because the cytotoxicity of the MC population is predominantly due to NK cells. In the cytotoxicity assay, samples of the patient's mononuclear cells (called 'effector cells' or E cells') are incubated with labeled target cells ('T cells') in a series of predetermined ratios, for example, 100:1 E to T, 50:1 E to T, 25:1 E to T. Cytotoxicity is measured as the amount of label released when the target cell is destroyed, and the percent cytotoxicity for a given E:T ratio is the ratio of label released when all of the target cells are destroyed using a cell lysis detergent as an experimental control. Several features of the cytotoxicity profile are important, including the maximum cytotoxicity, the relative difference between the maximum and the minimum, and the change in percent cytotoxicity between sequential dilutions.  Different patterns of these measures may be associated with different disease processes. Cytotoxicity may also be reported in terms of lytic units [LOINC: 34439-0], meaning the number of E cells needed to lyse a certain number of target cells.

[82722-0] Potassium [Mass/volume] in Red Blood Cells -- **Component:** Potassium **Property:** MCnc **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** RBC **Scale:** Qn  **Short name:** Potassium RBC-mCnc  **Definition:** Among its many physiological functions in the body, potassium controls heart function and subsequently blood pressure.  Patients with hypertension and those who may develop it have been found to have lower intracellular potassium levels than patients with normal blood pressure. The measurement of intracellular potassium is a useful marker to predict the risk, development and treatment of patients with hypertension.( http://pubs.acs.org/doi/pdf/10.1021/ac900776d) Calculation:  Hct= a(1-R)/a+R(b-a)  where 'a' is the fraction of water in the plasma (typically a=0.93), 'b' is the fraction of water inside the erythrocytes (typically b=0.65), 'R' ratio of sodium ion concentration measured before and after lysing the RBCs

[82812-9] Sodium [Mass/volume] in Red Blood Cells -- **Component:** Sodium **Property:** MCnc **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** RBC **Scale:** Qn  **Short name:** Sodium RBC-mCnc  **Definition:** Measurement of intracellular sodium is performed in concert with the intracellular measurement of potassium.  Intracellular potassium is a marker for the development of hypertension. The measurement is based on the fact that intracellular sodium is very low compared to plasma sodium.  When the cells are lysed in order to measure the intracellular potassium, the hematocrit of the sample is determined by the dilution of the plasma sodium by the now free intracellular sodium. (http://pubs.acs.org/doi/pdf/10.1021/ac900776d)
[82813-7] Adverse Childhood Experiences [ACE] -- **Component:** Adverse Childhood Experiences **Property:** - **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** - **Method:** ACE  **Definition:** From 1995 to 1997, Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC) and Kaiser Permanente surveyed over 17,000 participants on life-events and health-status. Study results demonstrate links between adverse childhood experiences (ACEs) and adult health. [PMID:9635069] CDC defines Adverse Childhood Experiences (ACEs) as: emotional abuse, physical abuse, sexual abuse, mother treated violently, household substance abuse, mental illness in household, parental separation or divorce, criminal household member, emotional neglect, and physical neglect [CDC:violenceprevention/acestudy]. Starting in 2009, states began collecting ACEs data using the Behavior Risk Factor Surveillance System (BRFSS), and the BRFSS survey form of the ACEs is available in LOINC [LOINC: 82152-0].
[82814-5] Emotional abuse--before 18 years old [ACE] -- **Component:** Emotional abuse^pre 18Yo **Property:** Find **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** Ord **Method:** ACE  **Definition:** The Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC) defines an Adverse Childhood Experience (ACE) of emotional abuse as: "A parent, stepparent, or adult living in your home swore at you, insulted you, put you down, or acted in a way that made you afraid that you might be physically hurt" during the first 18 years of life. [CDC:violenceprevention/acestudy]
[82815-2] Physical abuse--before 18 years old [ACE] -- **Component:** Physical abuse^pre 18Yo **Property:** Find **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** Ord **Method:** ACE  **Definition:** The Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC) defines an Adverse Childhood Experience (ACE) of physical abuse as: "A parent, stepparent, or adult living in your home pushed, grabbed, slapped, threw something at you, or hit you so hard that you had marks or were injured" during the first 18 years of life. [CDC:violenceprevention/acestudy]
[82816-0] Sexual abuse--before 18 years old [ACE] -- **Component:** Sexual abuse^pre 18Yo **Property:** Find **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** Ord **Method:** ACE  **Definition:** The Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC) defines an Adverse Childhood Experience (ACE) of sexual abuse as: "An adult, relative, family friend, or stranger who was at least 5 years older than you ever touched or fondled your body in a sexual way, made you touch his/her body in a sexual way, attempted to have any type of sexual intercourse with you" during the first 18 years of life. [CDC:violenceprevention/acestudy]
[82817-8] Emotional neglect--before 18 years old [ACE] -- **Component:** Emotional neglect^pre 18Yo **Property:** Find **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** Ord **Method:** ACE  **Definition:** The Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC) defines an Adverse Childhood Experience (ACEs) of emotional neglect as: the absence of "someone in your family helped you feel important or special, you felt loved, people in your family looked out for each other and felt close to each other, and your family was a source of strength and support"during the first 18 years of life. [CDC:violenceprevention/acestudy]

[82818-6] Physical neglect--before 18 years old [ACE] -- **Component:** Physical neglect^pre 18Yo **Property:** Find **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** Ord **Method:** ACE  **Definition:** The Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC) defines an Adverse Childhood Experience (ACE) of physical neglect as: the absence of "there was someone to take care of you, protect you, and take you to the doctor if you needed it" or presence of "you didn't have enough to eat, your parents were too drunk or too high to take care of you, and you had to wear dirty clothes" during the first 18 years of life. [CDC:violenceprevention/acestudy]
[82819-4] Parental separation or divorce--before 18 years old [ACE] -- **Component:** Parental separation or divorce^pre 18Yo **Property:** Find **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** Ord **Method:** ACE  **Definition:** The Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC) defines an Adverse Childhood Experience (ACE) of parental separation or divorce as: "Your parents were ever separated or divorced" during the first 18 years of life. [CDC:violenceprevention/acestudy]
[82820-2] Domestic violence--before 18 years old [ACE] -- **Component:** Domestic violence^pre 18Yo **Property:** Find **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** Ord **Method:** ACE  **Definition:** The Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC) defines an Adverse Childhood Experience (ACE) of domestic violence (also called "mother treated violently") as: "your mother or stepmother was pushed, grabbed, slapped, had something thrown at her, kicked, bitten, hit with a fist, hit with something hard, repeatedly hit for over at least a few minutes, or ever threatened or hurt by a knife or gun by your father (or stepfather) or mother's boyfriend" during the respondent's first 18 years of life. [CDC:violenceprevention/acestudy]
[82821-0] Household substance abuse--before 18 years old [ACE] -- **Component:** Household substance abuse^pre 18Yo **Property:** Find **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** Ord **Method:** ACE  **Definition:** The Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC) defines an Adverse Childhood Experience (ACE) of household substance abuse as: "A household member was a problem drinker or alcoholic or a household member used street drugs." during the first 18 years of life. [CDC:violenceprevention/acestudy]
[82822-8] Mental illness in household--before 18 years old [ACE] -- **Component:** Mental illness in household^pre 18Yo **Property:** Find **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** Ord **Method:** ACE  **Definition:** The Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC) defines an Adverse Childhood Experience (ACE) of mental illness in household as: "A household member was depressed or mentally ill or a household member attempted suicide." during the first 18 years of life. [CDC:violenceprevention/acestudy]
[82823-6] Criminal household member--before 18 years old [ACE] -- **Component:** Criminal household member^pre 18Yo **Property:** Find **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** Ord **Method:** ACE  **Definition:** The Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC) defines an Adverse Childhood Experience (ACE) of criminal household member as: "A household member went to prison" during the first 18 years of life. [CDC:violenceprevention/acestudy]

[82904-4] t(9;22)(q34.1;q11)(ABL1,BCR) fusion transcript [Presence] in Bone marrow by Molecular genetics method -- **Component:** t(9;22)(q34.1;q11)(ABL1,BCR) fusion transcript **Property:** PrThr **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Bone mar **Scale:** Ord **Method:** Molgen  **Short name:** t(9;22)(ABL1,BCR) Mar Ql
[82905-1] t(9;22)(q34.1;q11)(ABL1,BCR) fusion transcript/control transcript [Log Number Ratio] in Bone marrow by Molecular genetics method -- **Component:** t(9;22)(q34.1;q11)(ABL1,BCR) fusion transcript/control transcript **Property:** LnRto **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Bone mar **Scale:** Qn **Method:** Molgen  **Short name:** t(9;22)(ABL1,BCR)/control Mar-LnRto  **Definition:** The level of fusion gene transcript in the test sample is expressed as a log reduction; i.e., log10 of the ratio of chimeric BCR-ABL/normal ABL mRNA transcript seen in individuals with untreated CML (Chronic Myelogenous Leukemia) as compared to the ratio seen in the patient. In Quest's laboratory data, the median of BCR-ABL/ABL Ratio in previously untreated CML patients is 4.1325 in peripheral blood samples (N=120) and 5.09 in bone marrow samples (N=109). Based on the IRIS study (New England Journal of Medicine, 2003, 3349:1423-1432), it is not necessary to know the BCR-ABL:ABL ratio of a patient at baseline to calculate the subsequent change and this calculation should be based on the above-mentioned median. Based on this study, a 3 log of higher reduction by 12 months of therapy is associated with negligible risk of disease progression during the subsequent 12 months with patients are treated with imatinib. The following formula is used for calculating log reduction by Quest Laboratories: LOG10(4.1325)-LOG10 (BCR-ABL:ABL ratio) Unit/Log Reduction. Information from Quest Diagnostics. 2006 12 06.

[82913-5] Centromere protein B IgG Ab [Presence] in Serum by Line blot -- **Component:** Centromere protein B Ab.IgG **Property:** PrThr **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Ser **Scale:** Ord **Method:** Line blot  **Short name:** Centromere B IgG Ser Ql Line blot  **Definition:** This term was created for, but is not limited in use to, the submitter's test strip, a membrane strip pre-coated with a specific set of antigens in parallel lines. If antibodies to any of those antigens are present in the patient's serum, they will bind to the specific antigen. The bound patient antibodies are then detected using labelled anti-human antibodies. Various manufacturers classify this method as an immunoblot technique. LOINC uses the immunoblot (IB) method to mean that some electrophoretic/chromatographic separation occurs such that position (size) of the band is part of the assessment (as in Western blot). Because this assay does not involve a separation step but rather uses strips with pre-fixed antigens, the LOINC Method is Line blot.

[82937-4] SAP and XIAP [Interpretation] in Leukocytes by Flow cytometry (FC) -- **Component:** Signaling lymphocytic activation molecule (SLAM)-associated & X-linked inhibitor of apoptosis protein **Property:** Imp **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** WBC **Scale:** Nom **Method:** Flow cytometry  **Short name:** SAP + XIAP WBC FC-Imp  **Definition:** Analysis of SLAM (signaling lymphocytic activation molecule)-associated protein (SAP) and X-linked inhibitor of apoptosis protein (XIAP) levels by flow cytometry is performed as a preliminary screen for X-linked lymphoproliferative (XLP) disease and X-linked familial hemophagocytic lymphohistiocytosis (HLH). If SAP protein levels are absent or decreased, XLP is suspected. If XIAP protein levels are absent or decreased, HLH (aka XLP-2) is suspected. The diagnosis is confirmed by genetic analysis of the associated genes (SH2D1A gene for XLP, BIRC4 gene for HLH).

The location of the variant may be described at various levels, such as genomic (g.), coding (c.), and protein (p.) levels. Details may also include alternate identifiers for the variant (e.g dbSNP ID, Transcript reference sequence ID), the clinical significance (e.g. pathogenic, variant of unknown significance) and the change type (e.g. homozygous, heterozygous, wild type).

[82953-1] Total score [ALSFRS-R] -- **Component:** Total score **Property:** Score **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** Qn **Method:** ALSFRS-R  **Definition:** The 12 items in the ALSFRS-R are rated on a 5-point scale, 0 to 4, with a maximum score of 48 indicating the highest level of functioning. There is not information on the clinical interpretation of scores (i.e. a score in this range is classified as this level of functioning), but there is some evidence that scores are predictive of 9-month survival (90% for patients with scores over 30; 76% for patients with scores under 30). [PMID:10540002]
[82954-9] Amyotrophic lateral sclerosis functional rating scale - revised [ALSFRS-R] -- **Component:** Amyotrophic lateral sclerosis functional rating scale - revised (ALSFRS-R) **Property:** - **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** - **Method:** ALSFRS-R  **Definition:** The ALS Functional Rating Scale - Revised is a physician-administered rating scale designed to measure and monitor the progression of disability in patients with amyotrophic lateral sclerosis. [PMID:10540002] The 12 items in the ALSFRS-R are rated on a 5-point scale, 0 to 4, with a maximum score of 48 indicating the highest level of functioning.

AP diameter is measured from T8 posteriorly to the interior border of the sternum anteriorly, using the LAT chest x-ray film.

[82990-3] 3-Hydroxy-3-Methylglutaryl-coenzyme A reductase IgG Ab [Presence] in Serum or Plasma by Line blot -- **Component:** 3-Hydroxy-3-Methylglutaryl-Coenzyme A Reductase Ab.IgG **Property:** PrThr **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Ser/Plas **Scale:** Ord **Method:** Line blot  **Short name:** HMGCR IgG SerPl Ql Line blot  **Definition:** This term was created for, but is not limited in use to, the submitter's test strip, a membrane strip pre-coated with a specific set of antigens in parallel lines. If antibodies to any of those antigens are present in the patient's serum, they will bind to the specific antigen. The bound patient antibodies are then detected using labelled anti-human antibodies. Various manufacturers classify this method as an immunoblot technique. LOINC uses the immunoblot (IB) method to mean that some electrophoretic/chromatographic separation occurs such that position (size) of the band is part of the assessment (as in Western blot). Because this assay does not involve a separation step but rather uses strips with pre-fixed antigens, the LOINC Method is Line blot.

[83052-1] PD-L1 by clone 22C3 [Presence] in Tissue by Immune stain -- **Component:** Programmed cell death ligand 1 by clone 22C3 **Property:** PrThr **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Tiss **Scale:** Ord **Method:** Immune stain  **Short name:** PD-L1 by 22C3 Tiss Ql ImStn  **Definition:** This term should be used to report the presence of PD-L1 (CD274) protein expression in tumor cells as determined by immune staining with anti-PD-L1 clone 22C3. Patients with certain cancers, such as non-small cell lung cancer (NSCLC), whose tumors are positive for PD-L1 by 22C3 are eligible for anti-programmed death 1 (PD-1) receptor antibody therapy with pembrolizumab, which effectively prevents the binding of PD-L1 to PD-1. The percentage of viable tumor cells that show partial or complete PD-L1 membrane staining is reported using [LOINC: 83053-9]. A tumor with <50% staining by 22C3 is considered PD-L1 negative, and >= 50% is considered PD-L1 positive. This term was created for, but is not limited in use to, the Dako PD-L1 22C3 pharmDx test kit, which, as of November 2016, is FDA-approved as a companion diagnostic to assess whether patients with specific cancers are eligible for pembrolizumab therapy.

Patients with certain cancers, such as non-small cell lung cancer (NSCLC), whose tumors are positive for PD-L1 by 22C3 may be eligible for anti-programmed death 1 (PD-1) receptor antibody therapy, which effectively prevents the binding of PD-L1 to PD-1. The percentage of viable tumor cells that show partial or complete PD-L1 membrane staining is reported using [LOINC:83053-9], and the ordinal result for PD-L1 expression using [LOINC: 83052-1]. [83057-0] PD-L1 by clone SP142 [Presence] in Tissue by Immune stain -- **Component:** Programmed cell death ligand 1 by clone SP142 **Property:** PrThr **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Tiss **Scale:** Ord **Method:** Immune stain  **Short name:** PD-L1 by SP142 Tiss Ql ImStn  **Definition:** This term should be used to report the degree of PD-L1 (CD274) protein expression in tumor cells as determined by immune staining with anti-PD-L1 clone SP142. Patients with certain cancers, such as non small cell lung cancer (NSCLC) or urothelial carcinoma, whose tumors are positive for PD-L1 by SP142, may be eligible for anti-programmed death 1 (PD-1) receptor antibody therapy, which effectively prevents the binding of PD-L1 to PD-1. The percentage of viable tumor cells that show partial or complete PD-L1 membrane staining is reported using [LOINC: 83053-9]. If at least 50% of tumor cells or at least 10% of inflammatory cells stain positive with SP142, the tumor is considered to have high PD-L1 expression, and if 1 to 50% of tumor cells and 1 to 10% of inflammatory cells stain positive, the tumor has low expression. If <1% of both tumor and inflammatory cells stain positive, the tumor is considered to have no PD-L1 expression. This term was created for, but is not limited in use to, the Ventana PD-L1 (SP142) assay, which, as of November 2016, is FDA-approved as a companion diagnostic to assess whether patients with certain cancers are eligible for atezolizumab therapy.

[83137-0] OJ IgG Ab [Presence] in Serum by Line blot -- **Component:** OJ Ab.IgG **Property:** PrThr **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Ser **Scale:** Ord **Method:** Line blot  **Short name:** OJ IgG Ser Ql Line blot  **Definition:** This term was created for, but is not limited in use to, the submitter's test strip, a membrane strip pre-coated with a specific set of antigens in parallel lines. If antibodies to any of those antigens are present in the patient's serum, they will bind to the specific antigen. The bound patient antibodies are then detected using labelled anti-human antibodies. Various manufacturers classify this method as an immunoblot technique. LOINC uses the immunoblot (IB) method to mean that some electrophoretic/chromatographic separation occurs such that position (size) of the band is part of the assessment (as in Western blot).  Because this assay does not involve a separation step but rather uses strips with pre-fixed antigens, the LOINC Method is Line blot.

[84456-3] Dimensional Change Card Sort Test - raw score [NIH Toolbox] -- **Component:** Dimensional Change Card Sort Test - raw score **Property:** Score **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** Qn **Method:** NIH Toolbox  **Short name:** DCCS - raw score NIH  **Definition:** The NIH Toolbox Dimensional Change Card Sort Test raw score is the total number of accurate responses, with a ceiling of 40 points. Individuals 8 years old and older are not administered the first 10 items and instead are automatically awarded 10 points. There is no utility in administering the first 10 items to those 8 years old and older because almost all will score a 10 out of 10.

[84772-3] Total score [BSDS] -- **Component:** Total Score **Property:** Score **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** Qn **Method:** BSDS  **Definition:** The Bipolar Spectrum Diagnostic Scale (BSDS) was designed to identify the milder variants of bipolar disorder, such as bipolar II and bipolar not otherwise specified (NOS). The patient-reported assessment consists of two parts: 1) a paragraph describing 19 symptoms of bipolar disorder in which the subject puts a check mark next to the sentences that describes them; and 2) a multiple choice question that asks the subject to indicate how well the paragraph describes them overall. Total score for the BSDS can range from 0 to 25; a score above 13 indicates a "moderate probability" of bipolar disorder and is considered a positive screen for bipolar disorder. [PMID:15708426]
[84773-1] Total score [MDQ] -- **Component:** Total Score **Property:** Score **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** Qn **Method:** MDQ  **Definition:** The Mood Disorder Questionnaire (MDQ) is a self-report screening tool for Bipolar Spectrum Disorder. The MDQ consists of 15 questions about symptoms, clustering of symptoms, and functional impairment. For a positive screen (likely to have bipolar disorder), a patient must identify seven or more symptoms, endorse clustering of symptoms, and indicate that their functional impairment is "moderate" or "serious." [PMID: 15014728] [PMID:15306144]

[84874-7] Perforation into peritoneal cavity -- **Component:** Perforation into peritoneal cavity **Property:** Find **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Tumor **Scale:** Ord  **Short name:** Tumor perf into peritoneal cavity  **Definition:** Surgeon's assessment on the presence of perforation by the tumor into the peritoneal cavity. [84875-4] Tumor perforation [Type] -- **Component:** Tumor perforation **Property:** Type **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Tumor **Scale:** Nom  **Short name:** Tumor perforation Type  **Definition:** The surgeon's assessment of the nature of the perforation through the tumor. The type of tumor perforation is a prognostic factor in determining postoperative mortality and long-term survival. Perforation away from the tumor, related to colonic obstruction by the tumor, should be distinguished from perforation through the tumor. Perforation occurring during the course of surgery should be differentiated from the colonic obstructon and should be identified as such by the surgeon. This term was created for, but not limited in use to, the Colorectal Cancer Structured Reporting Protocol (2nd Edition, 2012) developed by the Royal College of Pathologists of Australasia (RCPA).
[84876-2] Obstruction of Bowel [Presence] -- **Component:** Obstruction **Property:** Find **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Bowel **Scale:** Ord **Method:** Observed  **Short name:** Obstruction of Bowel Ql  **Definition:** The presence of bowel obstruction due to tumors or other causes as assessed by the surgeon. This term was created for, but not limited in use to, the Colorectal Cancer Structured Reporting Protocol (2nd Edition, 2012) developed by the Royal College of Pathologists of Australasia (RCPA).
[84877-0] Synchronous tumor site Cancer -- **Component:** Synchronous tumor site **Property:** Anat **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Cancer.XXX **Scale:** Nom  **Short name:** Synchronous tumor site Cancer  **Definition:** Patients presenting with more than one histologically distinct malignancy may be classified as having synchronous tumors. Multiple primary malignancies (MPMs) are divided into 2 categories: 'synchronous', when tumors arise independently and within 6 months from the primary malignant tumor or 'metachronous', when the interval is longer (greater than 6 months). This term is used to report the location of the other synchronous tumor(s). This term was created for, but not limited in use to, the Colorectal Cancer Structured Reporting Protocol (2nd Edition, 2012) developed by the Royal College of Pathologists of Australasia (RCPA).
[84878-8] Recurrence or distant metastases details Cancer Narrative -- **Component:** Recurrence or distant metastases details **Property:** Find **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Cancer **Scale:** Nar  **Short name:** Recur or distant metast details Cancer  **Definition:** Surgeon's description of regional (local) recurrence and/or distant metastasis. This term was created for, but not limited in use to, the Colorectal Cancer Structured Reporting Protocol (2nd Edition, 2012) developed by the Royal College of Pathologists of Australasia (RCPA).
[84879-6] Tumor perforation [Presence] by Macroscopy -- **Component:** Tumor perforation **Property:** PrThr **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Tumor **Scale:** Ord **Method:** Macroscopy  **Short name:** Tumor perforation Ql Macro  **Definition:** The macroscopic finding for the presence or absence of perforation through the tumor. Perforation through the tumor into the peritoneal cavity is a well established adverse prognostic factor in colonic and rectal cancer. This term was created for, but not limited in use to, the Colorectal Cancer Structured Reporting Protocol (2nd Edition, 2012) developed by the Royal College of Pathologists of Australasia (RCPA).
[84880-4] Position of rectal tumor in relation to anterior peritoneal reflection [Anatomy] in Tumor -- **Component:** Position of rectal tumor in relation to anterior peritoneal reflection **Property:** Anat **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Tumor **Scale:** Nom  **Short name:** Pos rec tumor to ant peri relect Tumor  **Definition:** For rectal tumors, the relationship of the tumor in relation to the anterior peritoneal reflection may be classified as entirely above, astride (at), or entirely below the level of the anterior peritoneal reflection. This term was created for, but not limited in use to, the Colorectal Cancer Structured Reporting Protocol (2nd Edition, 2012) developed by the Royal College of Pathologists of Australasia (RCPA).

[84882-0] Histologic type in Colorectal cancer specimen by WHO classification -- **Component:** Histologic type **Property:** Type **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Colorectal cancer specimen **Scale:** Nom **Method:** WHO classification  **Short name:** Histo type CRC spec WHO  **Definition:** Colorectal cancer histologic type based on the current World Health Organization (WHO) classification. Colonrectal tumor types include adenocarcinoma, mucinous adenocarcinoma, signet-ring cell carcinoma, small cell carcinoma, squamous cell carcinoma, adenosquamous carcinoma, medullary carcinoma, and others. [84883-8] Deepest extent of tumor invasion [Type] in Colorectal cancer specimen -- **Component:** Deepest extent of tumor invasion **Property:** Type **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Colorectal cancer specimen **Scale:** Nom  **Short name:** Deepest tumor invasion CRC spec  **Definition:** The deepest extent or maximum degree of local tumor invasion into or through the bowel wall.  Results are based on the T component of the TNM staging system, as outlined in the AJCC Cancer Staging Manual.[PMID: 20180029] Invasion depths include submucosa (pT1), muscularis propria (pT2), through muscularis propria into pericolorectal tissues (pT3), penetrating the visceral peritoneum (pT4a), and directly invading or adherent to other organs or structures (pT4b). This term was created for, but not limited in use to, the Colorectal Cancer Structured Reporting Protocol (2nd Edition, 2012) developed by the Royal College of Pathologists of Australasia (RCPA).

[84891-1] Tumor response to neoadjuvant therapy -- **Component:** Response to neoadjuvant therapy **Property:** PrThr **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Colorectal cancer specimen **Scale:** Ord  **Short name:** Tumor resp to neoadjvt therapy  **Definition:** The response of the tumor to neoadjuvant treatment, such as chemotherapy and radiotherapy. Chemotherapy and/or radiotherapy before resection is associated with significant downstaging and improved prognosis. These specimens require close gross examination and additional blocking to demonstrate tumor. The degree of tumor regression has been shown to correlate with prognosis. This term was created for, but not limited in use to, the Colorectal Cancer Structured Reporting Protocol (2nd Edition, 2012) developed by the Royal College of Pathologists of Australasia (RCPA).
[84892-9] Residual tumor classification [Type] in Cancer specimen -- **Component:** Residual tumor classification **Property:** Type **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Cancer specimen **Scale:** Ord  **Short name:** Residual tumor classification Ca spec Ql  **Definition:** The residual tumor, or R, classification indicates the absence (R0) or presence (R1-R2) of residual tumor after treatment (e.g. surgical treatment alone, radiotherapy, chemotherapy, or multimodal therapy). It is used as an adjunct classification to the TNM system developed and maintained by the American Joint Committee on Cancer (AJCC). The R classification reflects the effectiveness of the primary treatment, is used when designing clinical management after treatment, and is a strong indicator of prognosis if applied in a consistent manner.[PMID: 12015777] This term was created for, but not limited in use to, the Colorectal Cancer Structured Reporting Protocol (2nd Edition, 2012) developed by the Royal College of Pathologists of Australasia (RCPA).
[84893-7] Supporting information about local extention AndOr metastasis for staging Tumor Narrative -- **Component:** Supporting information about local extention &or metastasis for staging **Property:** Find **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Tumor **Scale:** Nar  **Short name:** Info local ext+meta for staging Tumor  **Definition:** Clinical details about the anatomical extent of the tumor is recorded on the pathology requisition to facilitate accurate staging. Information may include a comment about the apparent completeness of resection, the presence or absence of clinically known metastatic disease and the site of any known metastases, including information that may not be apparent based on the surgical specimen alone. This term was created for, but not limited in use to, the Colorectal Cancer Structured Reporting Protocol (2nd Edition, 2012) developed by the Royal College of Pathologists of Australasia (RCPA) for structured reporting of colorectal cancers.

[84902-6] Extraprostatic extension site [Anatomy] of Prostate tumor -- **Component:** Extraprostatic extension site **Property:** Anat **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Prostate tumor **Scale:** Nom  **Short name:** Extraprostatic ext site PrT  **Definition:** The location of extraprostatic extension (EPE), if present. [84903-4] Extraprostatic extension extent [Type] of Prostate tumor -- **Component:** Extraprostatic extension extent **Property:** Type **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Prostate tumor **Scale:** Ord  **Short name:** EPE extent PrT Ql  **Definition:** Categorization of the extent of extraprostatic extension (EPE) as focal or non-focal (also called established or extensive). The identification of any EPE is important since both focal and non-focal EPE are associated with a significantly higher risk of recurrence. [PMID: 9712429] This term was created for, but not limited in use to, the Prostate Cancer (Radical Prostatectomy) Structured Reporting Protocol (2nd Edition, 2014) developed by the Royal College of Pathologists of Australasia (RCPA) for structured reporting of prostate cancer.
[84904-2] Gleason score at positive margin in Prostate tumor Qualitative -- **Component:** Gleason score at positive margin **Property:** Score **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Prostate tumor **Scale:** Ord  **Short name:** Gleason score at pos margin PrT Ql  **Definition:** The Gleason score of the tumor at the positive surgical margin (PSM) is a recommended element of the International Collaboration on Cancer Reporting (ICCR) dataset.(http://www.iccr-cancer.org/datasets) If there are multiple PSMs with differently scored cancers present, the highest score is recorded. The Gleason score at the PSM is a known independent predictor of biochemical recurrence and may be used in the selection of patients for adjuvant therapy.[PMID: 21992536] This term was created for, but not limited in use to, the cancer structured reporting protocols developed by the Royal College of Pathologists of Australasia (RCPA) for structured reporting of various cancers.

[84905-9] Margin involvement [Type] in Prostate tumor -- **Component:** Margin involvement **Property:** Type **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Prostate tumor **Scale:** Nom  **Short name:** Margin involved PrT  **Definition:** The type of positive margin involvement in prostate tumor is either extraprostatic extension (EPE) or intraprostatic (capsular incision). Margin involvement associated with EPE is diagnosed when malignant glands in extraprostatic tissue are transected by the resection margin. Intraprostatic margin involvement occurs when the urologist inadvertently develops the resection margin within the plane of the prostate rather than outside the capsule. Intraprostatic with a positive surgical margin is diagnosed when malignant glands are cut across adjacent to benign prostatic glands. In these cases, the edge of the prostate in this region is left in the patient. According to the largest series published, a significantly higher recurrence rate is found in patients with CI/intraprostatic margin involvement than in patients with organ confined disease with negative margins, or focal EPE with negative margins, although CI has a significantly better outcome than that associated with non-focal EPE and positive margins.[PMID: 17698141] This term was created for, but not limited in use to, the Royal College of Pathologists of Australasia (RCPA) Prostate cancer structured reporting protocol, 2nd edition (2014).
[84906-7] Bladder neck involvement of Prostate tumor -- **Component:** Bladder neck involvement **Property:** PrThr **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Prostate tumor **Scale:** Ord  **Short name:** Bladder neck involved PrT Ql  **Definition:** This term is used to record whether or not the urinary bladder neck is involved with the prostate tumor. Microscopically, invasion of the bladder neck can be identified when there are neoplastic glands within the thick smooth muscle bundles of the bladder neck in sections from the base of the prostate in the absence of associated benign prostatic glandular tissue.

[84912-5] Cells.chromosome region 5q31 deletion/Cells counted in Bone marrow by FISH -- **Component:** Cells.chromosome region 5q31 deletion/Cells counted **Property:** NFr **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Bone mar **Scale:** Qn **Method:** FISH  **Short name:** Cells.5q31 Del/Cells NFr Mar FISH  **Definition:** The percent of cells in bone marrow containing the 5q31 deletion involving the EGR1 gene can be detected by FISH and used for the diagnosis of chronic lymphocytic leukemia (CLL), multiple myeloma as well as other forms of cancer. [84922-4] Cells.chromosome 7 monosomy/Cells counted in Blood or Tissue by FISH -- **Component:** Cells.chromosome 7 monosomy/Cells counted **Property:** NFr **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Bld/Tiss **Scale:** Qn **Method:** FISH  **Short name:** Cells.Chr Mono 7/Cells NFr Bld/T FISH  **Definition:** The percent of cells in blood or tissue containing a single copy of chromosome 7 (monosomy 7) can be detected by FISH based on the number of chromosome 7 probe signals and used for the diagnosis of acute myeloid leukemia or myelodysplastic syndrome. This term is used to report the number of cells that have a chromosome 7 deletion out of total number of cells examined (e.g. 80 out of 100 cells, or 80%). Results are also typically reported in ISCN (International System for Human Cytogenetic Nomenclature) format [LOINC: 62356-1]. This term is based, but not limited in use to, the Vysis LSI D7S486(7q31)/CEP 7(7p11.1-q11.1) probe kit. To report percentage results for the 7q31 (D7S486) probe deletion, used [LOINC: 84921-6].

[85092-5] Liver diseases autoimmune IgG panel - Serum or Plasma by Line blot -- **Component:** Liver diseases autoimmune Ab.IgG panel **Property:** - **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Ser/Plas **Scale:** - **Method:** Line blot  **Short name:** Liver diseases IgG Pnl SerPl Line blot  **Definition:** This panel contains results for the presence of IgG autoantibodies in serum or plasma for the diagnosis of autoimmune liver diseases, including autoimmune hepatitis and primary biliary cirrhosis. This panel was created for, but not limited in use to, the submitter's test strip, a membrane strip pre-coated with the following antigens: AMA-M2, M2-3E, Sp100, PML, gp210, LKM-1, LC-1, SLA/LP, SS-A, Ro-52, Scl-70, CENP A, CENP B and PGDH. If antibodies to any of those antigens are present in the patient's serum, they will bind to the specific antigen. The bound patient antibodies are then detected using labelled anti-human antibodies. Various manufacturers classify this method as an immunoblot technique. LOINC uses the immunoblot (IB) method to mean that some electrophoretic/chromatographic separation occurs such that position (size) of the band is part of the assessment (as in Western blot).  Because this assay does not involve a separation step but rather uses strips with pre-fixed antigens, the LOINC Method is Line blot.

The patient-reported assessment consists of two parts: 1) a paragraph describing 19 symptoms of bipolar disorder in which the subject puts a check mark next to the sentences that describes them; and 2) a multiple choice question that asks the subject to indicate how well the paragraph describes them overall. [PMID:15708426]

[85151-9] XR Bone age -- **Component:** Bone age **Property:** Time **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** XXX>Bones **Scale:** Qn **Method:** XR  **Short name:** XR Bone age  **Definition:** This term is used to report the bone age (e.g. in years, months), which can be computed by various x-ray methods. To report which method was used to determine bone age, use the Bone age method term [LOINC: 85150-1]. For mapping to an X-ray procedure for determining bone age, see the Views bone age XR terms (e.g. [LOINC: 24724-7]).
[85152-7] Employment status of family member -- **Component:** Employment status **Property:** Type **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Family member **Scale:** Nom  **Short name:** Employment status Fam Mem  **Definition:** The employment status of a family member, such as the patient's mother or father. Employment status refers to whether or not a person is currently employed for wages or doing some other unpaid activity, such as volunteering, homemaking, or participating in educational instruction as a student. This term was created for, but not limited in use to, a clinical application that collects information on the patient's parents (or legal guardian), including their employment status.

[85355-6] PEDV S INDEL strain S gene [Cycle Threshold #] in Specimen by NAA with probe detection -- **Component:** Porcine epidemic diarrhea virus S INDEL strain S gene **Property:** ThreshNum **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** XXX **Scale:** Qn **Method:** Probe.amp.tar  **Short name:** PEDV S INDEL S gene Ct Spec Qn NAA+probe  **Definition:** Targeted PCR-based analysis of nucleic acids within the spike (S) gene specific to the S INDEL strain of the Porcine epidemic diarrhea virus (PEDV) and reported as a cross threshold (Ct) value. This assay is performed on various specimens, including fresh tissues (intestine), fluids (feces, intestinal content, oral fluid), fecal swabs, and other miscellaneous specimens (environmental, feed, feed pre-mix). This term was created for, but not limited in use to, the submitter's PEDV S gene In-Del differential PCR, which targets the highly conserved regions of the PEDV S gene in order to differentiate between the two strains of PEDV know to be circulating in North America: the original US strain (US PEDV prototype) [LOINC: 85356-4] and the variant US strain (S INDEL variant), represented by this term.
[85356-4] PEDV prototype strain S gene [Cycle Threshold #] in Specimen by NAA with probe detection -- **Component:** Porcine epidemic diarrhea virus prototype strain S gene **Property:** ThreshNum **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** XXX **Scale:** Qn **Method:** Probe.amp.tar  **Short name:** PEDV proto S Ct Spec Qn NAA+probe  **Definition:** Targeted PCR-based analysis of nucleic acids within the spike (S) gene specific to the prototype strain of the Porcine epidemic diarrhea virus (PEDV) and reported as a cross threshold (Ct) value. This assay is performed on various specimens, including fresh tissues (intestine), fluids (feces, intestinal content, oral fluid), fecal swabs, and other miscellaneous specimens (environmental, feed, feed pre-mix). This term was created for, but not limited in use to, the submitter's PEDV S gene Prototype differential PCR, which targets the highly conserved regions of the PEDV S gene in order to differentiate between the two strains of PEDV know to be circulating in North America: the original US strain (US PEDV prototype), represented by this term, and the variant US strain (S INDEL variant) [LOINC: 85355-6].

[85357-2] PEDV prototype and S INDEL strain S gene panel - Specimen by NAA with probe detection -- **Component:** Porcine epidemic diarrhea virus prototype & S INDEL strain S gene panel **Property:** - **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** XXX **Scale:** - **Method:** Probe.amp.tar  **Short name:** PEDV Pro+S INDEL S gene Pnl Spec NAA+pr  **Definition:** This panel contains the quantitative cross threshold (Ct) values for the detection of nucleic acids within the spike (S) gene specific to the prototype and S INDEL strains of the Porcine epidemic diarrhea virus (PEDV). This assay is performed on various specimens, including fresh tissues (intestine), fluids (feces, intestinal content, oral fluid), fecal swabs, and other miscellaneous specimens (environmental, feed, feed pre-mix). This term was created for, but not limited in use to, the submitter's PEDV S gene differential PCR, which targets the highly conserved regions of the PEDV S gene in order to differentiate between the two strains of PEDV know to be circulating in North America: the original US strain (US PEDV prototype) and the variant US strain (S INDEL variant).
[85358-0] Phosphatidylserine-prothrombin complex IgM Ab [Units/volume] in Serum or Plasma by Immunoassay -- **Component:** Phosphatidylserine-prothrombin complex Ab.IgM **Property:** ACnc **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Ser/Plas **Scale:** Qn **Method:** IA  **Short name:** PS-prothrom cmplx IgM SerPl IA-aCnc  **Definition:** Quantitative analysis of IgM antibodies to the phosphatidylserine-prothrombin complex in serum or plasma by immunoassay methods. This test is used to aid in the diagnosis of certain autoimmine thrombotic disorders, such as anti-phospholipid syndrome (aPS). This term was created for, but not limited in use to, the submitter's QUANTA Lite aPS/PT (anti-phosphatidylserine/prothrombin) IgM ELISA test kit.
[85359-8] Phosphatidylserine-prothrombin complex IgG Ab [Units/volume] in Serum or Plasma by Immunoassay -- **Component:** Phosphatidylserine-prothrombin complex Ab.IgG **Property:** ACnc **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Ser/Plas **Scale:** Qn **Method:** IA  **Short name:** PS-prothrom cmplx IgG SerPl IA-aCnc  **Definition:** Quantitative analysis of IgG antibodies to the phosphatidylserine-prothrombin complex in serum or plasma by immunoassay methods. [85360-6] Bordetella pertussis+bronchiseptica+holmesii IS481 DNA [Presence] in Nasopharynx by NAA with non-probe detection -- **Component:** Bordetella pertussis+bronchiseptica+holmesii IS481 DNA **Property:** PrThr **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Nph **Scale:** Ord **Method:** Non-probe.amp.tar  **Short name:** B pert+bron+holm IS481 Nph NAA+non-Probe  **Definition:** This test is primarily for the detection of the IS481 insertional element DNA of the Bordetella pertussis genome in human nasopharyngeal swab samples taken from patients suspected of having respiratory tract infection caused by B. pertussis. The IS481 insertion sequence can also be found in B. holmesii and B. bronchiseptica strains, so respiratory infections with either Bordetella pertussis, B. holmesii or B. bronchiseptica may yield positive test results in this assay, which does not differentiate between these strains. Since B. pertussis and B. holmesii present with similar clinical symptoms, additional testing would need to be performed to differentiate between these strains. This term was created for, but not limited in use to, the illumigene Pertussis DNA Amplification Assay, which uses loop-mediated isothermal DNA amplification (LAMP) technology to detect the IS481 insertional element.

[85379-6] Purine/Pyrimidine [Ratio] in Red Blood Cells by Spectrophotometry -- **Component:** Purine/pyrimidine **Property:** Ratio **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** RBC **Scale:** Qn **Method:** Spectrophotometry  **Short name:** Purine/Pyrimidine RBC Spect-Rto  **Definition:** The purine to pyrimidine ratio is a marker of pyrimidine-5-nucleotidase (P5N) which is an enzyme in the cytoplasm of human red blood cells. The function of P5N is unknown but a deficiency of the enzyme causes congenital haemolytic anemia. Individuals who are heterozygous for the recessive P5N gene demonstrate no clinical or hematological abnormalities while those homozygous for the recessive P5N gene have a corresponding 5-30% reduction in normal enzyme activity. P5N deficiency leads to chronic non-spherocytic haemolytic anaemia associated with mild to moderate hemolysis, increased basophilic stippling in 5% of the red blood cells and an elevation of red cell glutathione and pyrimidine nucleotides. The diagnosis of P5N deficiency is based on the change in the level of cytidine and uridine in red blood cells which is normally very low but increases to over 50% in P5N deficient patients. Measurement of P5N is by light absorption at different wavelengths. In acidic solution pyrimidine nucleotides absorb the maximum amount of light at 280 nanometers (nm). Purine nucleotides absorb the maximum amount of light at 260 nm. The ratio of light absorbed at 260 nm to that at 280 nm in a pool of red blood cells indicates the relative amount of purines to pyrimidines. The ratio is lower than normal when pyrimidine levels are increased.[PMID: 23992312][ Dacie and Lewis Practical Haematology, 11th edition, Barbara J. Bain, Imelda Bates, Mike A. Laffan, S. Mitchell Lewis, p259]
[85380-4] HIV immunoassay testing algorithm interpretation in Serum, Plasma or Blood -- **Component:** HIV immunoassay testing algorithm interpretation **Property:** Imp **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Ser/Plas/Bld **Scale:** Nom  **Short name:** HIV IA algorithm interp SerPlBld-Imp  **Definition:** This term is used to report the interpretation of results from an HIV  immunoassay testing algorithm that may be based on third, fourth, fifth and possibly other generations of HIV immunoassays. HIV testing has evolved significantly in the past 30 years. The first three generations of HIV immunoassays detected HIV antibodies (first IgG only, then IgG and IgM). The fourth-generation tests can detect, but not differentiate between, HIV-1 and HIV-2 antibodies as well as the HIV-1 p24 antigen. The inclusion of the p24 antigen allows for detection of HIV as early as two weeks after exposure and before seroconversion occurs (i.e. before antibodies are produced). In 2014, the U.S. Centers for Disease Control and Prevention published a recommended algorithm for HIV diagnostic testing based on fourth-generation HIV immunoassays [https://stacks.cdc.gov/view/cdc23447]. More recently, fifth-generation HIV immunoassays have been developed, which can detect and differentiate between HIV-1 and HIV-2 antibodies and the HIV-1 p24 antigen. The CDC is in the process of updating its testing algorithm and the latest information can be found at https://www.cdc.gov/hiv/testing/laboratorytests.html. Note that third generation assays are still available in the U.S. and earlier generations may also be available outside the U.S.

[85382-0] Osmolarity in Tear -- **Component:** Osmolarity **Property:** Osmolarity **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Tear **Scale:** Qn  **Short name:** Osmolarity Tear  **Definition:** The osmolarity or concentration of the ocular tear fluid will indicate if the patient is being affected by dry eye disease, which would result in a hyperosmolar tear fluid caused by either the reduction of tear fluid production by the lacrimal gland or the abnormal increase in evaporation of the tear fluid on the surface of the eye.  A hyperosmolar tear film is toxic to the ocular surface resulting in cell damage, apoptosis, inflammation and an unstable tear film contributing to abnormal visual acuity. This term was created for, but is not limited in use to, the submitter's TearLab Osmolarity System.

[85504-9] Presumptive ABO group [Type] in Saliva (oral fluid) by Molecular genetics method -- **Component:** ABO group.presumptive **Property:** Type **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Saliva **Scale:** Nom **Method:** Molgen  **Short name:** ABO.presumptive Sal by Molgen  **Definition:** The presumptive (predicted) ABO phenotype based on genetic analysis of the ABO gene. Hematopoietic stem cell transplant donor registries often collect samples from potential donors using buccal swabs or saliva as part of enrolling them in the registry. Because these samples do not contain fresh intact red blood cells (RBCs) and traditional serological ABO typing cannot be done, molecular typing is performed as part of the screening process to find an appropriate hematopoietic stem cell donor. Prior to transfusion or transplantation, the presumptive ABO result based on molecular typing must be confirmed using traditional serologic typing. This term was created for, but not limited in use to, the submitter's lab that performs sequence analysis of exons 6 and 7, at a minimum, in the ABO gene and predicts the ABO phenotype from the resulting DNA sequence.
[85505-6] Presumptive Rh [Type] in Saliva (oral fluid) by Molecular genetics method -- **Component:** Rh.presumptive **Property:** Type **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Saliva **Scale:** Nom **Method:** Molgen  **Short name:** Rh.presumptive Sal by Molgen  **Definition:** The presumptive (predicted) Rh factor for the D antigen (Rh positive or Rh negative) based on genetic analysis of the RHD gene. Hematopoietic stem cell transplant donor registries often collect samples from potential donors using buccal swabs or saliva (both described as "saliva" in the LOINC term) as part of enrolling them in the registry. Because these samples do not contain fresh intact red blood cells (RBCs) and traditional serological RhD blood group typing cannot be done, molecular typing is performed as part of the screening process to find an appropriate hematopoietic stem cell donor. Prior to transfusion or transplantation, the presumptive Rh factor result based on molecular typing must be confirmed using traditional serologic typing. This term was created for, but not limited in use to, the submitter's lab that performs sequence analysis of select exons in the RHD gene and predicts the RhD phenotype from the resulting DNA sequence.

[85512-2] Hepatitis D virus RNA [Units/volume] (viral load) in Serum or Plasma by NAA with probe detection -- **Component:** Hepatitis D virus RNA **Property:** ACnc **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Ser/Plas **Scale:** Qn **Method:** Probe.amp.tar  **Short name:** HDV RNA SerPl NAA+probe-aCnc  **Definition:** HDV screening of all Chronic Hepatitis B (CHB) patients is recommended and HDV viral load (HDVL) detection and measurement should be considered whether or not the patients already have antibodies to HDV. In CHB patients, HDV can occur as a co-infection or superinfection and the course of the disease may be accelerated. [PMID: 24293018]  This term was created for, but is not limited in use to, ARUP Laboratories' Hepatitis Delta Virus Quantitative Real Time RT-PCR assay. The viral load is reported in IU/mL. The IU conversion factor, as determined by validation, is 1 IU/mL = 138 copies/mL.

In CHB patients, HDV can occur as a co-infection or superinfection and the course of the disease may be accelerated. [85532-0] Influenza virus A whole genome [Nucleotide sequence] in Isolate by Sequencing -- **Component:** Influenza virus A whole genome **Property:** Seq **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Isolate **Scale:** Nom **Method:** Sequencing  **Short name:** FLUAV whole genome Islt-Seq  **Definition:** The Influenza A virus genome is contained on eight RNA segments. Segments 1-3 encode RNA polymerase subunits (segment 1 - PB2, segment 2 - PB1 and the PB1-F2 protein and segment 3 - PA). Segment 4 encodes for hemagglutinin (HA). HA is a determinant in the severity of a viral infection. Segment 5 encodes a nucleoprotein (NP) Segment 6 encodes neuraminidase (NA) Segment 7 encodes two matrix proteins (M1 and M2) Segment 8 encodes two distinct non-structural proteins (NS1 and NEP) Segments that encode multiple proteins due so by using different reading frames from the same RNA segment. [PMID: 19230160The result of this test is the raw amino acid sequence of the virus.

[85541-1] PRRSV Ingelvac MLV strain ORF5 gene [Cycle Threshold #] in Specimen by NAA with probe detection -- **Component:** Porcine reproductive and respiratory syndrome virus Ingelvac MLV strain ORF5 gene **Property:** ThreshNum **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** XXX **Scale:** Qn **Method:** Probe.amp.tar  **Short name:** PRRSV Ingel MLV ORF5 Ct Spec Qn NAA+prb  **Definition:** The Porcine Reproductive and Respiratory Syndrome Virus (PRRSv) genome contains at least eight opens reading frames (ORFs). ORF 5 contains about 600bp and is the least conserved region of PRRSV. Samples taken from infected pigs, as well as virus isolates, can be tested to determine the genetic sequence of the PRRSv. Examining the portion of the RNA that is most likely to mutate helps in determining how closely linked one PRRSv is to another, and can also be used to differentiate field strains from vaccine strains. This assay targets the ORF5 gene for Ingelvac MLV like PRRSV strain. [85580-9] Birth defects Hospital Registry report -- **Component:** Registry report **Property:** Find **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Hospital **Scale:** Doc **Method:** Birth defects  **Short name:** Birth defects Hosp Registry rpt  **Definition:** The Birth defects registry report for hospital healthcare providers contains a cumulative record of a patient's encounters for diagnosis and/or treatment of birth defects to send to birth defects registries. For Birth defects event reporting for ambulatory healthcare providers, use the Birth defects Outpatient Registry report [LOINC: 75609-8]. Birth defect reporting may include congenital or structural malformation, a biochemical or genetic disease, or any information relevant to incidents of birth defects. This term was created for, but not limited in use to, the HL7 Clinical Document Architecture (CDA) Hospital Birth Defects Event Report Document. Major sections for this guide include: 1) birth defects diagnosis, 2) history of symptoms and diseases 3) payers section, 4) care plan, 5) procedures, 6) results, 7) vital signs, 8) newborn delivery information, 9) family history, 10) maternal and paternal information, and 11) medication information.

[85609-6] Emotional score [MLHFQ] -- **Component:** Emotional score **Property:** Score **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** Qn **Method:** MLHFQ  **Definition:** The emotional subscore reported as part of the Minnesota Living with Heart Failure (R) Questionnaire is calculated by adding the numeric responses to 5 specific self-assessments regarding whether or not a patient's heart failure prevented them from living as they wanted during the past month (4 weeks) from an emotional perspective. The 5 assessments used (by question number in survey) are: 17 - making you feel you are a burden to your family or friends; 18 - making you feel a loss of self-control in your life; 19 - making you worry; 20 - making it difficult for you to concentrate or remember things and 21 - making you feel depressed.   The responses are based on a scale of 0-5 where 0 = No, 1 = Very Little and 5 = Very Much; therefore the emotional subscore is out of a total of 25 points, with a higher score indicating increasing difficulty.

[85618-7] Physical score [MLHFQ] -- **Component:** Physical score **Property:** Score **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** Qn **Method:** MLHFQ  **Definition:** The physical subscore reported as part of the Minnesota Living with Heart Failure (R) Questionnaire is calculated by adding the numeric responses to 8 specific self-assessments regarding whether or not a patient's heart failure prevented them from living as they wanted during the past month (4 weeks) from a physical perspective. The 8 assessments used (by question number in survey) are: 2 - making you sit or lie down to rest during the day; 3-  making your walking about or climbing stairs difficult; 4 - making your working around the house or yard difficult; 5 - making your going places away from home difficult; 6 - making your sleeping well at night difficult; 7 - making your relating to or doing things with your friends or family difficult; 12 - making you short of breath and 13 - making you tired, fatigued, or low on energy.   The responses are based on a scale of 0-5 where 0 = No, 1 = Very Little and 5 = Very Much; therefore, the physical subscore is out of a total of 40 points, with a higher score indicating increasing difficulty.

[85624-5] Kidney failure 2-year risk [Likelihood] by KFRE -- **Component:** Kidney failure 2Y risk **Property:** Likelihood **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** Qn **Method:** KFRE  **Short name:** Kidney fail 2Y risk KFRE.dynamic  **Definition:** The two-year "treated kidney failure" risk as calculated by the Kidney Failure Risk Equation (KFRE), for patients with chronic kidney disease stages 3 to 5. "Treated kidney failure" is defined as kidney failure that is treated with either dialysis or kidney transplant. The KFRE includes the following variables: patient age, sex, estimated glomerular filtration rate (eGFR), and urinary albumin-creatinine ratio (ACR). In this model, all variables are time-independent, in that the baseline measurement is used for all calculations. [PMID: 26757465]
[85625-2] Kidney failure 5-year risk [Likelihood] by KFRE -- **Component:** Kidney failure 5Y risk **Property:** Likelihood **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** Qn **Method:** KFRE  **Short name:** Kidney fail 5Y risk KFRE.dynamic  **Definition:** The five-year "treated kidney failure" risk as calculated by the Kidney Failure Risk Equation (KFRE), for patients with chronic kidney disease stages 3 to 5. "Treated kidney failure" is defined as kidney failure that is treated with either dialysis or kidney transplant. The KFRE includes the following variables: patient age, sex, estimated glomerular filtration rate (eGFR), and urinary albumin-creatinine ratio (ACR). In this model, all variables are time-independent, in that the baseline measurement is used for all calculations. [PMID: 26757465]
[85626-0] Kidney failure 2-year and 5-year risk panel by KFRE -- **Component:** Kidney failure 2Y & 5Y risk panel **Property:** - **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** Qn **Method:** KFRE  **Short name:** Kidney fail 2Y + 5Y risk KFRE.dyn Pnl  **Definition:** The kidney failure risk panel includes terms for reporting the risk of "treated kidney failure" as calculated by the Kidney Failure Risk Equation (KFRE), for patients with chronic kidney disease stages 3 to 5. "Treated kidney failure" is defined as kidney failure that is treated with either dialysis or kidney transplant, and the KFRE includes the following variables: patient age, sex, estimated glomerular filtration rate (eGFR), and urinary albumin-creatinine ratio (ACR). [PMID: 26757465] This panel includes the terms for two-year risk, five-year risk, and the four variables that are used for the calculation.

For example, a patient's occupation industry of "Commercials" may be classified under the CDC Census Occupation code 7470 for the title "Advertising and related services". The industry category may be the same as the patient reported industry.  A web-based tool, NIOSH Industry and Occupation Computerized Coding System (NIOCCS), may be used to translate industry and occupation (I&O) text to standardized I&O codes.[http://www.cdc.gov/niosh/topics/coding/] The associated North American Industry Classification System (NAICS) code or Standard Occupational Classification (SOC) code may also be provided with each CDC Census code.
