[85677-3] Congenital rubella syndrome case classification -- **Component:** Congenital rubella syndrome case classification **Property:** Imp **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** Nom  **Definition:** The appraised diagnosis of congenital rubella syndrome for the case report. This term was created for, but not limited in use to, the Appraisal status in the Center for Disease Control's Congenital Rubella Syndrome Case Report. Confirmed cases are clinically compatible and is laboratory-confirmed. Probable cases are not laboratory-confirmed but have at least two characteristic clinical features of Rubella disease. A possible case has some compatible findings but does not meet the criteria for a probable case. Infection only cases are those with laboratory evidence of infection but without any clinical symptoms or signs.

[85809-2] Bordetella pertussis and parapertussis and holmesii DNA panel - Specimen by NAA with probe detection -- **Component:** Bordetella pertussis & parapertussis & holmesii DNA panel **Property:** - **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** XXX **Scale:** - **Method:** Probe.amp.tar  **Short name:** B per + parap + holm DNA Pnl Spec NAA+pr  **Definition:** This panel contains qualitative results for targeted nucleic acid sequences within Bordetella pertussis, B. parapertussis, and B. holmesii DNA extracted from clinical specimens or culture isolates. This term is based on, but not limited in use to, the submitter's multi-target real-time PCR assay targeting multicopy insertion sequences (IS481, pIS1001, hIS1001) in combination with the pertussis toxin subunit S1 (ptxS1) gene for the identification of B. pertussis, B. parapertussis, and B. holmesii. The presence of IS481 and ptxS1 indicates B. pertussis, pIS1001 and ptxS1 indicates B. parapertussis, IS481 and hIS1001 indicates holmesii.[PMID: 21940464]

[86207-8] Porcine epidemic diarrhea virus IgG Ab [Presence] in Serum by Immunofluorescence -- **Component:** Porcine epidemic diarrhea virus Ab.IgG **Property:** PrThr **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Ser **Scale:** Ord **Method:** IF  **Short name:** PEDv IgG Ser Ql IF  **Definition:** This term is used to report the presence or absence of IgG antibodies against Porcine epidemic virus (PEDv) based on an immunofluorescence staining pattern. IgG antibodies to PEDv are typically be detected 7 to 14 days following exposure. Samples are compared to positive and negative controls. If cells fluoresce brilliant blue-green color against a background of non-infected cells, then the sample is considered positive; If cells do not fluoresce brilliantly but may show background non-specific fluorescence, then the sample is considered negative.; If cells fluoresce weakly and is not background fluorescence, then the sample is considered suspect. If high background fluorescence does not allow for an accurate determination to be made, then the sample is considered inconclusive.

[86219-3] Porcine epidemic diarrhea virus IgG Ab [Titer] in Serum by Immunofluorescence -- **Component:** Porcine epidemic diarrhea virus Ab.IgG **Property:** Titr **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Ser **Scale:** SemiQn **Method:** IF  **Short name:** PEDv IgG Titr Ser IF  **Definition:** This term is used to report the titer of IgG antibodies against Porcine epidemic virus (PEDv) based on immunofluorescence staining pattern. IgG antibodies to PEDv are typically be detected 7 to 14 days following exposure. Samples are compared to positive and negative controls. If cells fluoresce brilliant blue-green color against a background of non-infected cells, then the sample is considered positive; If cells do not fluoresce brilliantly but may show background non-specific fluorescence, then the sample is considered negative.; If cells fluoresce weakly and is not background fluorescence, then the sample is considered suspect. If high background fluorescence does not allow for an accurate determination to be made, then the sample is considered inconclusive.  Titers that are equal to or greater than 1:40 are considered positive.

[86320-9] Zika virus neutralizing antibody [Presence] in Specimen by Neutralization test -- **Component:** Zika virus Ab.Neut **Property:** PrThr **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** XXX **Scale:** Ord **Method:** Neut  **Short name:** ZIKV NAb Spec Ql Nt  **Definition:** Qualitative result for Zika virus neutralizing antibodies in clinical specimens and isolates. Serological cross-reactivity is strong between Zika and other flaviviruses, especially Dengue virus. Therefore, positive EIA or IF assays are presumptive only and should be confirmed by a neutralization assay, such as the plaque-reduction neutralization test (PRNT), which can discriminate between Zika virus and cross-reacting antibodies in primary flavivirus infections.

[8636-3] Q-T interval corrected -- **Component:** QTC interval **Property:** Time **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Heart **Scale:** Qn  **Short name:** Q-T interval corrected  **Definition:** The purpose of calculating a corrected QT interval (QTc) is to normalize each individual QT measurement from a given study to a single measurement that would represent the QT interval if the patient had a heart rate of 60 beats per minute. Several formulas are available to calculate QTc, including the Bazett formula and the Fridericia formula. Bazett's formula is used most commonly, but for situations in which the patient's heart rate is significantly elevated, the Fridericia formula may yield a more accurate QTc that reflects the QTc that would have been obtained were the patient in normal sinus rhythm. [PMID: 23972343] Use this LOINC code to report a QTc without specifying a method. For QTc by Fridericia use 76634-5, and for QTc by Bazett use 76635-2.

[86551-9] Influenza vaccine cycle -- **Component:** Influenza vaccine cycle **Property:** Type **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Vaccine **Scale:** Nom  **Short name:** Influenza vaccine cycle  **Definition:** The type of influenza vaccine (e.g. seasonal or non-seasonal) used. The seasonal flu shot protects against influenza viruses predicted to be the most common during the upcoming season based on research. This term was created for, but not limited in use to, the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC) National Healthcare Safety Network (NHSN) surveillance for healthcare personnel vaccinations. For NHSN, seasonal is the default answer.
[86552-7] Staff [#] who worked at facility at least one day from October 1 through March 31 -- **Component:** Staff who worked at facility at least one day from October 1 through March 31 **Property:** Num **Time aspect:** RptPeriod **System:** {Setting}^Population **Scale:** Qn  **Definition:** The number of staff (employees on facility payroll) who worked at a healthcare facility for at least 1 day between October 1 and March 31. Those who receive an influenza vaccination at the healthcare facility typically do not need to provide documentation for receiving the vaccine. This term was created for, but not limited in use to, the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC) National Healthcare Safety Network (NHSN) surveillance for healthcare personnel vaccinations.

Typically only those who receive a vaccination outside of the facility need to provide documentation. This term was created for, but not limited in use to, the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC) National Healthcare Safety Network (NHSN) surveillance for healthcare personnel vaccinations.

[86598-0] Influenza season [DateRange] -- **Component:** Influenza season **Property:** DateRange **Time aspect:** RptPeriod **System:** {Event} **Scale:** Qn  **Short name:** Influenza season DateRange  **Definition:** The date range (e.g. YYYY-YYYY) for when a given influenza season starts and stops. This term was created for, but not limited in use to, the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC) National Healthcare Safety Network (NHSN) surveillance for healthcare personnel vaccinations. The NHSN defines a (northern hemisphere) influenza season year as being from July 1 of the index year to June 30 of the following year and reports the season in years (for example, 2016-2017).

[86622-8] MRSA SCCmec and mecA+mecC genes panel - Nose -- **Component:** Staphylococcus aureus methicillin resistance SCCmec & mecA+mecC genes panel **Property:** - **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Nose **Scale:** -  **Short name:** SCCmec + mecA+mecC pnl Nose  **Definition:** This panel contains the qualitative results for the detection of methicillin-resistant Staphylococcus aureus (MRSA) DNA in nasal swabs from patients at risk for nasal colonization. Nasal cultures are the standard way to detect carriers of methicillin-resistant staph for the purpose of keeping them isolated or trying to "sterilize" them. The test targets DNA sequences in the mecA and mecC genes and at the junction of the staphylococcal cassette chromosome mec (SCCmec) and the Staphylococcus aureus orfX gene. This panel was created for, but not limited in use to, the Xpert MRSA NxG assay, which is intended to detect MRSA infections in healthcare settings.

[86635-0] Family planning report Document -- **Component:** Family planning report **Property:** Find **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** {Setting} **Scale:** Doc **Method:** {Role}  **Short name:** Family planning report Doc  **Definition:** A family planning report contains demographic and social characteristics of individuals participating in a specific family planning program, such as the Title X National Family Planning Program administered by the U.S. Department of Health and Human Services, Office of Population Affairs (OPA). A family planning report may contain information on use of family planning and related preventive health services as well as staffing and revenue. Family planning data may be used to monitor site-specific performance and compliance with legal requirements and to guide future strategic and financial planning.
[86636-8] Family planning report - FPAR 2.0 set -- **Component:** Family planning report - FPAR 2.0 set **Property:** - **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** -  **Definition:** This panel contains the set of LOINC codes for data elements in the Family Planning Annual Report and Health Information Technology (Health IT) (FPAR 2.0) specification. The FPAR 2.0 set includes both patient-level panels containing individual terms to capture specific data elements, as well as convenience group panels that contain sets of LOINC codes (e.g., the set of LOINC codes representing pregnancy tests), from which one or more codes may be used to gather results for a specific data element. Annual submission of the FPAR 2.0 to the U.S. Department of Health and Human Services, Office of Population Affairs (OPA) is required for all sites participating in the Title X National Family Planning Program.

This includes male patients seeking pregnancy with a female partner. Pregnancy intention may be used to help improve preconception health screenings and decisions, such as determining an appropriate contraceptive method, taking folic acid, or avoiding toxic exposures such as alcohol, tobacco and certain medications. This term was baesd on, but is not limited in use to, Power to Decide's One Key Question®, used by the Office of Population Affair's (OPA's) Family Planning Annual Report (FPAR).

[86744-0] Care team -- **Component:** Care team **Property:** Type **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** Nom  **Short name:** Care team  **Definition:** This term is used to describe the focus of the Care team that a particular care team member belongs to. An individual patient may have multiple care teams including one focused on a particular condition, another on an episode of care, another on an encounter, and one for care coordination.  Care team members include the people or organizations who are participating in or are intended to participate in the coordination and delivery of care for an individual. This term was created for, but is not limited in use to, HL7 Clinical Document Architecture (CDA) Care Team template and the FHIR Release 3 CareTeam resource (https://www.hl7.org/fhir/careteam.html).

[86806-7] Jurisdiction death registration Document -- **Component:** Jurisdiction death registration **Property:** Find **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** Doc  **Short name:** Jurisdiction death reg Doc  **Definition:** The Jurisdiction death registration document contains relevant death record information sent from appropriate local, state, and territorial vital statistics agencies to the national statistical agency. This term was created for, but not limited in use to, the Jurisdiction Death Information template in the HL7 Implementation Guide for CDA® Release 2: Vital Records Death Reporting, Release 1 (US Realm).
[86807-5] Death administrative information Document -- **Component:** Death administrative information **Property:** Find **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** Doc  **Short name:** Death admin info Doc  **Definition:** The death administrative information document contains details on the processes and procedures surrounding a death, including the pronouncement, certification and registration of the death, autopsy details, and the method of disposition of the body.
[86808-3] Provider death registration Document -- **Component:** Provider death registration **Property:** Find **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** Doc  **Short name:** Provider death registration Doc  **Definition:** A provider death registration document contains information from the death record collected by clinicians and sent to local, state, and territorial vital statistics agencies. This term was created for, but not limited in use to, the Provider Death Registration document in the HL7 Implementation Guide for CDA® Release 2: Vital Records Death Reporting, Release 1 (US Realm).

[86858-8] Dengue virus 2 neutralizing antibody [Titer] in Specimen by Neutralization test -- **Component:** Dengue virus 2 Ab.Neut **Property:** Titr **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** XXX **Scale:** SemiQn **Method:** Neut  **Short name:** DENV2 NAb Titr Spec Nt  **Definition:** Quantitative result for Dengue virus 2 neutralizing antibodies in clinical specimens. Serological cross-reactivity is strong between Dengue and other flaviviruses, especially Zika virus. Therefore, positive EIA or IF assays are presumptive only and should be confirmed by a neutralization assay, such as the plaque-reduction neutralization test (PRNT), which can discriminate between Dengue virus and cross-reacting antibodies in primary flavivirus infections.

[86904-0] Carboxyhemoglobin/Hemoglobin.total in Arterial blood by Pulse oximetry -- **Component:** Carboxyhemoglobin/Hemoglobin.total **Property:** MFr **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** BldA **Scale:** Qn **Method:** Pulse oximetry  **Short name:** COHgb % BldA PulseOx  **Definition:** Used to report the carboxyhemoglobin level as a percent of the total hemoglobin using pulse oximetry. Note that conventional pulse oximeters are not used to measure carboxyhemoglobin because they use two wavelengths of light that does not allow distinction between carboxyhemoglobin, methemoglobin, and oxyhemoglobin. However, newer pulse oximeters use multiple wavelengths (e.g., seven, eight or twelve) that allow separate measurement of carboxyhemoglobin. [PMID: 21459480]

[86927-1] Porcine respiratory coronavirus Ab/Negative control in Serum by Immunoassay -- **Component:** Porcine respiratory coronavirus Ab/Negative control **Property:** RelRto **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Ser **Scale:** Qn **Method:** IA  **Short name:** PRCoV Ab Ser IA
[86928-9] Porcine epidemic diarrhea virus whole genome [Nucleotide sequence] in Isolate by Sequencing -- **Component:** Porcine epidemic diarrhea virus whole genome **Property:** Seq **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Isolate **Scale:** Nom **Method:** Sequencing  **Short name:** PEDV whole genome Islt-Seq  **Definition:** Porcine Epidemic Diarrhea Virus (PEDv) is a large-enveloped RNA virus, which is a member of the genus Alphacoronavirus within the Coronaviridae family. The complete PEDv genome consists of 5 gene segments: open reading frame 1 (ORF1), spike (S), envelope (E), membrane (M), and nucleocapsid (N). It is encapsulated by a single N protein. The S protein is the major envelope type I glycoprotein of the virion and contributes to the stimulation of neutralizing antibodies in the infected host. Its association to adapted growth in vitro and attenuation in vivo lends itself to use for genetic comparison among PEDV isolates and effective vaccines. The M protein is required for assembly and can also induce the production of virus-neutralizing antibodies. The E protein contributes to coronavirus budding. The N protein has functions associated with viral replication and pathogenesis.
[86929-7] Lawsonia intracellularis Ab/Negative control in Serum by Immunoassay -- **Component:** Lawsonia intracellularis Ab/Negative control **Property:** RelRto **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Ser **Scale:** Qn **Method:** IA  **Short name:** L intracell Ab Ser IA  **Definition:** The result of this test can be read by a microplate photometer where the optical density (OD) is measured at 450 nm. The mean OD values for each of the controls and samples are then calculated followed by a calculation of the percent inhibition (PI) values for the positive control and samples.
[86930-5] Carbapenemase [Presence] in Isolate -- **Component:** Carbapenemase **Property:** PrThr **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Isolate **Scale:** Ord  **Short name:** Carbapenemase Islt Ql  **Definition:** The detection of carbapenemase-producing isolates by various phenotypic methods, including the Modified Hodge Test (MHT) and Carbapemenase Inactivation Method (CIM). In MHT, carbapenemase production is detected when the test isolate produces the enzyme and allows growth of a carbapenem susceptible strain (E.coli ATCC 25922) towards a carbapenem disk. The result is a characteristic cloverleaf-like indentation. For CIM, a disk containing the indicator antibiotic meropenem is incubated for 2 hours in a bacterial suspension of the isolate. When the isolate produces carbapenemase, the meropenem in the disk is inactivated allowing uninhibited growth of an indicator strain.[PMID: 28476242]

[86954-5] Foreign material [Presence] in Food -- **Component:** Foreign material **Property:** PrThr **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Food **Scale:** Ord  **Short name:** Foreign material Food Ql  **Definition:** The U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA) is a government agency whose duties include protecting the public by verifying food safety. When foreign material is found in the food supply, the FDA will publicize the finding and assist in the recall of the affected food. [https://www.fda.gov/AboutFDA/WhatWeDo/default.htm] The FDA uses the words foreign material, filth, and extraneous material interchangably, as any material that clearly does not belong in a food product. Examples of foreign material in food include metal or class shards, insects, and rodent excreta. [https://www.fda.gov/scienceresearch/fieldscience/laboratorymanual/ucm171979.htm]

For example, a specimen that tested positive on a preliminary screening test might be submitted for follow-up confirmatory testing.

[87274-7] Borrelia afzelii+burgdorferi+garinii IgG and IgM panel - Serum by Line blot -- **Component:** Borrelia afzelii+burgdorferi+garinii Ab.IgG & IgM panel **Property:** - **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Ser **Scale:** - **Method:** Line blot  **Short name:** B afz+burg+gari IgG+IgM Pnl Ser Ln blot  **Definition:** This test aids in diagnosis of Lyme disease caused by an infection with Borrelia species endemic to Europe and Asia, including B garinii, B afzelii, or B burgdorferi. The panel contains the qualitative results for the detection of IgM and IgG-class antibodies to B garinii, B afzelii, and B burgdorferi as well as the band patterns identified. The assay detects but does not differentiate between the species. The presence of specific bands suggests a recent infection of Lyme disease. This panel set was created for, but not limited in use to, the Borrelia ViraStripe IgG & IgM assay, which uses native, purified antigens immobilized on a test strip to which patient antibodies, if present, will bind. Various manufacturers label this method as an immunoblot technique. LOINC uses the immunoblot (IB) method to mean that some electrophoretic/chromatographic separation occurs such that position (size) of the band is part of the laboratory assessment (as in Western blot). Because this assay does not involve a separation step but rather uses strips with pre-fixed antigens, the LOINC Method is Line blot.
[87275-4] Borrelia afzelii+burgdorferi+garinii IgG Ab [Presence] in Serum by Line blot -- **Component:** Borrelia afzelii+burgdorferi+garinii Ab.IgG **Property:** PrThr **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Ser **Scale:** Ord **Method:** Line blot  **Short name:** B afz+burg+gari IgG Ser Ql Line blot  **Definition:** Qualitative detection of IgG antibodies against Borrelia afzelii, B burgdorferi, and B garinii species in human serum. This test does not differentiate between the Borrelia species. This term was created for, but not limited in use to, the Borrelia ViraStripe IgG assay, which uses native, purified antigens immobilized on a test strip to which patient antibodies, if present, will bind. Various manufacturers label this method as an immunoblot technique. LOINC uses the immunoblot (IB) method to mean that some electrophoretic/chromatographic separation occurs such that position (size) of the band is part of the laboratory assessment (as in Western blot).  Because this assay does not involve a separation step but rather uses strips with pre-fixed antigens, the LOINC Method is Line blot.

[87378-6] Isotopic Tubular extraction rate/1.73 sq M [Volume Rate/Area] in Serum or Blood -- **Component:** Tubular extraction rate/1.73 sq M **Property:** ArVRat **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Ser/Bld **Scale:** Qn **Method:** Isotopic  **Short name:** Isotopic TER/1.73 sq M SerBld-ArVRat  **Definition:** This test is for the tubular extraction rate (TER) based on the clearance of a radioisotope (e.g., 99mTc-MAG3). The TER can be calculated using various algorithms, including continuous infusion, single injection, single sample, and the two-compartment model. [PMID: 11545186]. Clearance calculation methods may differ for children [PMID:1831838]. Radioisotopes, such as 99mTc-MAG3, provide evidence of tubular clearance because 99mTc-MAG3 is selectively filtered by the renal tubule and not by glomerular filtration. Tc-99m Mertiatide (99mTc-MAG3) is also a radiopharmaceutical used for renography [PMID:11545186]. To report the results of a renography study (and not TER measurement), use [LOINC:39746-3].

[87424-8] Cytomegalovirus IgG and IgM panel - Serum or Plasma Qualitative -- **Component:** Cytomegalovirus Ab.IgG & IgM Panel **Property:** - **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Ser/Plas **Scale:** Ord  **Short name:** CMV IgG+IgM Pnl SerPl  **Definition:** This panel contains ordinal tests results for the diagnosis of primary, acute phase infection with cytomegalovirus (CMV) in patients with symptoms of infectious mononucleosis to determine whether Epstein-Barr virus or CMV is the causative organism or to determine whether a patient, such as an organ donor or transplant recipient, has had a recent infection or previous exposure to CMV. [87426-3] Inhibin A and B panel - Serum or Plasma -- **Component:** Inhibin A & B panel **Property:** - **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Ser/Plas **Scale:** Qn  **Short name:** Inhibin A + B Pnl SerPl  **Definition:** This panel contains quantitative results for inhibin A and B in serum. [87427-1] Neutrophil cytoplasmic Ab panel - Serum by Immunofluorescence -- **Component:** Neutrophil cytoplasmic Ab panel **Property:** - **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Ser **Scale:** - **Method:** IF  **Short name:** ANCA Pnl Ser IF  **Definition:** This panel contains quantitative and ordinal results for the detection of anti-neutrophil cytoplasmic antibodies (ANCAs) levels in serum by immunofluorescence methods. ANCAs are a group of auto-antibodies directed against antigens in the cytoplasm of neutrophil granulocytes and monocytes. They are associated with a number of autoimmune disorders, including systemic vasculitis. This test may be performed to monitor therapy with mycophenolate mofetil (trade names: CellCept, Myfortic), an immunosuppressive agent used in organ transplantation.
[87428-9] Drugs of abuse screen W Reflex confirm panel - Urine -- **Component:** Drugs of abuse screen W Reflex confirm panel **Property:** - **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Urine **Scale:** -  **Short name:** Drugs of abuse W Reflex Pnl Ur  **Definition:** Drug of abuse screen with confirmation in urine. Testing begins with screening assays followed by additional confirmation testing for positive results. Confirmation methods may include gas chromatography-mass spectrometry (GC-MS), gas chromatography-flame ionization detector (for ethanol), and liquid chromatography-tandem mass spectrometry (LC-MS/MS). The drugs tested and methods used for confirmation may vary across labs. Drugs of abuse commonly tested include amphetamines, barbiturates, benzodiazepines, cocaine, ethanol, marijuana, opiates, and phencyclidine.

[87433-9] Free hemoglobin and oxyhemoglobin panel - Plasma -- **Component:** Hemoglobin.free & oxyhemoglobin panel **Property:** - **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Plas **Scale:** Qn  **Short name:** Hgb Free + OxyHgb Pnl Plas  **Definition:** This panel contains quantitative results for free hemoglobin and oxyhemoglobin in plasma to test for hemolysis due to a transfusion reaction, surgery, or other causes. [87434-7] Protein and creatinine panel - Urine -- **Component:** Protein & creatinine panel **Property:** - **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Urine **Scale:** Qn  **Short name:** Protein + creat Pnl Ur  **Definition:** This panel contains quantitative results for protein and creatinine levels and the ratio of protein to creatinine in urine specimens to check for increased amounts of protein. [87435-4] Coccidioides immitis IgG and IgM panel - Serum or Plasma -- **Component:** Coccidioides immitis Ab.IgG & IgM panel **Property:** - **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Ser/Plas **Scale:** -  **Short name:** C immitis IgG + IgM Pnl SerPl  **Definition:** This panel contains quantitative (titer) Coccidioides immitis antibody results and ordinal C. immitis IgG & IgM antibody results in serum. Serologic testing for coccidioidomycosis is performed when patients show symptoms of pulmonary or meningeal infection and have a history of possible exposure to Coccidioides immitis (i.e. the patient lived or traveled in areas where Coccidioides immitis is prevalent). The IgG antibodies may be associated with a recent infection but also can be detected in the serum months or years after the initial infection. The presence of IgM antibodies suggest acute primary coccidioidomycosis and are detectable in most patients in 1 to 2 weeks after onset of symptoms. IgM antibodies may persist several months or longer.

[87444-6] Electron [Electrical Conductivity] of Water -- **Component:** Electron **Property:** ElecCond **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Water **Scale:** Qn  **Short name:** Electron Wat  **Definition:** The ability or power of water that contains ions to conduct electricity. Electrical conductivity may be expressed as micromhos per centimeter [umho/cm] in the U.S. or as Siemens per meter [S/m] in SI units. The more ions present (ionic contamination) in the water, the higher the conductivity.

[87458-6] Mean diameter [Length] of Feed particles -- **Component:** Particle diameter.mean **Property:** Len **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Feed **Scale:** Qn  **Short name:** Mean diameter Feed particles  **Definition:** The mean diameter of individual particles in a feed specimen (referred to as "the fineness of grind"). The desired feed particle size is about 700 microns. Particle size may be determined by passing the feed through a series of sieves with progressively smaller openings. The amount of feed in each sieve is used as an indication of acceptable feed grind. Particle size analysis, particle size measurement, or simply particle sizing is the collective name of the technical procedures used to determine the size range, and/or the average mean size of the particles in a powder or liquid sample. Specimen types include various animal food sources (e.g. corn stalks, hay, soybean, clove, grass).

[87477-6] Calcium in Specimen -- **Component:** Calcium **Property:** MFr **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** XXX **Scale:** Qn  **Short name:** Calcium MFr Spec  **Definition:** Quantitative measurement of calcium levels in various clinical or veterinary specimens, including tissue, feces, soil, cement and animal food sources (e.g. corn, soybean, hay, grass, grain). In animals, relatively high amounts of dietary calcium can be tolerated. However, when animals are fed calcium above the maximum level over a long period of time, the main side effects include interference with use of other minerals, especially phosphorus and zinc, and/or a significant reduction in feed intake affecting performance.

[87513-8] Trauma Process of care measures Document -- **Component:** Process of care measures **Property:** Find **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** {Setting} **Scale:** Doc **Method:** Trauma  **Short name:** Trauma Process of care meas Doc  **Definition:** This collection contains patient data elements gathered to inform the refinement of trauma management guidelines and protocols and improve patient outcomes. Data elements may include the patient's highest total Glasgow Coma Score (GCS), initial pupillary response, cerebral monitor information, transfusion measurements, cryoprecipitate measurements, lowest blood pressure measurement, angiography information, hemorrhage control surgery information, and withdrawal of life support treatment information. The information is typically collected and transmitted by centers participating in trauma quality improvement programs (TQIP). This term was created for, but not limited in use to, the Measures for Process of Care (PM) section in the National Trauma Data Standard (NTDS) Data Dictionary.

[87543-5] Beta-N-acetylhexosaminidase.A and beta-N-acetylhexosaminidase with reflex to HEXA gene panel [Enzymatic activity/mass] - Leukocytes -- **Component:** Beta-N-acetylhexosaminidase.A & beta-N-acetylhexosaminidase with reflex to HEXA gene panel **Property:** CCnt **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** WBC **Scale:** -  **Short name:** Hex A & Total w Rflx HEXA Pnl WBC-cCnt  **Definition:** This panel contains quantitative results for beta-hexosaminidase A (HEX A) and total beta-hexosaminidase enzymatic activity in white blood cells (leukocytes) for the carrier detection and diagnosis of Tay-Sachs disease. [87546-8] Dengue virus IgG and IgM panel - Serum Qualitative -- **Component:** Dengue virus Ab.IgG & IgM panel **Property:** - **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Ser **Scale:** Ord  **Short name:** DENV IgG+IgM Pnl Ser  **Definition:** This panel contains ordinal results for the detection of dengue virus IgG and IgM antibodies in serum to aid in the diagnosis of a dengue virus infection. The presence of IgG antibodies is consistent with exposure to the dengue virus sometime in the past. The presence of IgM antibodies is consistent with an acute-phase infection.
[87547-6] Borrelia burgdorferi Ab and Anaplasma phagocytophilum, Babesia microti and Ehrlichia chaffeensis IgG panel - Serum -- **Component:** Borrelia burgdorferi Ab & Anaplasma phagocytophilum & Babesia microti & Ehrlichia chaffeensis Ab.IgG panel **Property:** - **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Ser **Scale:** -  **Short name:** Tick IgG + B burgdor Ab Pnl Ser  **Definition:** This panel contains results for the detection of antibodies against Borrelia burgdorferi and IgG antibodies against Anaplasma phagocytophilum, Babesia microti and Ehrlichia chaffeensis for evaluation of the most common tick-borne diseases found in the United States, including Lyme disease, human monocytic and granulocytic ehrlichiosis, and babesiosis.  This test may be performed on patients with symptoms of a tick-borne disease, including fever, myalgia, headache, and nausea, and with suspected tick exposure.
[87548-4] Anaplasma phagocytophilum and Ehrlichia sp DNA panel - Blood Qualitative by NAA with non-probe detection -- **Component:** Anaplasma phagocytophilum & Ehrlichia sp groEL gene panel **Property:** - **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Bld **Scale:** Ord **Method:** Non-probe.amp.tar  **Short name:** A phag+Ehrl sp DNA Pnl Bld NAA+n-prb  **Definition:** This panel contains ordinal results for detection of nucleic acids within the groEL gene of pathogenic Anaplasma phagocytophilum and various Ehrlichia species. This test is for evaluating patients suspected of acute anaplasmosis or ehrlichiosis. The submitter's lab confirms the presence of the specific organism nucleic acid by performing a melting curve analysis of the targeted amplicon. The submitter's assay is able to detect and differentiate among Anaplasma phagocytophilum, Ehrlichiosis chaffeensis, Ehrlichia muris eauclairensis, and Ehrlichia ewingii/canis. The assay cannot differentiate between Ehrlichia ewingii and Ehrlichia canis due to close proximity of the melting curves.
[87549-2] 1,25-Dihydroxyvitamin D and 1,25-Dihydroxyvitamin D2 and 1,25-Dihydroxyvitamin D3 panel - Serum or Plasma -- **Component:** 1,25-Dihydroxyvitamin D & 1,25-Dihydroxyvitamin D2 & 1,25-Dihydroxyvitamin D3 panel **Property:** - **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Ser/Plas **Scale:** Qn  **Short name:** DHVD+DHVD2+DHVD3 Pnl SerPl  **Definition:** This panel contains quantitative results for 1,25-Dihydroxyvitamin D2 (DHVD2) and 1,25-Dihydroxyvitamin D3 (DHVD3, also known as calcitriol) as well as the total DHVD. This test may be ordered to assess vitamin D status, especially in patients with renal disease, and for patients with clinical evidence of a vitamin D deficiency, such as in patients with vitamin D-dependent rickets due to hereditary deficiency of renal 1-alpha hydroxylase.

[87551-8] Neutrophil cytoplasmic Ab.IgG and Saccharomyces cerevisiae IgA and IgG in Serum -- **Component:** Neutrophil cytoplasmic Ab.IgG & Saccharomyces cerevisiae Ab.IgA & IgG **Property:** - **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Ser **Scale:** -  **Short name:** ANCA IgG + S cerev IgA + IgG Pnl Ser  **Definition:** This panel contains qualitative results for neutrophil cytoplasmic antibodies and quantitative results for saccharomyces cerevisiae IgA and IgG antibodies in serum to help distinguish two forms of inflammatory bowel disease (IBD): ulcerative colitis (UC) and Crohn's disease (CD). Patients with UC have been shown to have measurable neutrophil specific antibodies whereas patients with CD often have antibodies directed against Saccharomyces cerevisiae IgA and/or IgG isotypes.[PMID: 11515844]
[87552-6] IgA, IgA1 and IgA2 panel - Serum or Plasma -- **Component:** IgA & IgA subclass 1 & IgA subclass 2 panel **Property:** - **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Ser/Plas **Scale:** Qn  **Short name:** IgA + IgA1 + IgA2 Pnl SerPl  **Definition:** This panel contains quantitative results for IgA and IgA subclasses 1 and 2 in serum. IgA1 is the dominant subclass, accounting for over 80% of total serum IgA. IgA2 is more concentrated in secretions and thought to be more functionally active than IgA1. IgA testing may be performed for the diagnosis of abnormal protein metabolism, to determine the cause of an immune deficiency due to an IgA deficiency, or to determine the cause of an anaphylactic transfusion reaction. Such patients are found to have no detectable levels of IgA or complete deficiency of one of the IgA subclasses. A deficiency in one IgA subclass may result in a corresponding increase in the other IgA subclass. When very high levels of either IgA subclass are reported, IgA myeloma is suspected.
[87553-4] Aspergillus fumigatus and Saccharopolyspora rectivirgula and Thermoactinomyces vulgaris IgG panel - Serum -- **Component:** Aspergillus fumigatus & Saccharopolyspora rectivirgula & Thermoactinomyces vulgaris Ab.IgG panel **Property:** - **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Ser **Scale:** Qn  **Short name:** A fumigts+S rectiv+Tvulgaris IgG Pnl Ser  **Definition:** This panel contains quantitative results of Aspergillus fumigatus, Saccharopolyspora rectivirgula and Thermoactinomyces vulgaris IgG antibody levels in serum for the evaluation of patients suspected of having hypersensitivity pneumonitis (HP).
[87554-2] Epstein Barr virus Ab panel - Serum -- **Component:** Epstein Barr virus Ab panel **Property:** - **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Ser **Scale:** -  **Short name:** EBV Ab Pnl Ser  **Definition:** This panel contains results for antibodies to Epstein Barr virus (EBV) antigens in serum for the evaluation of infectious mononucleosis. EBV-associated antigens include viral capsid antigen (VCA), early antigen (EA), EBV nuclear antigen (EBNA). Anti-VCA IgM antibodies appears early in EBV infection and often disappears within 4 to 6 weeks. Anti-VCA IgG and anti-EA IgG appear in the acute phase of EBV infection. Anti-EBNA antibodies appear 2 to 4 months after onset of symptoms and persist throughout life.[CDC: epstein-barr/laboratory-testing]

[87620-1] Candida auris ITS2 gene [Presence] in Specimen by NAA with probe detection -- **Component:** Candida auris ITS2 gene **Property:** PrThr **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** XXX **Scale:** Ord **Method:** Probe.amp.tar  **Short name:** C auris ITS2 Spec Ql NAA+probe  **Definition:** Qualitative results for the detection of internal transcribed spacer 2 (ITS2) region of the ribosomal gene in Candida auris DNA from clinical specimens, including swabs from nasal, axilla, and groin as well as from various surfaces in clinical settings. The test is used for rapid screening of C. auris in order to control and prevent its spread in the healthcare facilities. This term was created for, but not limited in use to, the New York Public Health lab's real-time PCR assay, which detects C. auris and related species: Candida duobushaemulonii, Candida haemulonii, and Candida lusitaniae.

[88003-9] TOAST Classification of subtypes of acute ischemic stroke -- **Component:** Acute ischemic stroke subtype **Property:** Class **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** Nom **Method:** TOAST  **Short name:** TOAST Classification  **Definition:** A systematic way of diagnosing the sub-types of ischemic stroke was developed as part of the Trial of Org 10172 in Acute Stroke Treatment (TOAST). Intervention for stroke patients must occur quickly and sometimes before diagnostic testing can be completed, and the TOAST classification gives clinicians a way to categorize the probable stroke sub-type based on clinical and imaging findings before definitive diagnostic testing is completed. [PMID:7678184] Since its publication in 1993, the TOAST classification has been used extensively in research and especially therapeutic studies related to stroke. In addition, its classification of probable small versus large vessel disease may help guide treatment decisions. [PMID: 16146496]

[88076-5] Injury severity score NACA -- **Component:** Injury severity score **Property:** Find **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** Nom **Method:** NACA  **Short name:** ISS NACA  **Definition:** The NACA (National Committee of Aeronautics) score system is an eight-level scale used to describe the disease or injury severity in patients treated by emergency medical services (EMS) where 0 represents no injury or disease and 7 represents lethal injuries or diseases (or death). The NACA score is defined by the most serious clinical state experienced at any time during the EMS event.[PMID: 28407809][PMID: 26507648] The NACA scoring system was introduced during the Vietnam war for accidents in aviation. Several versions of the scoring system currently exist, including scoring in either roman or arabic numerals and a seven-level scale where 0 is omitted.

[88080-7] Multiple birth set identifier -- **Component:** Multiple birth set identifier **Property:** ID **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** Nom  **Short name:** Multiple birth set ID  **Definition:** A unique identification number for a multiple birth set to identify set members. Each infant (or fetus/stillbirth) in the set is assigned the same identification number. The number is used for analysis of characteristics of sets of births and fetal deaths in multiple deliveries and allows receivers of the data to more readily match members of multiple birth sets.

[88106-0] Parathyrin.intact [Mass/volume] in Tissue fine needle aspirate -- **Component:** Parathyrin.intact **Property:** MCnc **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Tiss.FNA **Scale:** Qn  **Short name:** PTH-Intact Tiss FNA-mCnc  **Definition:** Parathyroid hormone (PTH) analysis in rinsed material obtained from fine-needle aspiration biopsy (FNAB) helps to discriminate thyroid tissues from enlarged parathyroid glands and also facilitates parathyroid localization prior to surgery. Various groups have reported on the utility of this technique with specificity of 91% to 100% and sensitivity of 91% to 100%. Measuring PTH in the rinse material proved very useful in cases of non-diagnostic cytology. Comparing the results of the PTH rinse material with serum PTH is highly recommended. An elevated PTH in the serum could falsely elevate PTH in the washings if the rinse is contaminated with blood. In these cases, only PTH values significantly higher than the serum should be considered as true positives.

[88119-3] Mother's Bridged race [CDC] -- **Component:** Bridged race **Property:** Type **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Mother **Scale:** Nom **Method:** CDC  **Definition:** Used by the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC), National Center for Health Statistics (NCHS) to report race data that has been adjusted in such a way that an individual is assigned a single race code. The adjustment process is used for persons for whom more than one race is reported. In such cases, a single race is assigned by using an algorithm defined by NCHS. The goal is to provide race statistics that are comparable with those used historically in order to facilitate time series analysis.

Direct detection (non-PCR) based methods do not involve DNA amplification and therefore avoid amplification of contaminants. This panel was created for, but not limited in use to, Nanosphere's Verigene Gram-Negative Blood Culture test (BC-GN), which uses microarray analysis with probe to identify gram-negative bacteria, including Acinetobacter sp, Citrobacter sp, Enterobacter sp, Proteus sp, Escherichia coli, Klebsiella pneumoniae, Klebsiella oxytoca, and Pseudomonas aeruginosa, as well as the six antibiotic resistance gene markers: CTX-M, KPC, NDM, VIM, IMP, OXA.

This term was created for, but not limited in use to, Nanosphere's Verigene Gram-Positive Blood Culture test (BC-GP), which uses microarray analysis with probe to identify gram-positive bacteria, including Staphylococcus spp., S. aureus, S. epidermidis, S. lugdudensis, Streptococcus spp., S. pyogenes, S. agalactiae, S. anginosus group, S. pneumoniae, E. faecalis, E. faecium, and Listeria spp. as well as the mecA, vanA, and vanB genes.

[88335-5] Predominant leukocyte identified [Type] in Ear by Light microscopy -- **Component:** Predominant leukocyte identified **Property:** Type **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Ear **Scale:** Nom **Method:** Microscopy.light  **Short name:** Predominant leukocyte Ear Micro  **Definition:** The predominant leukocyte type (e.g. neutrophils, eosinophils, basophils, lymphocytes, monocytes) identified in an ear swab sample from the left external canal. In veterinary medicine, this test is useful for characterizing the type of inflammation present, guiding treatment, and tracking response to treatment. The clinical course for each ear may be tracked separately in order to monitor response to treatment.

[88338-9] Coccal bacteria [Presence] in Ear by Light microscopy -- **Component:** Coccal bacteria **Property:** PrThr **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Ear **Scale:** Ord **Method:** Microscopy.light  **Short name:** Coccal bact Ear Ql Micro  **Definition:** The presence (none, few, marked, etc.) of coccal (round-shaped) bacteria in an ear swab sample taken from the left external ear. In veterinary medicine, determining whether coccal or bacilliform bacteria are present in the external ear can help with diagnosis and guide treatment. Common coccal organisms include Streptococcal and Staphylococcal spp.[MERCK VET: eye-and-ear] The clinical course for each ear may be tracked separately in order to monitor response to treatment.
[88339-7] Bacilliform bacteria [Presence] in Ear by Light microscopy -- **Component:** Bacilliform bacteria **Property:** PrThr **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Ear **Scale:** Ord **Method:** Microscopy.light  **Short name:** Bacilliform bact Ear Ql Micro  **Definition:** The presence (none, few, marked, etc.) of bacilliform (rod-shaped) bacteria in an ear swab sample taken from the left external ear. In veterinary medicine, the presence of rod-shaped bacteria is an indication for treatment with systemic antibiotics rather than topical. Common bacilliform organisms include Pseudomonas aeruginosa, Escherichia coli, and Proteus mirabilis.[MERCK VET: eye-and-ear] The clinical course for each ear may be tracked separately in order to monitor response to treatment.
[88340-5] Mite [Presence] in Ear by Light microscopy -- **Component:** Mite **Property:** PrThr **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Ear **Scale:** Ord **Method:** Microscopy.light  **Short name:** Mite Ear Ql Micro  **Definition:** The presence and amount of ear mites seen on a slide that contains an ear swab sample of the left external ear. In veterinary medicine, this test is useful for the initial diagnosis of an external ear mite infestation, guides the treatment based on if and how many mites are seen, and allows for the tracking of response to treatment. The clinical course for each ear may be tracked separately in order to monitor response to treatment.
[88341-3] Cerumen [Presence] in Ear -- **Component:** Cerumen **Property:** PrThr **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Ear **Scale:** Ord  **Short name:** Cerumen Ear Ql  **Definition:** The amount of ceruminous debris seen in the left ear by visual inspection of the ear and/or ear swab or as seen by light microscopy. In veterinary medicine, the presence of excessive cerumen can cause symptoms of hearing loss, lead to the development of an infection, and inhibit the effectiveness of topical medications.[MERCK VET: eye-and-ear] The clinical course for each ear may be tracked separately in order to monitor response to treatment.
[88342-1] Malassezia sp [Presence] in Ear by Light microscopy -- **Component:** Malassezia sp **Property:** PrThr **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Ear **Scale:** Ord **Method:** Microscopy.light  **Short name:** Malassezia sp Ear Ql Micro  **Definition:** The presence (none, few, marked, etc.) of Malassezia (fungi) in an ear swab sample taken from the left external ear. In veterinary medicine, Malassezia species, particularly M. pachydermatis, are commonly found in low numbers in the ear canals of healthy dogs and cats. When the ears are pruritic (itchy), an overgrowth of the fungi is suspected and anti-fungal treatment may be needed. When a dark exudate is seen in the canal, the presence of either Malassezia species or a parasite is likely but may also be seen with a bacterial infection.[MERCK VET: eye-and-ear] The clinical course for each ear may be tracked separately in order to monitor response to treatment.
[88343-9] Leukocytes [Presence] in Ear by Light microscopy -- **Component:** Leukocytes **Property:** PrThr **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Ear **Scale:** Ord **Method:** Microscopy.light  **Short name:** WBC Ear Ql Micro  **Definition:** The amount of leukocytes (inflammatory cells) present in an ear swab sample from the left external canal. In veterinary medicine, this test is useful for the initial diagnosis of an external ear infection, guides the treatment based on if and how many inflammatory cells are seen, and allows for the tracking of response to treatment. The clinical course and response to treatment for each ear may be tracked separately.
[8834-4] Pulmonary vascular Resistance index -- **Component:** Hemodynamic resistance/Body surface area **Property:** ArResis **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Pulmonary vasculature **Scale:** Qn  **Short name:** PV RI
[88344-7] Intracellular bacteria [Presence] in Ear by Light microscopy -- **Component:** Intracellular bacteria **Property:** PrThr **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Ear **Scale:** Ord **Method:** Microscopy.light  **Short name:** Intracellular bact Ear Ql Micro  **Definition:** The presence (none, few, marked, etc.) of intracellular bacteria (e.g. cocci or bacilli) in an ear swab sample taken from the left external ear. In veterinary medicine, the presence of intracellular bacteria in ear specimens indicates a more active leukemic cell response than simply the presence of white cells. In cases where white cells and bacteria are in proximity but the bacteria do not appear to be ingested suggests either sample contamination, ineffective neutrophils, or that the bacteria seen are not the cause of the inflammation.

[88419-7] Vigorous physical activity.work domain [IPAQ] -- **Component:** Vigorous physical activity.work domain **Property:** ARat **Time aspect:** 7D **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** Qn **Method:** IPAQ  **Definition:** A patient's vigorous physical activity in the work domain can be reported as a continuous variable based on the duration (e.g. in minutes) and frequency (e.g. days) of work-related vigorous-intensity activities, such as heavy lifting, digging, heavy construction work, or climbing up stairs. The activity level is reported in MET-minutes/week or MET-minutes/day, although the former is more frequently used and is thus suggested.
[88420-5] Moderate physical activity.work domain [IPAQ] -- **Component:** Moderate physical activity.work domain **Property:** ARat **Time aspect:** 7D **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** Qn **Method:** IPAQ  **Definition:** A patient's moderate physical activity in the work domain can be reported as a continuous variable based on the duration (e.g. in minutes) and frequency (e.g. days) of work-related moderate-intensity activities, such as carrying light loads. The activity level is reported in MET-minutes/week or MET-minutes/day, although the former is more frequently used and is thus suggested.

[88459-3] Serotonin release 1.0 IU/mL heparin.low molecular weight [Units/volume] in Serum -- **Component:** Serotonin release 1.0 IU/mL heparin.low molecular weight **Property:** ACnc **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Ser **Scale:** Qn  **Short name:** SRA 1.0 IU/mL LMW heparin Ser-aCnc  **Definition:** The SRA test is a functional assay that measures heparin-dependent platelet activation. Patient serum is incubated with donor platelets containing radioactive 14C serotonin and different concentrations of heparin. Antibody present in the patient serum will bind and activate donor platelets, releasing radiolabeled serotonin from the platelet granules. A positive SRA is expected to show >20% release of the 14C serotonin when mixed with patient serum and low-dose heparin. In addition, the high-dose heparin must reduce the % release obtained with low-dose heparin by at least 50% in order to demonstrate that the platelet activation is heparin dependent.
[88460-1] Serotonin release 50 IU/mL heparin.low molecular weight [Units/volume] in Serum -- **Component:** Serotonin release 50 IU/mL heparin.low molecular weight **Property:** ACnc **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Ser **Scale:** Qn  **Short name:** SRA 50 IU/mL LMW heparin Ser-aCnc  **Definition:** The SRA test is a functional assay that measures heparin-dependent platelet activation. Patient serum is incubated with donor platelets containing radioactive 14C serotonin and different concentrations of heparin. Antibody present in the patient serum will bind and activate donor platelets, releasing radiolabeled serotonin from the platelet granules. A positive SRA is expected to show >20% release of the 14C serotonin when mixed with patient serum and low-dose heparin. In addition, the high-dose heparin must reduce the % release obtained with low-dose heparin by at least 50% in order to demonstrate that the platelet activation is heparin dependent.

[88517-8] Mycobacterium tuberculosis stimulated gamma interferon release by CD4+ and CD8+ T-cells [Units/volume] corrected for background in Blood -- **Component:** Mycobacterium tuberculosis stimulated gamma interferon release by CD4+ and CD8+ T-cells^^corrected for background **Property:** ACnc **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Bld **Scale:** Qn  **Short name:** M TB IFN-g CD4+CD8+ bckgrnd cor Bld-aCnc  **Definition:** The quantitative levels (corrected for background) for an IFN-gamma response by CD4+ T-helper and CD8+ T cytotoxic lymphocytes stimulated by the presence of TB antigens, specifically ESAT-6 and CFP-10. This term was created for, but not limited in use to, the QuantiFERON-TB Gold Plus test, which includes two tubes with the Mycobacterium tuberculosis (MTB)-complex-associated antigens ESAT-6 and CFP-10 that stimulate a cell-mediated immunity (CMI) response. The first tube contains antigens that stimulate a response from CD4+ T-helper lymphocytes [LOINC: 64084-7], and the second tube (this term) contains the antigens included in tube 1 as well as an additional set of peptides targeting the CMI response from CD8+ cytotoxic T lymphocytes. In the QuantiFERON-TB Gold Plus test, the amount of IFN-gamma is measured by enzyme-linked immunosorbent assay and reported in IU/mL. Results are adjusted for background, heterophile antibody effects, or nonspecific IFN-gamma in blood samples.

[88723-2] Asialoganglioside GM1 IgG+IgM Ab [Units/volume] in Serum by Immunoassay -- **Component:** Asialoganglioside GM1 Ab.IgG+IgM **Property:** ACnc **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Ser **Scale:** Qn **Method:** IA  **Short name:** GM1 Asialo IgG+IgM Ser IA-aCnc  **Definition:** After washing off unbound substances, a horseradish-peroxidase (HRP) labeled antibody mix against human IgG and IgM is added to the wells and incubated for another two hours. Following a second washing step in which unbound antibody-enzyme reagent is removed, a substrate solution containing tetramethylbenzidine (TMB) is added to the wells. A blue color develops in proportion to the amount of antiganglioside autoantibodies bound to the microplate in the initial step. Color development is stopped by adding an acidic stop solution (diluted sulfuric acid) which turns the blue solution into yellow. The intensity of the color is measured at 450 nm. The measured absorbance is proportional to the titer of anti-ganglioside antibodies present in a given sample. The titers of anti-ganglioside autoantibodies are expressed as ratios of the calibrator and can be assigned to titer categories. Sample reports are quantitative.

[88856-0] Hematopoietic stem cell transplantation risk score EBMT -- **Component:** Hematopoietic stem cell transplantation risk score **Property:** Score **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** Ord **Method:** EBMT  **Short name:** HSCT risk score EBMT  **Definition:** This term represents the European Group for Blood and Marrow Transplantation (EBMT) cumulative hematopoietic stem cell transplantation (HSCT) risk score based on the following factors: the age of the patient, the stage of disease, the time from diagnosis, the donor type, and the donor recipient gender combination. The scores for the individual factors are added together and the total is used to determine an overall likelihood of success of an HSCT. Patients who have an elevated score are less likely to have positive outcomes after transplantation. The EBMT score remains valid for all acquired hematological disorders, for allogeneic and autologous HSCT (score 0-5), and is not affected by the HSCT technology or by standard or reduced intensity conditioning.[PMID: 21643021]
[88857-8] Donor-recipient gender combination score for risk calculation -- **Component:** Donor-recipient gender combination score for risk calculation **Property:** Score **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** Ord  **Short name:** Don-rec gender comb score
[88858-6] Donor type score for risk calculation -- **Component:** Donor type score for risk calculation **Property:** Score **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** Ord  **Short name:** Donor type risk score
[88859-4] Disease stage score for risk calculation -- **Component:** Disease stage score for risk calculation **Property:** Score **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** Ord  **Short name:** Disease stage score for risk calculation

[88861-0] Hematopoietic stem cell transplantation risk panel EBMT -- **Component:** Hematopoietic stem cell transplantation risk panel **Property:** - **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** - **Method:** EBMT  **Short name:** HSCT risk panel EBMT
[88862-8] Hematopoietic stem cell transplantation risk score interpretation EBMT -- **Component:** Hematopoietic stem cell transplantation risk score interpretation **Property:** Imp **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** Ord **Method:** EBMT  **Short name:** HSCT risk score interp EBMT  **Definition:** This term represents the European Group for Blood and Marrow Transplantation (EBMT) cumulative hematopoietic stem cell transplantation (HSCT) risk score interpretation based on the following factors: the age of the patient, the stage of disease, the time from diagnosis, the donor type, and the donor recipient gender combination. The scores for the individual factors are added together to determine the overall likelihood of success of an HSCT. One study used the EBMT score interpretations and found the following 5-year survival rates: low risk patients had a 50% 5-year survival rate; intermediate risk patients had a 41% 5-year survival rate; poor risk patients had a 31% 5-year survival rate.[http://www.bloodjournal.org/content/126/23/3223]
[88863-6] Lung cancer antibody panel - Serum or Plasma by Immunoassay -- **Component:** Lung cancer antibody panel **Property:** - **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Ser/Plas **Scale:** - **Method:** IA  **Short name:** Lung cancer antibody panel SerPl IA  **Definition:** This panel of terms was developed by, but not limited to, Oncimmune. Elevated levels of the autoantibodies in this panel against lung tumor proteins are present in 40% of lung cancer patients at the time the malignancy is diagnosed. This panel of six autoantibodies is used to assist in the early diagnosis of lung cancer in patients at high risk of developing it, either because of risk factors such as smoking or the presence of a nodule on a CT scan.[PMID: 21733826]

[88865-1] Other Chemical [Identifier] in Specimen -- **Component:** Chemical XXX **Property:** Prid **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** XXX **Scale:** Nom  **Short name:** Other Chemical Spec  **Definition:** Veterinary and human toxicology testing may include analysis of thousands of chemicals, including organochlorines, organophosphates, carbamates, aliphatic hydrocarbons, industrial pollutants, disinfectants, antioxidants, miscellaneous pesticides, polycyclic aromatics, alkyl benzenes, some natural products, vitamins, plant and fungal toxins and some drugs. LOINC has qualitative and quantitative codes for many specific chemicals that are more commonly tested for, but for chemicals that are identified that do not have a specific LOINC code, this code is used to report that the chemical was detected, and either [LOINC: 88867-7] or [LOINC: 88866-9] is use to report its quantity, if applicable.

LOINC has qualitative and quantitative codes for many specific chemicals that are more commonly tested for, but for chemicals that are identified that do not have a specific LOINC code, [LOINC: 88865-1] code is used to report that the chemical was detected, and this term is use to report its quantity, if applicable.

[88868-5] Veterinary toxicology quantitative panel - Unspecified specimen -- **Component:** Veterinary toxicology panel **Property:** - **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** XXX **Scale:** Qn  **Short name:** Vet toxic pnl XXX Qn  **Definition:** Veterinary toxicology testing may include analysis of thousands of chemicals, including organochlorines, organophosphates, carbamates, aliphatic hydrocarbons, industrial pollutants, disinfectants, antioxidants, miscellaneous pesticides, polycyclic aromatics, alkyl benzenes, some natural products, vitamins, plant and fungal toxins and some drugs. This panel contains quantitative LOINC terms for the more commonly reported substances, but it is not an exhaustive list. It also contains three generic terms to report the detection [LOINC:88865-1] and quantity in MCnt [LOINC: 88866-9] or in MCnc [LOINC: 88867-7] of a substance that does not have a specific LOINC code assigned. [LOINC: 88869-3] is the corresponding panel for qualitative veterinary toxicology reporting.
[88869-3] Veterinary toxicology panel [Presence] - Specimen -- **Component:** Veterinary toxicology panel **Property:** PrThr **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** XXX **Scale:** Ord  **Short name:** Vet toxic pnl Spec Ql  **Definition:** Veterinary toxicology testing may include analysis of thousands of chemicals, including organochlorines, organophosphates, carbamates, aliphatic hydrocarbons, industrial pollutants, disinfectants, antioxidants, miscellaneous pesticides, polycyclic aromatics, alkyl benzenes, some natural products, vitamins, plant and fungal toxins and some drugs. This panel contains qualitative LOINC terms for the more commonly reported substances, but it is not an exhaustive list. It also contains a generic term to report the detection [LOINC: 88865-1] of a substance that does not have a specific LOINC code assigned. [LOINC: 88868-5] is the corresponding panel for quantitative veterinary toxicology reporting.

[88884-2] Fatty acid omega-3 and omega-6 panel - Serum or Plasma -- **Component:** Fatty acid omega-3 & omega-6 panel **Property:** - **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Ser/Plas **Scale:** -  **Short name:** FA omega-3 + omega-6 panel SerPl  **Definition:** The terms in this panel are used to monitor the omega-3 and omega-6 levels of patients with known cardiovascular disease and who are on supplemental therapy with omega-3/omega-6 supplements or being evaluated to receive it. Early data suggests healthy levels of omega-3 and omega-6 could have positive health outcomes on patients with age-related macular degeneration, rheumatoid arthritis, cancer and others. Relative intake of omega-6s and omega-3s (omega-6/omega-3) is believed by some researchers to gauge the impact of the development of some chronic diseases including cardiovascular disease and cancer, but no ideal ratio has been determined. Similarly, no normal ranges have been defined for serum or plasma phospholipid omega 3s, reported as the percentage of total phospholipid fatty acids by weight. EPA and DHA can be measured as the percentage of total erythrocyte fatty acids and is referred to as the omega-3 index. The omega-3 index is typically 3-5% in individuals living in Western countries that have a low intake of fish in their diet. [https://ods.od.nih.gov/factsheets/Omega3FattyAcids-HealthProfessional/]

[88980-8] Swollen joint # -- **Component:** Swollen joint **Property:** Num **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** Qn  **Definition:** This term was created for, but is not limited in use to, the Clinical Disease Activity Index (CDAI). For the CDAI, 28 joints are counted which includes shoulders, elbows, wrists, metacarpophalangeals (MCPs), proximal interphalangeals (PIPs) including thumb IP, and knees. Note that none of the lower extremity joints other than the knees are counted in the CDAI.
[88981-6] Tender joint # -- **Component:** Tender joint **Property:** Num **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** Qn  **Definition:** This term was created for, but is not limited in use to, the Clinical Disease Activity Index (CDAI). [88982-4] Rheumatoid arthritis disease activity level [CDAI] -- **Component:** Rheumatoid arthritis disease activity level **Property:** Imp **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** Ord **Method:** CDAI  **Definition:** The Clinical Disease Activity Index is a measure of a patient's Rheumatoid Arthritis activity. Upper extremity joints are assessed for swelling and tenderness, and a separate score is assigned for overall number of tender joints and overall swollen joints. The final score is the sum of the tender and swollen joint scores ([LOINC:88981-6] and [LOINC:88980-8]) along with the scores of the Patient Global Assessment of Disease Activity [LOINC:75894-6] and the Provider Global Assessment of Disease Activity [LOINC:75846-6]. The total score is interpreted as follows: 0.0-2.8: Remission; 2.9-10.0: Low Activity; 10.1-22.0: Moderate Activity; 22.1-76.0: High Activity. [https://www.rheumatology.org/Portals/0/Files/CDAI%20Form.pdf]

[88993-1] Acute kidney injury risk [Likelihood] by Calculated by NephroCheck -- **Component:** Acute kidney injury risk **Property:** Likelihood **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** Qn **Method:** Calculated.NephroCheck  **Short name:** Acute kidney injury risk NephroCheck  **Definition:** This term was created for the NephroCheck® test kit, which calculates the likelihood of acute kidney injury (AKI) based on urine insulin-like growth factor binding protein 7 (IGFBP-7) and tissue inhibitor of metalloproteinase 2 (TIMP-2). Both IGFBP-7 and TIMP-2 are biomarkers for AKI. Patients with a result of >0.3 have a higher risk of developing moderate or severe AKI within the subsequent 12 hours compared to those with a result of 0.3 or less. This test kit is intended to help determine the risk of AKI in conjunction with clinical evaluation for ICU patients with acute cardiovascular or respiratory compromise within the past 24 hours.[PMID: 26948834]

[8899-7] Pulse intensity Brachial artery - right by palpation -- **Component:** Arterial pulse intensity **Property:** Force **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Brachial artery.right **Scale:** Ord **Method:** Palpation  **Short name:** Pulse intensity Brach a-R Palpation
[88997-2] Eicosapentaenoate (C20:5w3)/Fatty acids.C14-C24 in Serum or Plasma -- **Component:** Eicosapentaenoate/Fatty acids.C14-C24 **Property:** SFr **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Ser/Plas **Scale:** Qn  **Short name:** EPA/Fatty acids.C14-C24 SFr SerPl
[88998-0] Omega-3 (EPA+DHA) index in Serum or Plasma -- **Component:** Eicosapentaenoate+Docosahexaenoate/Fatty acids.C14-C24 **Property:** SFr **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Ser/Plas **Scale:** Qn  **Short name:** Omega-3 index SFr SerPl  **Definition:** The omega-3 index, defined as eicosapentaenoate (EPA)+ docosahexaenoate (DHA)/Fatty acids.C14-C24, is a clinically useful measurement for evaluating blood levels of EPA and DHA. There are many health benefits from maintaining a good level of omega-3 fatty acids including reduced inflammation, risk of heart disease, cancer, arthritis, and many more. Deficiency in omega-3 fatty acids can result in fatigue, poor memory, dry skin, heart problems, mood swings, and poor circulation.[https://www.umm.edu/health/medical/altmed/supplement/omega3-fatty-acids] There have been a number of clinical trials to determine the effect of omega-3 supplementation based on a 1g/day dose of EPA, which successfully increased the total omega-3 level to >5% for 84% of the study participants. The remaining 16% had levels below 5%, which is the threshold at which significant reduction in sudden coronary death reduction is evident. The variability in the blood levels of omega-3 in patients who received the same supplemental dose of omega-3 has been attributed to genetic differences in fatty acid metabolism, body weight, baseline omega-3 level, age, gender, and physical activity.[PMID: 25285179]
[88999-8] Omega-3 (EPA+DHA) index risk in Serum or Plasma -- **Component:** Eicosapentaenoate+docosahexaenoate/Fatty acids.C14-C24 index risk **Property:** Imp **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Ser/Plas **Scale:** Ord  **Short name:** Omega-3 index risk SFr SerPl  **Definition:** The omega-3 index, defined as eicosapentaenoate (EPA)+ docosahexaenoate (DHA)/Fatty acids.C14-C24, is a clinically useful measurement for evaluating blood levels of EPA and DHA. There are many health benefits from maintaining a good level of omega-3 fatty acids including reduced inflammation, risk of heart disease, cancer, arthritis, and many more. Deficiency in omega-3 fatty acids can result in fatigue, poor memory, dry skin, heart problems, mood swings, and poor circulation.[https://www.umm.edu/health/medical/altmed/supplement/omega3-fatty-acids] There have been a number of clinical trials to determine the effect of omega-3 supplementation based on a 1g/day dose of EPA, which successfully increased the total omega-3 level to >5% for 84% of the study participants. The remaining 16% had levels below 5%, which is the threshold at which significant reduction in sudden coronary death reduction is evident. The variability in the blood levels of omega-3 in patients who received the same supplemental dose of omega-3 has been attributed to genetic differences in fatty acid metabolism, body weight, baseline omega-3 level, age, gender, and physical activity.[PMID: 25285179] This test measures the level of omega-3 as a percent of total fatty acids, and the result of that index is associated with a high, moderate, or low risk of cardiovascular disease.

[89041-8] Lung cancer antibody [Interpretation] in Serum or Plasma by Immunoassay -- **Component:** Lung cancer antibody **Property:** Imp **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Ser/Plas **Scale:** Nom **Method:** IA  **Short name:** Lung cancer antibody SerPl IA-Imp  **Definition:** Screening for lung cancer using computerized tomography (CT) has been shown to decrease mortality by 20%. CT is an expensive procedure that also can reveal non-malignant changes on the chest CT. The EarlyCDT-Lung assay is a cheaper alternative with fairly good sensitivity and specificity for detecting early, asymptomatic non-small cell lung cancer (NSCLC) and small cell lung cancer (SCLC). To achieve the desired specificity of 90%, the autoantibody cutoff values were chosen based on that goal. The overall sensitivity/specificity of the assay was determined to be 38%/88%. When the sensitivity and specificity were measured based on the type of tumor, NSCLC was 34%/88% and SCLC was 50%/88%.[http://cancerpreventionresearch.aacrjournals.org/content/4/7/1126.long#ref-1]

[89090-5] Everyday Cognition - Study Partner Report Form [ECog.Partner] -- **Component:** Everyday Cognition - Study Partner Report Form **Property:** - **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** - **Method:** ECog.Partner  **Definition:** The Everyday Cognition Scale (ECog) is a 39 item questionnaire used to measure a patient's cognitive function and aid in the detection and diagnosis of dementia and other neurodegenerative diseases that are associated with aging. The final score is an average of the individual scores for each item answered out of the 39 items in the questionnaire and ranges from 1 to 4. If an item is not answered or "I don't know" is given as the answer, a value of null is assigned for the purpose of calculating the final score. There are two versions of the ECog: one that the patient completes [LOINC:89133-3] and one that the patient's partner completes [this panel].

[89133-3] Everyday Cognition - Participant Self Report Form [ECog] -- **Component:** Everyday Cognition - Participant Self Report Form **Property:** - **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** - **Method:** ECog  **Definition:** The Everyday Cognition Scale (ECog) is a 39 item questionnaire used to measure a patient's cognitive function and aid in the detection and diagnosis of dementia and other neurodegenerative diseases that are associated with aging. The final score is an average of the individual scores for each item answered out of the 39 items in the questionnaire and ranges from 1 to 4. If an item is not answered or "I don't know" is given as the answer, a value of null is assigned for the purpose of calculating the final score. There are two versions of the ECog: one that the patient completes [this panel] and one that the patient's partner completes [LOINC: 89090-5].

[89177-0] Movement system diagnosis -- **Component:** Movement system diagnosis **Property:** Find **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** Nom  **Short name:** Movement system dx  **Definition:** The movement system represents the collection of systems (cardiovascular, pulmonary, endocrine, integumentary, nervous, and musculoskeletal) that interact to move the body or its component parts.    The following criteria can be used with diagnostic classification systems/labels: 1) Use recognized movement-related terms to describe the condition or syndrome of the movement system. Include, if deemed necessary, the name of the pathology, disease, disorder, anatomical or physiological terms, and stage of recovery associated with the diagnosis; 2) Be as succinct and direct as possible to improve clinical usefulness; 3) Strive for movement system diagnoses that span all populations, health conditions, and the lifespan. Whenever possible, use similar movement-related terms to describe similar movements, regardless of pathology or other characteristics of the patient or client.

[89203-4] Airway suction device [Type] -- **Component:** Airway suction device **Property:** Type **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** {Device} **Scale:** Nom  **Short name:** Airway suction device Type  **Definition:** The airway suction device type, such as manual or mechanical, used to remove mucus, fluids, blood, or other substances from the patient's upper airway. Manual suctioning units are operated by hand whereas mechanical suctioning units are electrically powered and typically have more power. They produce a vacuum that can suction substances from the airway.
[89204-2] Patient Health Questionnaire-9: Modified for Teens total score [Reported.PHQ.Teen] -- **Component:** Patient Health Questionnaire-9: Modified for Teens total score **Property:** Score **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** Qn **Method:** Reported.PHQ.Teen  **Definition:** The Patient Health Questionnaire-9: Modified for Teens (PHQ-9 Teen) total score is the sum the first of 9 items (the answers to the other 4 items are used to assess the functional impairment due to depression and screen for dysthymia and suicide risk). These 9 items are scored from 0 to 3, with higher scores indicating more severe symptoms of depression.

The BDI FS contains 7 items from the BDI II and, like the BDI II, evaluates depression symptoms in alignment with the the Diagnostic and Statistical Manual of Mental Disorders, fourth edition (DSM-IV) criteria for depression. [PMID: 19010075]

[89243-0] Karnofsky Performance Status score -- **Component:** Performance status **Property:** Score **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** Qn **Method:** Karnofsky  **Short name:** KPS score
[89244-8] Karnofsky Performance Status panel -- **Component:** Performance status panel **Property:** - **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** - **Method:** Karnofsky  **Short name:** KPS panel  **Definition:** The Karnofsky Performance Status (KPS) is a tool used to measure a patient's functional status. It can be used to compare the effectiveness of different therapies and to help assess the prognosis of certain patients, such as those with certain cancers. The KPS score ranges from 0 to 100. Higher scores are associated with better functional status, with 100 representing no symptoms or evidence of disease, and 0 representing death [PMID: 23870327]. This panel contains LOINC terms to report the KPS score [LOINC: 89243-0] and its interpretation [LOINC: 89245-5].
[89245-5] Karnofsky Performance Status [Interpretation] -- **Component:** Performance status **Property:** Imp **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** Ord **Method:** Karnofsky  **Short name:** KPS Imp
[89246-3] ECOG Performance Status panel -- **Component:** Performance status panel **Property:** - **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** - **Method:** Eastern Cooperative Oncology Group  **Short name:** ECOG Perform Status Pnl  **Definition:** The Eastern Cooperative Oncology Group (ECOG) performance status is a tool used to measure a patient's level of daily functioning and physical ability. The ECOG score ranges from 0 to 5. Lower scores are associated with better functional status, with 0 representing no symptoms or evidence of disease, and 5 representing death. [PMID: 7165009] This panel contains LOINC terms to report the ECOG score [LOINC:89247-1] and its interpretation [LOINC:89262-0].
[89247-1] ECOG Performance Status score -- **Component:** Performance status **Property:** Score **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** Qn **Method:** Eastern Cooperative Oncology Group  **Short name:** ECOG Perform Status score  **Definition:** The Eastern Cooperative Oncology Group (ECOG) performance status grade or score, which indicates a patient's level of daily functioning and physical ability. The ECOG grade ranges from 0-5 where 0=fully active, able to carry on all pre-disease performance without restriction,1=restricted in physically strenuous activity but ambulatory and able to carry out work of a light or sedentary nature, e.g., light house work, office work, 2=ambulatory and capable of all selfcare but unable to carry out any work activities; up and about more than 50% of waking hours, 3=capable of only limited selfcare; confined to bed or chair more than 50% of waking hours, 4= completely disabled; cannot carry on any selfcare; totally confined to bed or chair, and 5= dead.

[89251-3] Condition present on admission -- **Component:** Condition present on admission **Property:** Find **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** Ord  **Short name:** Condition present on admission  **Definition:** Whether or not a specific condition was present at the time a patient was admitted. Note that the concept of "condition" is different than a "diagnosis". Conditions include symptoms, diseases, injuries, and non-pathologic states, while diagnoses are specifically identified by a provider as the source of a patient's signs and symptoms. For example, a patient admitted to the hospital with conditions that include abdominal pain, fever, and vomiting may be diagnosed with appendicitis by a provider based on the history, physical exam, and various test results. To report whether a diagnosis was present on admission, use [LOINC: 78026-2].

Circumstances that can affect measurement of certain variables include when the patient is asleep, in pain, crying, febrile, or during a period of apnea. This panel is attached to the generic LOINC code for each measurement so that the circumstance under which the measurement was taken can be reported along with the primary result.
